Client   –   Hindalco Industries Ltd  Project   –   2X30 MW CGPP   TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR MAIN PLANT SYSTEM  TCE.13 833 A - ME - 6002 - 6 001, Rev - R 0   Page   4   of   342  1.0   S COPE OF ENQUIRY   AND INFORMATION TO BIDDERS  1.1   SCOPE   OF ENQUIRY  1.1.1   Aditya Alumina refinery complex in Kansariguda, Odisha is set up by Hindalco   Industries Limited   (HIL) .  The project   is   based on the Kodingamali bauxite deposit in the Rayagada district of Odisha approved  by the Government of Odisha. Th is complex   includes an Alumina refinery with a capacity of 0.85  million TPA and a cogeneration power plant   to meet the steam and power demand .  1.1.2   This specification covers the design, engineering, manufacture, inspection, testing at   Bidder 's and/or  his SUB - VENDOR's   works, packing, transportation from place of manufacture to site, unloading,  handling and storage at site, erection, testing at site, commissioning and carrying out performance  testing of   main plant package for   proposed cogeneration   power p lant (CGPP)   having two (2) units of  3 0 MW   capacity   consisting of steam generator, steam turbine –   generator, electrical system, and C&I  system along with all associated auxiliaries and accessories and   associated civil works as specified  herein   at Aditya Alumina   Refinery ,   proposed to be located at   Kansariguda , Dist.   Rayagada , Odisha  India.  1.1.3   It is not   the   intent to specify completely herein, all the details of design and construction of the  equipment / plant. However, the equipment/plant shall conform in all respects to high standards of  engineering, design and workmanship and be capable of performing in c ontinuous commercial  operation upto the   Bidder 's guarantees in a manner acceptable to the   Purchaser   /   Owner’s Engineer ,  who will interpret the meaning of specifications and drawings and shall reject any work or material  which in his judgement is not in ful l accordance therewith.  1.1.4   All   equipment /services necessary for proper erection, commissioning and performance testing of all  items of the   plant   covered under this contract shall be provided by the   Bidder . The cost of the same  shall be   included   in the contract price.  1.1.5   The scope of work under this package shall include all necessary supply and services whether  mentioned in these specifications or not, but which are necessary for the satisfactory, reliable and  safe operation & maintenance of the plant and required for ach ieving guaranteed performance of  the expansion project.  1.1.6   The scope of services will include design, engineering, manufacture, procurement of sub - contracted  items, inspection & testing, transportation from place of manufacturing to site,   unloading, storage,  erection, painting, testing and commissioning and carrying out the performance guarantee tests &  reliability run for CGPP.  1.1.7   The objective of   T echnical   S pecification is to cover the facility highlights and project essentials for  implementation   and to receive bid for   main plant   package along with associated documentation as  mentioned in this specification from the bidder.  1.1.8   All   equipment   shall be new with sourcing from reputed make. The equipment shall also have proven  design and successful operating history in similar installations. If manufacturer of any equipment is  having technology collaboration or license agreement, the proof of val idity for the same shall be  furnished to owner. In case of boiler, steam turbine generator equipment, the design validation and

B idder shall consider the cost  associated with this back - up guarantee in their quoted price.  1.1.9   The bid price shall be all - inclusive and shall cover all items and services necessary for the successful  completion   of   the project. Any equipment or services, if specifically, not included in the specification  but found necessary for the safe and satisfactory functioning of the units shall be supplied, erected  and commissioned by the bidder at no extra cost to the Owner.  1.1.10   Bidder shall bear all costs associated with preparation and submission of Bid as defined in the scope  of work and Owner will not in any case be responsible or liable for these costs, regardless of the  conduct or outcome of bidding process.  1.1.11   Any time prior to the deadline for submission of Bid, Owner may for any reasons, whether at their  own initiative or in response to clarifications requested by Bidders, modify the enquiry including  specification by amendment. The amendment will be notified   in writing to all qualified Bidders to  whom the Technical Specification Document has been issued and will be binding on them. The Bidder  shall acknowledge the receipt of the amendment promptly upon the receipt of the same. In order to  give   Bidder’s   time in   preparing of Bid due to an amendment, Owner may, at his discretion extend the  deadline set for submission of the Bid.  1.2   INSTRUCTION TO BIIDERS  1.2.1   GENERAL INSTRUCTION  1.2.1.1   The Owner will receive Bids submitted by Bidder in   accordance with the terms set out herein. All bids  shall be prepared and submitted in accordance with these instructions to the Bidders. Owner  reserves the right to reject Bids received after the time and date mentioned in covering letter.  1.2.1.2   The submission of any   Bid , along with the related documents and specification, shall constitute an  agreement that the   Bidder   shall have no cause of action or claim against the   Owner   for rejection of  its   Bid   or if the   Owner   may elect to withdraw the invitation to   Bid . The   Owner   shall always be at  liberty to reject or accept any   Bid   or  1.2.1.3   Bid s at its sole discretion and any such action shall not be called into question and the   Bidder   shall  have no claim in that regard against the   Owner . The   Owner   is not bound to give any reasons for the  rejection of any or all the   Bid s.  1.2.1.4   The   Bidder   shall note the following:  a)   Not more than one (1)   Bid   for the work shall be submitted by one (1)   Bidder   or one (1) firm of  Bidders .  b)   If the   Bidder   deliberately gives wrong information in its   Bid   to create circumstances for the  acceptance of its   Bid , the   Owner   reserves the right to reject such   Bid   and / or cancel the order,  if placed.  c)   Bid   documents submitted by the   Bidder   shall become the property of the   Owner   and the   Owner  shall have no obligation to return the same to the   Bidder .

The basic  consideration and essence of the Tender   Specification is the strict adherence to the time schedules  mutually agreed to and stipulated in the Contract.  1.2.3   COST OF PREPARATION OF   BID  Bidder shall bear all costs associated with preparation and submission of Bid as defined in the scope  of work and Owner will not in any case be responsible or liable for these costs, regardless of the  conduct or outcome of bidding process.  1.2.4   SITE VISIT  1.2.4.1   Bidder shall at his cost and responsibility inspect the site, examine and obtain at its cost and  responsibility, all information required and satisfy himself regarding all matters and things such as  access to site, communications, transport, right of way,   the type and number of equipment and  facilities required for the work, availability of local labour, materials and their rates, local working  conditions, weather, flood levels, sub - soil conditions, natural drainage, and all information that may  be necessar y for preparing its bid, performance of work and other obligations and related matters.  Ignorance of the site and other said conditions shall not be accepted by the Owner as basis for any  claim for compensation or extension of time or loss of profits etc.   and the Owner shall not be liable  for the same in any manner whatsoever.

The   Owner   shall not be liable  for the same in any manner whatsoever.  1.2.5   CLARIFICATION OF TENDER DOCUMENTS  1.2.5.1   The   Bidder   is required to carefully examine the Tender Documents and fully ascertain himself as to  all the conditions and matters, which may in any way, affect the contract to be performed or the cost  thereof.  1.2.5.2   If any   Bidder   finds discrepancies or omissions in the Tender Documents or is in doubt as to the true  meaning of any part, he shall at once request in writing by E - mail to the   Owner   for any  interpretations/ clarifications. The   Owner   shall issue interpretations and clarifications in writing.  1.2.5.3   Owner shall not in any circumstances, way or manner whatsoever be liable to the successful Bidder  for any consequential loss or damage including loss of profits.  1.2.6   AMENDMENT TO SPECIFICATION  Any time prior to the deadline for submission of Bid, Owner may for any reasons, whether at their  own initiative or in response to clarifications requested by Bidders, modify the enquiry including  specification by amendment. The amendment will be notified   in writing to all qualified Bidders to  whom the Technical Specification Document has been issued and will be binding on them. The Bidder  shall acknowledge the receipt of the amendment promptly upon the receipt of the same. In order to  afford Bidders time i n preparing of Bid due to amendment, Owner may, at his discretion extend the  deadline set for submission of the Bid.  1.2.7   LANGUAGE OF   BID  All correspondence and documentation pertaining to this   Contract   shall be in the English language  only. The official text of this   Contract   shall be English, regardless of any translation that may be made  for the convenience of the Parties. All correspondence, information, literature, data, manuals,  definitive documents, notices, waivers and all other communication, written or otherwise, betw een  the Parties in connection with this   Contract   shall be in English.

Any major departure from these drawings and descr iptive material submitted will not be  permitted.  1.2.10.3   Oral statements made by the Bidder at any time regarding quality, quantity or arrangement of the  equipment or any other matter will not be considered.

While the Bids are under  consideration, Bidders and / or their representatives or   other interested parties are advised to refrain  from contacting by any means, the Owner and / or his employees / representatives on matters  related to the Bids under consideration. The Owner if necessary will obtain clarifications on the Bids  by requesting   for such information from any or all the Bidders, either in writing or through formal  meetings as may be necessary. Bidders will not be permitted to change the substance of their Bids  after the Bids have been opened.  1.2.13   DEADLINE FOR SUBMISSION OF   BID S

In such case all rights and obligations of the  Owner and Bidders under this Tender shall be subjected t o the extended deadline.  1.2.13.3   Owner reserves the right to reject any bid received after the deadline prescribed. The Owner shall  not be   responsible for delays due to post or courier.  1.2.14   EFFECT AND VALIDITY OF   BID  1.2.14.1   The submission of any Bid connected with these documents and specifications shall not constitute  an agreement and the Bidder shall have no cause of action or claim, against the Owner for rejection  of his Bid. Only the Final Proposal shall be part of the Co ntract to be awarded pursuant hereto,  notwithstanding any thing to the contrary contained herein. The Owner shall always be at liberty to  reject or accept any Bid or Bids or to annul the bidding process and reject all Bids at anytime at his  sole discretion   and any such action will not be called into question and the Bidder shall have no claim  in that regard against the Owner and the Owner shall have no obligation whatsoever to inform the  affected Bidder or Bidders about the ground (s) or reason(s) for its s uch action.  1.2.14.2   The Bid should be kept valid for acceptance for a period of one hundred and eighty (180) calendar  days from the last date of submission of the Bids. Bids valid for a shorter period run the risk of  rejection.  1.2.14.3   In exceptional circumstance, prior to expiry of the original bid validity period, the Owner may request  that the bidders extend the period of validity for a specified additional period. The request and the  responses thereto shall be made in writing. It is   understood that the bidder agrees to reasonably  consider such a request. A bidder agreeing to the request will not be permitted to modify his bid.  1.2.15   CORRECTION OF ERRORS  1.2.15.1   Bids determined to be substantially responsive will be checked by the Owner for any arithmetic  errors. Errors will be corrected by the Owner. Where there is a discrepancy between the amounts in  figures and in words, the amount in words will govern. The amo unt stated in the Bid will be adjusted  by the Owner in accordance with this procedure for the correction of errors and, with the  concurrence of the Bidder, shall be considered as binding upon the Bidder. If the Bidder does not  accept the corrected amount o f Bid, his Bid will be rejected.  1.2.15.2   The owner reserves the right to accept or reject any variation or deviation. Alternate offers, for other  than   the configurations allowed in the Tender Documents will be considered by the Owner if found  techno - commercially attractive to the Owner Alternate Offers of only such Bidders will be evaluated  whose Primary Bids are found to be responsive.

Client   –   Hindalco Industries Ltd  Project   –   2X30 MW CGPP   TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR MAIN PLANT SYSTEM  TCE.13 833 A - ME - 6002 - 6 001, Rev - R 0   Page   13   of   342  1.2.16   EVALUATION OF   BID S  1.2.16.1   Evaluation of Technical and Commercial bid  The Owner will open Envelope A & B comprising the technical and commercial Bids of the Bidders.  Bids which are found deficient on general scrutiny will be treated as non - responsive and shall be  rejected. Such deficiencies are:  (i)   Bids not properly signed.  (ii)   Furnishing of incomplete or incorrect information and / or documents.  (iii)   Bids are not substantially responsive. For this purpose a Bid shall be substantially responsive  if it conforms to all the terms and conditions of this Tender without any material deviation.  No recourse shall be made to any extrinsic evidence or mater ial to determine a Bid’s  responsiveness and the same shall be based on the contents of Bid itself.  (iv)   Original copy of bid is not signed on each page by the Bidder.  (vi)   Bid is not signed by a duly authorised person holding powers of attorney to sign the bid as  required by the Tender.  1.2.16.2   Detailed Scrutiny  The detailed scrutiny shall comprise Technical Scrutiny and Commercial Scrutiny as under:  The Owner will carry out a detailed scrutiny of each responsive Bid in order to determine whether  the Bid is in accordance with the requirements set forth in the Tender Documents. In order to reach  such a determination, the Owner will examine and compare t he Bid on the basis of the information  supplied by the Bidders, taking into account the following factors;  (i)   Overall completeness and compliance with the relevant technical & commercial aspects  and requirements of the Tender Documents; variations including deviations as identified in  Schedules, suitability of the facilities offered in relation to the environ mental and climatic  conditions prevailing at the Site; and quality, function and operation of any process control  concept included in the Bid.  (ii)   Other relevant factors, if any, listed in the Data Sheets and other sections of Bid or that  Owner deems necessary or prudent to take into consideration.  The Scrutiny would also involve the Owner obtaining clarifications from the Qualified Bidders and the  discussions with them in order to analyse the variations in specified scope and / or technical  /commercial variations from Tender Documents  Bids which are found deficient on detailed scrutiny will be rejected. Such deficiencies are:  (i)   The bid does not include all information as required under ‘ Bidder’s Submittals ’
--- Page 16 ---
Client   –   Hindalco Industries Ltd  Project   –   2X30 MW CGPP   TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR MAIN PLANT SYSTEM  TCE.13 833 A - ME - 6002 - 6 001, Rev - R 0   Page   14   of   342  (ii)   The bid is conditional.  (iii)   Documents referred to in the Bid are found to be incorrect or disclose that the Bidder is not  financially sound or competent to make the bid.  (iv)   The owner is subject to any restraint in respect of use and / or   operation of any of its facility.  (v)   The Bidder does not furnish all clarifications regarding the Bid requested by Owner either  in writing or through formal meetings as may be necessary.  In respect of the Bids not found deficient, each relevant Bidder shall be required to submit Price  Implication Bid in respect of resolved commercial and technical issues in a separate envelope, which  together with the Bid is in compliance with the Tender D ocuments except for the listed unresolved  variations/ deviations prepared by the Owner.  1.2.16.3   Final Evaluation  Evaluated Price in INR shall be determined by summing up of the following:  (a)   Total Lump sum Price.  (b)   Adjustment for errors as defined above.  (c)   Cost loading for unresolved variations/ deviations both   technical and commercial as  determined by Owner.  (d)   Performance Evaluation Loadings for guaranteed performance parameters.  (e)   Loading factors for delay in delivery beyond the specified completion schedule.  (f)   Each bid will be assessed for its’ total cost of ownership for the life time of the plant taking  into consideration among other things the cost of operating & maintaining the plant  equipment’s, as applicable.  1.2.17   AWARD OF   CONTRACT  1.2.17.1   Owner may invite the Bidder for price negotiation. Bidders shall attend such negotiation meetings  and if requested by Owner shall provide the analysis of rates/ break - up of amount quoted by him to  demonstrate the reasonability. As a result of negotiation,   Bidder may offer rebate on his earlier  quoted price.  1.2.17.2   After selection of the successful   Bidder , a Letter of Intent ("LOI") shall be released by the   Owner   in  favour of such a   Bidder . This   Bidder   shall have to give a Letter of Acceptance ("LOA") within a period  of one week from the date of issue of LOI. If the   Owner   does not receive LOA within this period, the  next eligible   Bidder   may be considered.  1.2.17.3   After receipt of the LOA by the   Owner , the   Bidder   shall enter into the Contract with the   Owner .

Frequency   :   50 Hz (+3% to   - 5%)  o.   Design Ambient Temperature of electrical  equipment  :   50 Deg. C  p.   Relative Humidity for design of   Electrical equipment   :   90%  2.2   FACILITY DESIGN CRITERIA  The proposed   cogeneration   power plant shall comprise of   two (2) units. Each unit shall comprise of  one   ( 1 ) no.   steam generator and its auxiliaries ,   one   ( 1 ) no. steam turbine generator and   its auxiliaries  balance of plant systems with most efficient and proven machines utilising state - of - art technology  available in the world. The plant shall be designed for a useful life higher than 25 years.  The plant design should allow for the future addition of another unit of similar capacity with minimal  retrofitting to the existing facility.   All the common facilities shall be designed for continuous  operation of both the units. Also, t he plant must be capable of operating with a single unit without  any issues affecting performance or operations .  The design of the Facility shall take into account   and comply with all statutory requirements in  addition to the applicable codes listed   in Clause 2.3 hereof . The ratings and capabilities for the Facility  and all equipment shall be such that the Facility and all portions thereof can operate continuously at  guaranteed net plant output as well as at minimum load, including operational events such as startup  and   shutdown, over the entire range of ambient conditions prevailing at the Site. The ratings and  capabilities of all equipment and systems shall also me et the specified sizing criteria for respective
--- Page 21 ---
Client   –   Hindalco Industries Ltd  Project   –   2X30 MW CGPP   TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR MAIN PLANT SYSTEM  TCE.13 833 A - ME - 6002 - 6 001, Rev - R 0   Page   19   of   342  equipment and systems and shall not limit operation of the unit at its maximum capability, i.e. ,  operation of boiler and turbine at their maximum ratings, without exceeding any safety and emission  limits.  The Facility shall be provided for safe, reliable, ease of maintainability, cost effective operation where  continuity of supply   of steam and power to refinery plant   is the first consideration. Only those  systems which have been field - proven and fully integrated shall be used. All equipment shall be sized  to ensure satisfactory operation under system variations conditions of voltage, frequency, load,  weather, temperat ure, and to safely withstand sudden variations of load and voltage as may be met  under worki ng conditions, including those due to startup loads, transient short - circuit and other fault  conditions.  The design shall incorporate every reasonable safeguard and provision for the safety of all personnel  concerned in the construction, commissioning, operation and maintenance of the installation.  The plant shall be designed to perform in the most efficient manner at 100% maximum continuous  rating (MCR). However, at times, the unit shall be able to operate on part load depending on the  requirement. The unit and auxiliaries shall be suitably designed considering the requirements as  mentioned in Specification. The unit shall also be capable to   operate at valve wide open (VWO)  condition   of 105% TMCR . The rated system   frequency is 50 Hz.  The design parameters and technical requirements are provided under   the attached technical  datasheets   and the following requirements shall be satisfied by the Bidder:  a)   Higher efficiency to lower operating cost and better plant performance.  b)   Utilization of proven design for extended plant life and system reliability.  c)   Redundancy in the equipment to assure a high degree of plant availability.  d)   Design, construction & operational safety in compliance to Owner’s requirements & statutory  rules.  e)   Lower start up time, quick ramp up & down and better unit response.  f)   Capability of adequate cold / warm / hot start - up of the unit in a year.  g)   State - of - the -   Art control & automation system for unit & auxiliaries.  h)   Equipment layout for easy maintenance & manoeuvring in the plant.  i)   Protection of surrounding natural environment and ecosystem.  2.3   CODES AND STANDARDS  All equipment, systems and services covered under this specification shall comply with all currently  applicable statutes, regulations, and safety codes in the locality where the equipment will be  installed. All codes and standards referred to in   Technical Specification   shall be understood to be the  latest version on the date of offer made by the bidder unless otherwise indicated.

Indian Electricity Rules.  2.4   ENVIRONMENTAL GUIDELINES  2.4.1   Noise Emission  During construction, noise mitigation strategies shall be employed to minimize impact to the  surrounding community. As a minimum, permanent plant equipment, excluding equipment, used  during emergency or abnormal operating conditions, such as, Safety Valves , start - up   vent   and  pressure relief valves   shall not exceed 85 dBa   at one meter away and a height of 1.5meters.  Far Field Noise  Testing shall be conducted in accordance with a project - specific test plan prepared by the   Bidder and  approved by the Owner in conformity to applicable standards. Sound level   surveys shall be conducted  in accordance with the approved procedure to evaluate compliance with the Noise Emissions Limit  and witnessed by the Owner or Owner’s Engineer, or both. Testing for and corrections to a free field  condition shall conform to appli cable standards , such as   t he measured noise level produced   shall not  exceed   8 5 dBA at a distance of 1.0 m and 1.5 m height from its boundary in any direction under any  load condition   by any rotating equipment   except for TG, HP & LP bypass valves, air motor s,   safety  valves and regulating drain valves .   The noise level for TG, regulating drain   valves and for air motors  shall not exceed 90 dBA and 95 dBA respectively, and that   for safety valves and associated vent pipes  shall not exceed 105 dBA.   If necessary, suitable by the use of low noise trims, baffle   plate  silencers/line silencers, acoustic lagging (insulation), thick - walled   pipe work as and where necessary.  Environmental requirement shall be complied with, and Guaranteed Emissions shall be met.  Guarantees shall be verified by tests conducted in accordance with all national and local  requirements. Test procedures shall be developed and submitted to the Owner for approval 180 days  before the tests.  2.4.2   Exhaust Gas Emissions and Air Quality  Exhaust gas emissions shall not exceed the emission rates as mentioned below and these  requirements apply under all normal operating conditions.  Guarantees shall be verified by tests conducted in accordance with all national and local  requirements. Test   procedures shall be developed and submitted to the Owner for approval 180 days  before the tests.

It is believed that the bidder has technical expertise in execution of similar power plants and they will  notify to owner for any errors or omissions in the specification and provide alternatives, where they  believe it is needed.  All equipment/services necessary for proper erection,   commissioning,   and performance testing of all  items of the plant covered under this contract shall be provided by the Bidder. The cost of the same  shall be included in the contract price. The SIL 3 certified safety system shall be of the same family as of  DCS and shall reside on the plant data highway along with the   DCS controllers. --- Page 48 ---
Client   –   Hindalco Industries Ltd  Project   –   2X30 MW CGPP   TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR MAIN PLANT SYSTEM  TCE.13 833 A - ME - 6002 - 6 001, Rev - R 0   Page   46   of   342  TCE.13 833 A - ME - MP - 6001). The plot plan identifies the relative locations of various facilities  within the plot. Bidder shall   furnish their proposed main plant area layout along with the bid.  All general arrangement and layout drawings shall be dimensioned in millimeters and generally  drawn to scale in accordance with Good Industry Practices.  ▪   Plot plan and detailed design of the plant shall meet all the requirements and norms of  statutory authorities, electrical inspector, MOEF, local government/municipal authorities. IBR,  Factory inspector, Fire inspector, Chief controller of explosives, Safet y distance between  various facilities and buildings and any other requirements under which the project comes  under the purview.  ▪   Bidder shall also ensure and provide the requirements of transportation, construction,  erection, maintenance requirements and functional requirements while preparation of the  plot plan. Adequate area and suitable hoists / trolleys shall be considered to fa cilitate  maintenance.  ▪   In order to prevent any vibration from the steam turbine generating sets from being  transmitted to the turbine building and other equipment located inside the building, the turbo  set shall be founded on an independent pedestal, isolated from all other foun dations and  superstructure by adequate clearances all around.  ▪   All heavy equipment and heavy rotating machinery in particular as far as possible, should be  located on ground floor. Their foundations should be clear of column foundations since such  an arrangement will avoid vibrations being transmitted to the building   from the rotating  machinery.  ▪   Free space for the rotor withdrawal, condenser tube removal and other areas reserved for  such erection or maintenance activities shall be clearly identified in the drawing.  ▪   All skids housing mechanical equipment shall be provided with a clear space of at least 1000  mm for proper operation and maintenance.  ▪   As far as possible, equipment layout should follow the logic and sequence of the flow process.  This will result in simple and convenient piping layouts.   When flow takes place essentially by  gravity, receiver vessels should be placed at a lower elevation   than the source vessel.  ▪   Adequate consideration should be given to the prevention and containment of fire. Necessary  access   shall be provided for the entry of firefighting personnel in the event of a fire break - out  and also for the safe exit of operating personnel.  ▪   Adequate ventilation and safety aspects should be considered while developing the layout of  equipment handling explosive,   hazardous,   or toxic substances.  ▪   When the noise generated by equipment and machinery is expected to be in excess of  recommended limits specified, such machinery should be provided with acoustic hoods or  isolated from general working area by locating inside a walled room.

Client   –   Hindalco Industries Ltd  Project   –   2X30 MW CGPP   TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR MAIN PLANT SYSTEM  TCE.13 833 A - ME - 6002 - 6 001, Rev - R 0   Page   47   of   342  ▪   The arrangement of duct work shall be as required for various equipment/system design and  to secure accessibility and maximum space utilization.  ▪   The layout of piping shall ensure that all valves are located so as to be conveniently accessible.  The overhead piping shall have a minimum working clearance of 2.1m over all walkways and  working areas (even   after considering movement of piping) and 7 metres   above roadways  unless otherwise approved by the   Owner / Owner’s Engineer . All valves, 100 NB and above shall  have a clear approach space of 1m to facilitate its operation and maintenance.  All accessible areas shall be provided with minimum clear headroom as follows, unless  otherwise specified:  Finished floors to ceiling (buildings)   : 3000 mm  Doors, Walkways, Platforms, Stairs etc.   : 2100 mm  False ceiling of office areas   : 2400 mm  Safety cage for ladders   : 2500 mm  Access for forklift trucks   : 2800 mm  Main roads/Railway crossings & crane access   : 9000 mm  Other plant roads and truck access   : 6000 mm  Cable & Pipe rack   : 3000 mm  (Except Road/Rail crossings)  ▪   All piping shall be grouped wherever practicable and shall be routed to present a neat  appearance. Drains shall be provided at all low points and vents at high points as per actual  layout.  ▪   All necessary auxiliary platforms and access stairways ladders for operation and maintenance  of equipment, piping, valves, instruments, etc., shall be provided by the Bidder.  ▪   One (1) no. passenger   elevator of 800 kg for TG building and One (1) no. of 1 50 0 kg capacity  passenger - cum - goods elevator   common   for   boiler area shall be considered.  ▪   In case where two isolation valves are provided, one valve shall be near the tap off and the  second valve at an accessible location. If any of the valves are not accessible, suitable access  platform shall be provided by the Bidder. Necessary walkways with   a minimum 1000 mm wide  shall be provided.  ▪   Pipe trenches for essential systems like condensate suction piping may be provided. If trench  is provided, access to pump suction strainers and expansion joint shall be ensured and the  trench width shall be adequate to enable assembly and disassembly of f lange joints.   While  finalising the trenches, a minimum space of 300 mm shall be provided from the outer face of  pipe. Clear space of 150 mm shall be provided below the pipe support for easy drainage. All  trenches will be constructed with gentle slope in   the bottom and with   interconnected   drains

Owner /  Owner’s Engineer   shall also reserve the right to coordinate with sub - vendor directly or through  Bidder as required by them based on their discretion.  4.4   PAITING & COATING REQUIREMENTS  Painting   requirements of the equipment, structures, piping etc. present in the power plant are  covered here . The painting system should ensure optimal protection of the installation with  minimum need for maintenance, environmentally friendly & easy to apply. Stat utory and Local  regulations shall be strictly followed in selection and application of paint.  4.4.1   General  a)   All paints &   paint material used   shall be procured from approved manufacturers   and applied   as per their coating  system data sheet.  b)   In general shop fabricated equipment will be delivered to the site coated with a shop applied system or the  manufacturer's standard finish in accordance with the requirements of this specification.  c)   Necessary precautions shall be provided to all equipment, structures to protect other surfaces from abrasive  blasting, coating over spray and spatter.  d)   The Contractor shall submit the recommended painting for the project, procedure for its application, for review  and approval by the Customer. Contractor shall maintain proper painting records. Disposal of painting waste  shall comply with applicable laws, r egulations. Necessary safety precautions shall be implemented.  4.4.2   Paint Application

Exposure to concentrations  above the maximum allowable limits shall be avoided. The storage, the method of application and  the working conditions significantly affect the degree of toxicity of paints and varn ishes.  (b)   Personnel shall be prohibited from eating, drinking and smoking at the workplace.  (c)   Wearing of personal protection clothing (gloves, helmet, screen, safety shoes, working clothes)  shall be compulsory.  (d)   Efficient ventilation systems shall be designed and installed so that the atmosphere of the work  place continuously remains healthy and the toxic and dangerous products are collected and  discharged.  (e)   Disposal of painting waste resulting from painting, shall comply with applicable laws, regulations,  ordinances etc., of the local authority, state or the nation pertains to the work and coating  materials.  (f)   Health and Safety rules in effect on site or in the production unit shall be strictly followed by the  Contractor's and/or its sub - contractor's personnel.  4.4.4   Inspection  (a)   Inspection will be performed by an inspector authorised by Purchaser / Engineer. The painting work  shall be subjected to inspection by purchaser / engineer at all times. The vendor shall offer stage  inspection on surface preparation, primer application, su bsequent coats and paint qualification  test.  (b)   Vendor shall provide the expert technical services of the paint manufactures as and when required  by purchaser / engineer to ensure both the surface preparation and applications are carried out as  per their recommendations.  4.4.5   Suggested   Colour   Codes for Painting  Colours for various equipment in power plant shall be as per IS - 5. Quality  Assurance/Quality   Control   procedures   followed   by   the   Bidder   shall   address   the   QA/QC  requirements during the following phases of the project:  a.   Engineering  b.   Procurement  c.   Construction including testing and commissioning.  Bidder shall submit documents to demonstrate that the Bidder and his sub - vendors possess, QA/QC  procedures meeting the requirements set forth in this section. Bidder shall submit the quality  assurance plans for all other equipments under this contract alon g with his bid. These quality plans  and reference documents/standards etc. shall be subject to Owner’s approval without which  manufacture shall not proceed. In the approved Quality Plans, the Owner/ Owner’s Engineer   shall  identify the Customer Hold Points.   A Customer Hold Point signifies a stage in any item of work which  requires documented proof of approval based on tests/checks which shall be carried out in  presence of the Owner or his   Owner’s Engineer   and beyond which the work shall not proceed  without consent of the Owner/ Owner’s Engineer   in writing.  4.2   Engineering  The Bidder shall prepare and submit a project Quality Assurance Procedure that covers the  engineering phase of the Works. The procedure shall be designed for this project and shall address  the specifics of how the Bidder will control, monitor and verify t he requirements contained in this  section. The Bidder shall utilise all of his QA/QC resources, manuals and procedures when preparing  the plan.   Owner /   Owner’s Engineer   will review the plan prior to utilization and upon issue the  plan will become the b asis by which Owner /   Owner’s Engineer   will evaluate the Bidder’s  engineering QA/QC activities as they relate to this project. Calibration of test equipment  Calibration of tools, gauges, instruments and other measuring and testing devices shall be included.  QA personnel qualification and certification  The qualification required of QA/QC personnel shall be outlined.  Quality assurance/Quality control records  The system which shall ensure that all quality assurance records are maintained for ready reference  and shall also provide for the accumulation, evaluation, retention and distribution of QA/QC  records.  Interface with statutory authorities  Participation of statutory authorities, which require testing, witnessing and inspection shall be  provided.  Audits  Requirements for audits to verify   implementation of the QA/QC program on a regular basis shall be  included.  Mechanical completion acceptance  Bidder shall perform final inspection of the Work in accordance with the Contract documents.  Periodic reporting of QA/QC activities (once a month or earlier) as required shall be submitted.  Quality Assurance/Quality Control Plan  Bidder shall furnish a detailed Quality Assurance/Quality Control Plan (QA/QC) for each equipment  / system covered under this specification for   Purchaser / Owner’s Engineer ’s review and approval.

All   Bidder 's employees shall be familiar with the Safety Policy and their role  and obligations in its implementation. Bidder shall develop and submit a comprehensive safety plan to ensure safety of equipment,  systems and all operating and maintenance personal at site during the commissioning activities.  Prior to commencement of any commissioning activities, a joint audit   team represented by Owner,  Engineer and Contractor shall do a detailed check of the system to ensure mechanical completion.

The  VENDOR shall provide all services to establish and maintain quality of workmanship in his Works  and that of his SUB - VENDORS to ensure the mechanical accurac y of components, compliance with  approved drawings, identification and acceptability of all materials, parts and Equipment.  For supply of systems, VENDOR shall, at the start of the 'Contract', furnish a total list of items in  their scope of work. This list, giving a brief description of the item, quantity, names of probable  SUB - VENDORS, and a blank column for agency for final   approval of drawings / data sheets, shall be  submitted for approval by   Owner’s Engineer . (The blank column shall be filled by   Owner’s Engineer .)  The list shall be submitted within two weeks from the date of LOI.  For systems and major items such as pressure/load bearing items, machineries etc., the VENDOR  shall furnish quality plan giving details of checks / tests to be conducted by them on material,  process, sub - assembly and assembly. These shall include requireme nts as prescribed in the  applicable specifications, codes and statutory requirements. The quality plan shall be reviewed by  the   Owner   /   Owner’s Engineer   and the stages to be witnessed and verified shall be indicated by the  Owner   / Owner’s Engineer   in the qu ality plan and approved.  The VENDOR shall give the   Owner’s Engineer   written notice of any material being ready for testing  as per format enclosed.   The clear notice period shall be seven (7) days for local inspection and  fifteen (15) days for outstation inspection. Such tests shall be to the VENDOR'S account except for  t he expenses of the   Owner’s Engineer . The   Owner’s Engineer , unless the inspection of the tests is  virtually waived, shall fix a date for inspection with the VENDOR and attend such tests within fifteen  (15) days of t he date on which the Equipment is notified as being ready for test / inspection failing  which, the VENDOR may proceed with the tests and shall forthwith forward to the   Owner’s  Engineer   duly certified copies of tests in triplicate. If the VENDOR fails to offer the Equipment for  inspection as per the agreed date, he is liable to pay for the time and expenses for the infructuous  visit of the   Owner’s Engineer .  In all cases where inspection and tests are required whether at the premises or Works of the  VENDOR or of any SUB - VENDOR or at laboratory, the VENDOR, except where otherwise specified,  shall provide free of charge all facilities such as labour, materials,   electricity, fuel, water, stores, test  bed, apparatus and instruments, laboratory tests etc. as may be required by the   Owner’s Engineer  or his authorised representative to carry out effectively such tests of the Equipment in accordance  with the 'Contract'   and shall give facilities to the   Owner’s Engineer   or to his authorised  representative to accomplish testing.

Failure of the   Owner’s  Engineer   to take such an action shall not prevent the VENDOR from proceeding with the Work. --- Page 76 ---
Client   –   Hindalco Industries Ltd  Project   –   2X30 MW CGPP   TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR MAIN PLANT SYSTEM  TCE.13 833 A - ME - 6002 - 6 001, Rev - R 0   Page   74   of   342  The equipment covered under this specification shall be stored in the type of storage as  recommended by the Manufacturer / Owner /   Owner’s Engineer .  4.10.3   CONSTRUCTION OF STORES  Normally, the Owner makes available the space, at terms specified elsewhere, for the Contractor  to construct the necessary stores as specified.  To the extent feasible, materials should be stored near the point of erection. The storage areas  should have adequate unloading and handling facilities with adequate passage space for movement  of material handing equipment such as cranes, fork lift trucks,   etc. The storage of materials shall be  properly planned to minimise wasteful handling during retrieval of items required for erection.  The outdoor storage areas as well as semi - closed stores shall be provided with adequate drainage  facilities to prevent water logging. The Contractor shall check the satisfactoriness of these facilities  prior to monsoon.  The stores sheds shall be built in conformity with fire safety requirements. The stores shall be  provided with adequate lights and fire extinguishers. ‘No smoking’ signs shall be placed at strategic  locations. Safety precautions shall be strictly enforced.  In case of storage of certain equipment / materials, it may be necessary to maintain specified  temperature ranges. In such cases, it is the responsibility of the Contractor to provide adequate  heating / air conditioning facilities on round the clock basis.   The Contractor shall check that space  heaters, heating bulbs and air conditioners are kept energised continuously as applicable. In the  event there is a prolonged failure of electric power, rendering such air conditioners / space heaters  inoperative, the   Contractor shall make alternative arrangements to ensure that items under storage  do not suffer any damage / deterioration on this account.  Adequate lighting facility shall be provided by the Contractor in storage areas and storage sheds  and security personnel positioned to ensure enforcement of security measures to prevent theft and  loss of materials entrusted to him by the Owner. The Contrac tor shall carry out regular inventory  of materials received, issued and erected and notify the Owner of any loss when noticed.  The Contractor shall provide adequate number of competent stores personnel including store -  keepers, clerical staff, inspection engineers, watchmen and security staff to efficiently store and  maintain the equipment / material entrusted to him.  Where necessary, the Contractor shall provide necessary space heating and other protective care  to equipment in case it will take a long time to haul them to the stores. Any equipment left in the  open under such conditions shall be, if required, covered wi th tarpaulin.  All inflammable items should be stored in separate location with proper fencing with adequate  safety demarcation & precautions.  4.10.4   MAINTENANCE DURING STORAGE
--- Page 77 ---
Client   –   Hindalco Industries Ltd  Project   –   2X30 MW CGPP   TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR MAIN PLANT SYSTEM  TCE.13 833 A - ME - 6002 - 6 001, Rev - R 0   Page   75   of   342  Where unloading from the carriers and transport to warehouses is excluded from the Contractor’s  scope, the Contractor shall take over the materials from the Owner’s storage area / sheds.  Where materials is unloaded by the Owner before the Contractor arrives at site or even when the  Contractor is already at site, the Owner can hand over the material to the Contractor and thereupon  it will be the responsibility of the Contractor to store and   maintain these material in a proper and  approved manner.  While taking over the materials, the Contractor shall carry out inventory and inspection of the same  and submit a report of his observations to the Owner. After taking over of the equipment /  materials, the Contractor is solely responsible for the same unt il handing over to the Owner the  erected plant and surplus materials, if any. The Contractor is responsible for maintenance of the  equipment / materials in storage, during erection and until commissioning including protection of  the same against theft, ele ments of nature and corrosion damages. In case commissioning of the  equipment is delayed, the Contractor shall be responsible for maintenance of the erected plant and  equipment as directed by Owner /   Owner’s Engineer .  The Contractor shall repack the equipment after inspection and store the same in an orderly  manner, preserving their identification slips, tags and instruction booklets, etc., required during  erection. The storage of   materials shall be equipment - wise. Loose parts shall be stored in sheds on  racks, preserving the identification marks and tags in good condition. The group codes shall be  displayed on the racks.  The Contractor shall carry out frequent inspection of materials stored and also extend necessary  facilities to the Owner /   Owner’s Engineer   to carry out such inspection, when considered necessary.  The Contractor shall implement the suggestions of the Owner /   Owner’s Engineer   in this  connection.  At no time shall   any materials be stored directly on ground. All materials shall be stored minimum  200 mm above the ground on proper platform made up of RCC/MS channels/wooden sleepers to  avoid water contact with equipment during heavy rainfall.  Welded and cast units shall be stored in such manner that there is no danger of distortion.  All the storing area should be monitored regularly or protected for damages created by Rats/ or  any other reason.  All special measures to prevent corrosion shall be taken by the Contractor.  Materials which carry protective coating shall not be wrapped in paper, cloth, etc., as these are  liable to absorb and retain moisture. The materials without coating should be packed in VCI bags  to avoid rust. The Contractor shall inspect the materials an d in case of signs of wear or damages to  protective coating, that portion shall be cleaned with carbon tetra chloride or any other approved  solution and coated with an approved protective paint. In case of VCI, damaged one should be
--- Page 78 ---
Client   –   Hindalco Industries Ltd  Project   –   2X30 MW CGPP   TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR MAIN PLANT SYSTEM  TCE.13 833 A - ME - 6002 - 6 001, Rev - R 0   Page   76   of   342  changed immediately after notice.   Complete record of all such observations and protective  measures taken shall be maintained.  The grease recommended for protection of surfaces against rusting is refined from petroleum oil  with approved additives, grease shall be only in traces.  The protection offered by grease does not last more than six to twelve months.  The rust preventive oil for application on machined / fabricated components shall meet the  following requirements.  •   Kinematic viscosity at 50 0 C is 400 CST.  •   Flash point in open vessel is 240 0 C Minimum.  •   Acidity number 2 mg.  •   Maximum percentage of water 0.08.  •   Protection offered normally does not last more than six to twelve months.  While handling the equipment, no dragging on the ground is permitted. The Contractor shall submit  his scheme for handling major pieces of equipment for approval by the Owner /   Owner’s Engineer  prior to actual handling of the same. In cases where a scheme is recommended by the Owner /  Manufacturer, it shall be strictly adhered to.  The Contractor shall periodically inspect materials with specific reference to   -  •   Ingress of moisture and corrosion damages.  •   Damage to protective coating.  •   Open ends in pipes, vessels and equipment  •   In case any open ends are noticed, the Contractor shall get them capped.  •   Insulation of electrical equipment.  Regular insulation checks are essential and these values shall be properly logged.  •   Any damages to equipment / materials.  In case of any damages, these shall be promptly notified to the Owner. In all cases, the  repairs / rectification shall be carried out strictly as directed by the Owner /   Owner’s  Engineer .  If such repairs / rectification is necessitated due to causes attributable to the Contractor,  such repairs / rectification shall be carried out at no extra cost to the Owner. In other cases,  compensation for such repair / rectification works will be as mu tually agreed to.  In case such damages are attributable to the Contractor and if, in the opinion of the Owner  /   Owner’s Engineer , the Contractor is not competent to carry out necessary repairs /  rectification, the Owner shall get this done by any other agency and recover the  expenditure incurred by him from the Contractor.

Wherever   the   specific   recommendations   of   the  Manufacturer / Owner /   Owner’s Engineer   are at variance with what is indicated below, the former  shall prevail.  Special Storage   –   Air Conditioned  The following materials are normally stored in air conditioned storage.  •   Welding Electrodes  These deteriorate under adverse conditions of storage and are preferably stored in   air -  conditioned   storage. As an alternative, if approved, these can also be stored in hot boxes /  ovens.   It is essential to regularly inspect the conditions of electrodes and ensure that  temperature is   always maintained within specified range .  •   Ball, needle and roller bearings:  These shall be stored in their original packing.  •   Photographic materials and films.  These shall be stored in their original moisture vapour proof   packages.  •   Rubber Goods:  These shall be protected against sunlight and artificial light as well as high humidity conditions.  •   Electronic instruments and components.  These shall be stored in sealed packages.   Bidder shall arrange racks and proper storage for  sensitive instruments like transmitters, analyser sensors and for electronic components (if any)  Closed storage  The following materials are normally stored in closed storage.  •   Cement bags.  •   All small parts / components liable to be misplaced / lost. These shall include the following:  a.   Small structural cleats, bolts, nuts, washers, etc.  High tensile bolts and nuts shall be stored in separate sealed boxes with proper identification  to avoid mix up with other items.  b.   Pipe hangers, spring hangers, etc.  The   identification numbers on spring hangers shall be carefully preserved during storage.  Stainless steel and copper tubes, pipe fittings, etc.  c.   Electrical clamps, cable boxes, cable glands, ferrules, fuses, control cables, copper cables and  grounding   strips, push button switches, etc.  d.   Pilot valves, pull rods, keys, control valves. These shall be greased, wrapped in paraffin paper  and stored in packing boxes.

Large  diameter conduits / pipes in boxes.  •   Boiler, drum, tubes, tube panels and headers.  •   Air pre - heater tube plates.  •   Pressure vessels, reaction towers and columns.  •   Fabricated vessels and tanks shall have their openings firmly blanked.  •   Cable drums.  •   Large power transformers.  4.10.6   SPECIFIC GUIDELINES FOR STORAGE  Given below are the guidelines for certain other major equipment / materials. These are broad  guidelines only and storage shall be strictly as directed by the Manufacturer / Owner /   Owner’s  Engineer .  Rotating Equipment such as Pumps  These are normally stored in assembled condition in closed / semi - closed storage. When stored in  semi - closed storage, they shall be covered with tarpaulin and bed plates shall rest fully on sleepers.  The Contractor is responsible for proper lubrication of these equipment as directed.  The gear boxes and oil sumps shall be kept filled with recommended lubricating gear oil. The  couplings and bearings shall be covered with approved grease and nozzle flanges screwed with caps  or suitably plugged.  The shaft and internals shall be, if so directed, given a fresh coat of approved protective paint.  Pumps, if delivered in non - assembled condition, shall be stored with the parts properly greased  and wrapped in VCI bags. Smaller parts are to be stored on racks whereas larger parts on sleepers  in closed storage. Occasional flushing with hot lubricating o il may have to be carried out, if so  directed, in the cases of equipment such as boiler feed pump.  The shafts of rotating equipment shall be turned by 90 0 C every week to avoid rotor journals from  rusting and to ensure that shaft is free to rotate.  The Contractor shall maintain record of lubrication with details of lubricants used as well as  protective measures carried out.  Piping and Valves
--- Page 82 ---
Client   –   Hindalco Industries Ltd  Project   –   2X30 MW CGPP   TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR MAIN PLANT SYSTEM  TCE.13 833 A - ME - 6002 - 6 001, Rev - R 0   Page   80   of   342  All pipes shall be stored on sleeper beds with open ends securely capped. The bevelled ends shall  be   protected with a coating of approved grease. If so directed, inside surfaces of piping shall be  coated with approved protective solution.  Pipes shall be neatly arranged and stacked for easy identification and retrieval.  Pipes shall be blown with air prior to installation and it shall be ensured that there is no obstruction.  Valves shall be stored with the discs screwed down on the seat so that packing surfaces are not  exposed to dust. All machined surfaces shall be covered with grease. Preservation of identification  tags is essential to avoid installation in a wrong service.   Smaller valves shall be stored in closed  storage whereas large CI valves may be stored in semi - closed storage.  Pipe fittings shall be stored on racks in closed storage in packed condition, with openings suitably  capped.  Switch gear & Control Panels  Depending on whether these are of indoor or outdoor design, these shall be stored in closed / semi -  closed storage.  The spaced heaters, where provided, shall be kept connected with power supply irrespective of  mode of storage where space heaters are not provided, use of adequate number of heating bulbs  is recommended.  Insulation resistance shall be measured at regular intervals and megger values properly logged.  Transformers  It is advisable to assemble and set the transformer filled with oil in its permanent location soon  after receipt even if it is not likely to be put in service for a while. In case of gas filled transformers,  a positive pressure should be maintained durin g storage as recommended by the Manufacturer. If  transformer is to be stored partially or fully dis - assembled, it shall be protected against moisture  ingress / absorbtion by keeping them immersed in transformer oil or as recommended by the  Manufacture / Ow ner /   Owner’s Engineer .  Prior to charging any transformer, it is essential to check the satisfactoriness of insulation values  and di - electric strength of oil.  Cells, Accumulators, Batteries  These shall be stored in a dry and well ventilated place and protected from extreme cold. Internal  parts of large cells are loose and the cells should be handled with care to avoid displacement of  internals. Saw dust shall never be used for packing batte ries as it is hygroscopic.  Cables
--- Page 83 ---
Client   –   Hindalco Industries Ltd  Project   –   2X30 MW CGPP   TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR MAIN PLANT SYSTEM  TCE.13 833 A - ME - 6002 - 6 001, Rev - R 0   Page   81   of   342  Cable drums if stored indoors, shall be in dry, ventilated stores. Cables are normally supplied with  ends sealed. When a length is cut, the end should be resealed. The drums shall be stored with  adequate spacing between them for better air circulation.  Motors  Besides general procedures such as   regular inspection and recording of insulation resistances, in  the case of LT motors, if so recommended, low voltage current flow through the windings may be  maintained during storage.  Instruments  These are stored on racks in closed storage and in air conditioned storage if so recommended.  These shall be stored in their packages in dry condition and shall be handled with extreme care.  Instrument flanges and nozzles, if directed, shall be coated with technical Vaseline.  In the case of fragile   instruments, the storage shall be in a separate zone away from where heavy  materials are stored to minimise danger of damage.  Steel  Reinforcement steel : Reinforcement steel shall not be kept in direct contact with the ground but  stacked on top of an arrangement of timber sleepers or the like. If stored for long periods, these  may be coated with cement wash before stacking to prevent loose scales and rust . Fabricated  reinforcement shall be carefully stored to prevent damage, distortion, corrosion and deterioration.  Structural steel : Structural steel could safely be stored in open storage but with sufficient space in  between for handling and crane movement. All steel shall be stored 300 mm above ground on  suitable packing to avoid damage. It should be stored according to sizes and se ctions preferably in  the order required for erection. The area for open storage should be properly graded to avoid water  logging in the storage area. Steel shall not be stored in the vicinity of areas where excavation or  grading will be don e. Scratched or abraded steel shall be given a coat of primer for protection after  unloading and handling prior to erection.   All milled and machined surfaces shall be properly  protected from rust / corrosion by suitable coating.  Cement  Cement shall be stored in bags in a separate shed away from where mechanical and electrical  equipment are stored to minimise the possibility of cement dust settling on the equipment.  The storage shed where cement is stored shall be absolutely water tight. The flooring shall be a 15  mm thick concrete slab laid on a dry course soling or as recommended. The ground shall be drained  away from the shed to prevent accumulation of water in it s vicinity.  The bags shall be neatly stacked with adequate passage in between for easy retrieval. The cement  shall be issued on ‘First - In - First - Out’ basis.
--- Page 84 ---
Client   –   Hindalco Industries Ltd  Project   –   2X30 MW CGPP   TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR MAIN PLANT SYSTEM  TCE.13 833 A - ME - 6002 - 6 001, Rev - R 0   Page   82   of   342  In case different grades of cement are stored in the same shed, each grade shall be stacked  separately to avoid mix up and clear indication boards shall be provided to identify different grades.  Synthetic Paints  These have limited life and as a safeguard against possible deterioration, the drums shall be  inverted at regular intervals as specified to minimise risk of hard settling of pigments.  Slings & Wire Ropes  All slings and wire ropes shall be coated with approved grease and hung from racks in rolled  condition.  4.2   DOCUMENTATION  The Contractor’s store - keeping function will include maintaining various records as required by the  Owner /   Owner’s Engineer . These records shall include but not be limited to:  Supplier wise record of equipment / material received stored and issued for   erection as well as  stock position.  Record of inspection and repairs carried out, protective measures and lubrication of equipment in  storage as well as erected until the same are taken over by the Owner.
