Client   –   Hindalco Industries Ltd  Project   –   2X30 MW CGPP   TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR MAIN PLANT SYSTEM  TCE.13 833 A - ME - 6002 - 6 001, Rev - R 0   Page   83   of   342  CHAPTER - 5  MECHANICAL DESIGN REQUIREMENT & EQUIPMENT  SPECIFICATION
--- Page 86 ---
Client   –   Hindalco Industries Ltd  Project   –   2X30 MW CGPP   TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR MAIN PLANT SYSTEM  TCE.13 833 A - ME - 6002 - 6 001, Rev - R 0   Page   84   of   342  5.0   MECHANICAL DESIGN REQUIREMENTS & EQUIPMENT SPECIFICATION  5.1   GENERAL  5.1.1   Each s team generator and turbine generator unit shall be designed to produce maximum   continuous  output power   of 30 MW   with steam parameters of   66   kg/cm 2   (a) pressure and   485 0 C   temperature  at the turbine inlet.   Two units shall be designed to operate   independently having interconnection  with each other so that any turbine can be   operated by any of the boilers. Also, provision shall be  kept in the entire system to integrate another unit of similar capacity in future with minimum  retrofitting works.  5.1.2   The unit shall be designed to operate as a base load station. However, continuous operation under  cyclic modes during certain periods of   a day   is also envisaged. The design should cover adequate  provision for quick startup and loading of the unit to full load at a fast rate and apart from constant  pressure operation would also have the facility for sliding pressure operation. The design of the  e quipment and control system would permit participation of the machine in automatic load  frequency   control.  5.1.3   The CGPP shall be designed to generate its rated power while maintaining the MP and LP steam  supply to refinery at rated parameters   while meeting its internal consumption of steam and power  during normal operating hours .   Also, it shall be capable of supplying MP and LP process steam during  peak operation of refinery. The steam and power demand   during normal and peak hours   of each unit  is indicated below:  Sl No  Pressure ( bara )   Temp e rature ( 0 C)   Refinery Demand  Type   Min   Max  Minimum  Mechanical  Design  Min   Max  Minimum  Mechanical  design  Normal   Peak  1   MP (Medium  Pressure)   1 2   1 4   15.4   190   2 2 0   2 3 0   75   TPH   90   TPH  2   LP (Low  Pressure   4 . 3   6 . 5   7   16 0   19 0   20 0   115   TPH   140  TPH  3  Refinery  Power  Requirement  19.8  MW  21.8  MW  Notes:  1.   The steam and power requirement is solely demand of refinery and shall be supplied at CGPP  boundary. Bidder to   consider consumption of auxiliary steam and power   for CGPP   and design  the system accordingly.  2.   The MP and LP steam parameters to be made available at plant boundary.  5.1.4   The boiler, turbine -   generator and associated equipment and systems shall be of proven   design, using  proven materials with well established physical and chemical properties   and as appropriate to the  service as intended.
--- Page 87 ---
Client   –   Hindalco Industries Ltd  Project   –   2X30 MW CGPP   TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR MAIN PLANT SYSTEM  TCE.13 833 A - ME - 6002 - 6 001, Rev - R 0   Page   85   of   342  5.1.5   The unit and the auxiliaries shall be designed for maximum reliability and availability, operability and  maintainability. The bidder shall specifically state the design features incorporated to achieve the  high degree of reliability and availability.  5.1.6   The unit shall be suitably designed and provided with necessary instrumentation for rapid starting,  loading and unloading without causing undue vibration, distortion, differential expansion or  producing excessive thermal stresses. The design of the control   system and relevant equipment shall  adhere to the fail safe operation at all system conditions. The fail safe operation signifies that loss of  signal/air/power supply, loss of excitation or failure of any component will not cause hazardous  conditions and   at the same time prevent occurrence of false trips.  5.1.7   The work falling under the purview of Indian Boiler Regulations (IBR) shall completely meet or exceed  all the requirements of the latest edition of IBR. Any other standard acceptable to IBR can also be  considered, provided the requirements of that standard   are equivalent or exceed IBR requirements.  5.2   BOILER AND   ACCESSORIES  5.2.1   Boiler and auxiliaries shall be complete with necessary equipment,   structural , foundation bolts,  piping, valves, fittings and painting, instrumentation and control equipment with installation  hardware, ducting, cabling, electrical system/equipment and all other associated items for safe and  reliable operation of the plant.  5.2.2   Each steam generat or unit shall be rated to deliver for conditions specified in Attachment 1: Technical  data sheet. The steam purity shall be ensured as per the requirement of steam turbine and other  operating auxiliary   plant over the entire load range.  5.2.3   The steam generator shall be of CFBC technology, sub - critical, single drum, natural circulation and  non - reheat type. The boiler shall have capability to fire   100% Indian coal, 100% Indonesian Coal and  100% South African Coal or any combination of coals specified in this specification   and utilize   Low  Diesel   Oil   ( LDO )   or Bio - Diesel   as start - up fuel and during low load operation.   Also, the steam  generator shall be suitable to fire biomass   (Max   10% )   along with coal.  5.2.4   The steam generator shall consist of combustion chamber, cyclone/dust collectors, Siphon seal/seal  pot. Bidder may consider hot cyclone or cold cyclone or internal recirculation / U - beam technology  as per their   standard manufacturing practice. The similar design should be in satisfactory operation  for minimum two years.  5.2.5   Bidder   shall   design   the   combustion   chamber   for   staged   combustion   for   better   combustor  temperature control and also to limit the formation of nitrogen oxides.  5.2.6   All pressure part tubes and headers shall be of seamless type. Welded tubes are not acceptable.  5.2.7   Suitable number of safety valves in drum, superheater and start up vent along with silencers shall be  provided.  5.2.8   A nitrogen based inerting system shall be provided for preservation of steam generator during shut  down.
--- Page 88 ---
Client   –   Hindalco Industries Ltd  Project   –   2X30 MW CGPP   TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR MAIN PLANT SYSTEM  TCE.13 833 A - ME - 6002 - 6 001, Rev - R 0   Page   86   of   342  5.2.9   The boiler structure shall be designed to support all loads located on the structure. In addition, other  loadings may be introduced on the structure from piping, enclosures, adjacent structures and  equipment. Loading due to wind and seismic conditions shal l be considered   in the design.  COMBUSTION CHAMBER  5.2.10   The combustion chamber assembly shall be arranged for natural circulation of water/steam. The  combustion chamber shall be a gas tight enclosure. The areas where a reducing atmosphere, erosion  are expected, the same shall be refractory lined and protected.   The combustion chamber design shall  be suitable to provide pressure withstanding capacity as per guideline of NFPA 85.  5.2.11   The combustion temperature shall be uniform in the entire volume of the furnace and shall be in the  range of 830 - 8 7 0°C and shall be optimum considering sorbent dosing in the boiler. The particle  velocity in the combustor shall be maximum 5 m/sec. The same shall   not cause any explosion in any  of the fuel path.  5.2.12   The circulation system shall ensure adequate circulation ratio during rapid start - ups and load changes  to prevent overheating of the water wall tubes with maximum possible heat flux in the furnace.  5.2.13   Heat transfer surfaces in furnace region shall be avoided as far as possible. If any heat transfer section  is placed in the combustor, suitable measures shall be provided to protect from erosion problems for  the same. The placement of heat transfer section s in the combustor by provision of suitable vertical  panels, horizontal panels or wing walls would be acceptable provided, Bidder has supplied such  designs and such units are in successful operation for a period of not less than five years.  5.2.14   Suitable allowance shall be provided for the water wall, economizer tubes and in - furnace super  heater to counter the erosion conditions. Bidder shall clearly bring out in his offer the proven  preventive measures provided for the water walls and in furnace   heat transfer sections to counter  erosion.  CASING  5.2.15   A gas tight enclosure shall be designed for the entire boiler from the lower furnace to the ID fan  suction considering explosion/implosion forces developed due to furnace pressure excursions. The  combustor shall be of membrane type water wall construction.   In other areas where welded wall  construction is used, no skin casing is required, but skin casing of steel plates shall be used for all  enclosures and behind all wide spaced tubes where refractory is used.  CYCLONES  5.2.16   The cyclones shall be designed to provide a high recycle rate in order to ensure a uniform  temperature in the furnace. The inlet and outlet temperature difference in cyclone shall be as  minimum as possible   and, in any case,   shall not exceed +10 Deg C.
--- Page 89 ---
Client   –   Hindalco Industries Ltd  Project   –   2X30 MW CGPP   TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR MAIN PLANT SYSTEM  TCE.13 833 A - ME - 6002 - 6 001, Rev - R 0   Page   87   of   342  5.2.17   The cyclones offered by bidder should be water / steam cooled. If the water/steam cyclone is having  refractory liner, the refractory shall be suitable to withstand the temperature applications and shall  be capable of withstanding thermal expansion and ther mal shocks. The cyclone design shall ensure  that there shall not be any choking / slagging in cyclone bottom outlet and seal pot zones.  5.2.18   If other designs of separators such as non - cooled or refractory cyclones are proposed by bidder, the  same shall be supported by suitable justification with good operating experience for more than two  years.  5.2.19   If any alternative solid collecting technology other than cyclone is proposed, the Bidder shall indicate  the same with design details along with their experience of the same in a boiler of similar capacity in  successful operation for more than two years.  SIPHON SEAL/SEAL POT  5.2.20   The Bidder shall provide proven siphon seal box/pot design. The siphon seal shall be designed to  allow free solids flow from low - pressure region (cyclone outlet) to the higher - pressure condition  (combustor/furnace) without air locks and moving parts. Air p urges at strategic locations on return  leg in seal box shall be provided to assist solids flow to the combustor.  5.2.21   Other solid re - injection device is also acceptable provided, Bidder has already supplied such designs  of similar capacity and the same are in successful operation for a period of not less than two years.  STEAM DRUM  5.2.22   The   drum   internals   system   will   comprise   cyclone   separators   and   steam   washers   or  scrubbers/moisture separators, baffles, hood, internal feed piping and sampling connections. The  design of the drum steam/water separator ensures that the steam quality leaving the drum is as per  the requirement of steam turbine generator   over the entire load range. The drum dished ends at  both ends shall be provided with properly sized hinged manhole doors.  5.2.23   All nozzles shall be of the long body type so that this will extend through the steam generator lagging.  The ends of nozzles smaller than 50 mm shall have plain ends for butt welding. The nozzles 50 mm  and larger shall have bevelled ends for butt - welding.   All nozzles shall be of full penetration welds and  shall be ground flush with the inner diameter of the drum.  SUPERHEATER  5.2.24   The super heater shall be of proven design and shall be designed to maintain superheat steam  temperatures at super heater outlet over the entire steam temperature control range (i.e. from 50%  BMCR   to 100%   BMCR   rating) under the following conditions:  ▪   With the steam generator heat absorption surface condition varying from clean to  normally expected and permissible fouling limits;
--- Page 90 ---
Client   –   Hindalco Industries Ltd  Project   –   2X30 MW CGPP   TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR MAIN PLANT SYSTEM  TCE.13 833 A - ME - 6002 - 6 001, Rev - R 0   Page   88   of   342  ▪   With minimum possible use of desuperheating spray;  ▪   With permissible and normally expected unbalance or stratification in flue gas flow;  ▪   With all specified mode of fuel burning;  5.2.25   Bidder shall provide necessary measures to prevent erosion of super heater tubes. Interstage  desuperheater shall be installed at one or more points in super heater sections to ensure rapid  temperature/control response.  5.2.26   Complete superheater temperature control system with necessary attemperators, piping, block,  control, isolation, drain, vent, check valves and instrumentation & controls shall be provided.  5.2.27   The super heater supports or hangers exposed to hot gases within the setting shall be of a heat -  resisting material suitable for long life under the service condition.  5.2.28   All SH tubes shall have an allowance of minimum 1 mm towards erosion over and above the designed  wall thickness.  5.2.29   The maximum flue gas velocity in the convection zone shall be limited to 12 m/sec so as to limit  erosion problems.  5.2.30   The maximum tube metal temperature (skin temperature) shall be below the oxidation limit as  applicable to the material.  ECONOMIZER  5.2.31   The economiser shall be of non - steaming type and shall have provision for recirculation of water  while starting the unit. The economiser tube banks shall be of in - line design with bare tubes. Anti  vibration baffles/ plates shall be properly located for gas   distribution.  5.2.32   Suitable SS shields shall be provided on tubes connecting to headers inside the flue gas path.  5.2.33   All economizer tubes shall have an additional wall thickness of minimum 1 mm as erosion allowance  in addition to the calculated design thickness.  5.2.34   The heating surfaces shall be readily accessible for inspection, cleaning and maintenance and  replacement. Drains and vents shall be provided to ensure that complete venting during a fill and  draining during shutdown is achieved.  5.3   AIR AND FLUE GAS   SYSTEM  (a)   The air and flue gas system shall be designed to meet the requirements of 100% BMCR  conditions and with the specified fuels. Ducts, flues and wind boxes shall be capable of
--- Page 91 ---
Client   –   Hindalco Industries Ltd  Project   –   2X30 MW CGPP   TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR MAIN PLANT SYSTEM  TCE.13 833 A - ME - 6002 - 6 001, Rev - R 0   Page   89   of   342  withstanding a minimum continuous operating and transient design pressure as per guideline  of NFPA 85.  (b)   The air and flue gas system shall consist of   2   x   7 0% Primary air fans, 2 x   7 0% Secondary air fans  and 2 x   7 0% Induced draft fans. In each type of fans,   2 0% margin on flow will be provided i.e.  both fans operating together will be able to meet 1 4 0 % of BMCR requirement. This margin will  be considered based on worst coal firing, frequency of 50 Hz, temperature of maximum  ambient plus 5 deg C for PA fans & SA fans and maximum flue gas temperature plus 15 deg C  for ID fans. Silencers shall be provided   for fans.  (c)   Forced oil lubrication system for each of the fans and blowers shall be provided consisting of  2 x 100% oil pumps each with motor, 2 x 100% oil coolers and filters; one (1) oil tank associated  valves, piping etc. Bidder can also offer splash type oil lubri cation system as per their standard  practice.  (d)   Annubar type flow meter   shall be provided   for the measurement of air flow of PA & SA fans at  individual limbs and total air flow after air - preheater.  (e)   PA, SA, and ID fans shall be   provided with vibration isolation system (spring mounted) or  conventional block/frame type RCC foundation. The fans shall be provided with vibration  monitoring equipment.  (f)   The system shall also consist of blowers / fans meant for seal and purge air, ash coolers, seal  pots/loop seals. The number of blowers / fans shall be selected keeping 100% standby for each  of the above system provided with necessary interconnections.  (g)   Adequate number of interconnecting, isolating and control dampers with drives shall be  provided in air & flue gas ducts to permit proper operations. The control dampers shall be  pneumatically operated and isolation dampers shall be electrically operated.  (h)   All gas and air ducts shall be designed to ensure uniform distribution of gas and air flow to  the various   portions of the unit. These ducts shall consist of turning vanes / baffle plates,  stiffening devices, metallic/non - metallic expansion joints,   bracings, access doors and brackets,  support structures and hangers as necessary.  (i)   All the fans and blowers shall be provided with electrically operated hoists both on fan and  motor side.  5.4   TUBULAR AIR PREHEATER (TAPH)  (j)   Bidder shall include tubular air pre - heater for preheating the air. Air - preheaters shall be  designed in such a way that cold end elements/tubes should not get affected due to acid dew  point corrosion of flue gas. Suitable material or preventive measures sh all be provided.  (k)   Air - side bypass shall be provided for primary and secondary air to avoid cold end corrosion  during   start - up and low load operation.
--- Page 92 ---
Client   –   Hindalco Industries Ltd  Project   –   2X30 MW CGPP   TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR MAIN PLANT SYSTEM  TCE.13 833 A - ME - 6002 - 6 001, Rev - R 0   Page   90   of   342  5.5   FUEL FEEDING SYSTEM  (a)   The coal burning system shall be designed to meet the requirement of steam generator at  100% BMCR condition for the range of coals specified.  (b)   T hree   no. coal bunker for each boiler with total storage capacity of   24   hours shall be provided.  Also, provision to be given to fill biomass in one of the bunkers within 1/3 rd   of its volume.  Isolation gates shall be provided at each bunker outlet. The gates shall be dust proof, rack and  self cleaning type pinion. The gates shall be provided with chains for manual operation.  Suitable bunker emptying arrangement shall be provided.  (c)   The coal and biomass   density of 1 6 00 kg/m3 shall be   considered for structural design of  bunker. The storage capacity of bunker shall be selected considering coal density of 800 kg/m3  and 150 kg/m3 for biomass. All bracing and stiffening shall be on external side of hopper.  (d)   Suitable dust extraction / bunker ventilation system shall be provided bunkers. Minimum two  (2) number of Radar type level measuring instrument for each bunker shall be provided.  (e)   Gravimetric feeders shall be provided to extract coal /biomass from bunker outlet and also for  controlling feed rate of fuel depending on the load requirement of boiler. Proper sealing of  feeders shall be ensured during operation. Air required for sealing   can be taken from cold air  side of secondary air system. Variable frequency drive shall be provided for gravimetric  feeders.  (f)   Coal /biomass from gravimetric feeders shall be fed into the furnace through drag link  conveyors either directly or through the return legs of the siphon seals as per standard practice  of Bidder. Preventive measures shall be provided for sealing of fuel fe eding system from the  combustor and for proper distribution of coal inside combustor.  (g)   The feeders shall be designed with adequate wide range of capacity as well as reserve capacity  to allow for variation in size, quality and moisture content.  (h)   The Bidder shall choose the number of feeders suitably. The capacity of the feeders shall be so  selected that 100% BMCR requirement shall be met with N - 1 feeders in operation for any fuel  firing conditions.  (i)   Feeders shall be capable of reverse rotation also to facilitate bunker emptying through feeders.  (j)   All surfaces in contact with coal shall be made of corrosion and erosion resistant stainless steel  unless otherwise specified elsewhere in this specification.  (k)   At each coal feeder port, compressed air shall be provided to assist coal feeding. Access doors  shall be provided at appropriate location on each feeder. Suitable sight glasses shall be  provided on feeder casing for visual inspection of coal.  (l)   All surfaces in contact with coal shall be made of corrosion and erosion resistant stainless steel  unless otherwise specified in this specification.
--- Page 93 ---
Client   –   Hindalco Industries Ltd  Project   –   2X30 MW CGPP   TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR MAIN PLANT SYSTEM  TCE.13 833 A - ME - 6002 - 6 001, Rev - R 0   Page   91   of   342  5.6   LIMESTONE FEEDING SYSTEM  (a)   The powdered limestone feeding system shall be complete with bunkers, suitable feeding  system, isolation gates, chutes and etc to meet the required emission   specifications when  firing the range of coals specified.  (b)   One (1) no. limestone bunker for each boiler shall be considered with   24   hours storage  requirement for maximum sulphur capture under firing the design range of fuel. The powdered  limestone bunker shall be provided with pneumatic fluidising arrangements to ensure  uninterrupted flow of powdered limestone from the bunker. The dry   air required for the above  shall be from the service air system.  (c)   Isolation gates shall be provided at each powdered limestone bunker outlet. The gates shall be  of dust - proof, rack and self cleaning type pinion. The gates shall be provided with chains for  hand operation also.  (d)   Suitable dust extraction / bunker ventilation system shall be provided for each bunker.  Minimum two (2) no’s of Radar type level measuring instrument for each bunker shall be  provided.  (e)   The selection of number of feeders shall be such that with N - 1 feeders in operation, it shall  meet the   requirement of maximum sulphur capture under any fuel firing condition.  (f)   Rotary air lock feeders or any other suitable arrangement (based on standard practice of the  bidder) shall be provided in each feed stream to act as a pressure seal between combustion  chamber and feed system.  (g)   The downstream of rotary air lock feeders shall have motor operated isolating gate valves for  maintenance works with air poking arrangements with dry service air.  (h)   Bidder shall provide a suitable powdered limestone feeding system vent arrangement to  ensure good feeder stability.  5.7   BED MATERIAL FEEDING SYSTEM  (a)   For the first start - up of the CFBC steam generator and/or after a shutdown, in which bed  material is required to be fed to the combustor, Bidder shall offer one bed material bunker of  minimum capacity equal to thrice the bed material quantity required for   initial start - up  operation, along with conveying system to the combustor. Regular makeup to the combustor  during normal operation shall be fed from bed material bunker for solid inventory. Bed  material bunker shall be provided with vent filters.  (b)   The downstream of the bed material bunker shall have rotary air lock feeder with necessary  air sealing arrangements and dry service air for poking/cleaning. Rotary air lock feeders shall  be used to feed and control bed material to the combustor either dire ctly or through drag link  conveyors.
--- Page 94 ---
Client   –   Hindalco Industries Ltd  Project   –   2X30 MW CGPP   TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR MAIN PLANT SYSTEM  TCE.13 833 A - ME - 6002 - 6 001, Rev - R 0   Page   92   of   342  (c)   The downstream of the rotary air lock feeder shall have motor operated isolating gate valves  for maintenance works with air poking arrangements using seal and purge air.  (d)   Minimum two (2) number of Radar type level measuring instrument for the bunker shall be  provided. The initial supply of bed material (crushed refractory or sand) up to plant acceptance  is in the scope of the Bidder.  5.8   BED ASH REMOVAL SYSTEM  (a)   The total bed ash shall be based on maximum ash generated as well as gypsum inert and  unreacted lime under different combination of fuel firing as specified elsewhere for 100%  BMCR. Bidder shall indicate the percentage of total ash for which the bed ash sy stem is  designed and shall furnish the basis for the same.  (b)   Adequate redundancy shall be provided for ash drain lines to drain the bed ash from  combustor. The gate of the drains shall be pneumatically operated.  (c)   The bed ash drained from the combustor shall be cooled to a maximum temperature of 150 0 C  at bed ash cooler.   The bed ash cooler shall be water cooled type.   Bidder shall offer bed ash  collection and cooling system with standard ash cooler & knife gate valve (KGV). The bed ash  removal system (bed ash   cooler, ash cooling fan / blower for air cooled type and other  associated systems) shall be designed with N+1 redundancy for 100% BMCR under entire range  of fuel, wherein N is number of operating streams. Bidder may also use the air from fluidizing  fans wi th suitable controls. Bidder shall explore the feasibility to recover the heat from bed in  process.  5.9   FLY ASH REMOVAL SYSTEM  (a)   The total ash shall be based on the maximum ash generated firing design range of fuel as  specified to achieve 100% BMCR. Bidder shall indicate in his bid the percentage of total ash for  which the fly ash system is designed and shall furnish the basis for t he same.  (b)   The ash hoppers shall be designed for an ash   density of 1760 kg/m3 structurally and shall  provide 8 hours ash   storage firing entire design range of fuel with ash density of 800 kg/m3.  The hopper material thickness shall be minimum 6.00 mm. All bracing and stiffening shall be  on external side of hopper. The hoppers shall be suitably insulated on the exterior.  (c)   The hoppers shall be of pyramid shape with plate angle of at least 70° with the horizontal with  an   access door on each side of baffle. Ash hoppers for bag filter shall be provided with heating  elements on all sides of hopper. One man hole per hopper and approach to the access doors  shall be provided.  (d)   Each ash hopper shall be provided with a dust tight sealed hinged inspection door and an  access door of approximately 450 mm x 450 mm size. The hoppers shall be provided with the  required outlet connections.

Loop seals  (c)   In addition to the above if the bidder requires protective refractory in any other region, the  same shall also be included.  (d)   All refractory required for furnace wall, cyclone and other areas shall be of good operating  history and from reputed supplier who has supplied the same refractory material for the  services indicated above in a boiler of similar capacity or higher and has   undergone successful  operation for a period of not less than two years in one CFBC boiler.  (e)   For all refractory the materials, are to be inspected and cleared at the manufacturing works by  the CFBC refractory specialist or Third Party Inspection (TPI) and the entire refractory  application is to be carried out in his presence along with refractory   manufacturer supervision  at site.  5.11   START UP OIL AND IGNITION SYSTEM  (a)   Low   Diesel   Oil   ( LDO )   or Bio - diesel   shall be used as start - up/support oil system to bring the  furnace of the CFB steam generator up to the required operating temperature during start - up.  The Fuel oil system shall be designed to cater to maximum of 30%   BMCR   requirement. Fuel  burners shall have High Energy arc (HEA) igniters for flame initiation.  (b)   Two   ( 2 ) no   fuel oil   tank shall be provided for main plant area.   Each   tank shall be sized  considering 8 hours storage requirement for Boiler   and all diesel generators.  (c)   T he fuel oil   supply   system shall consist of   Three   ( 3 ) 100% capacity   fuel oil   (1 working for each  unit + 1 common standby) pressurising pumps, suction strainers, compressed air atomised  burners,   fuel oil   and air piping, fuel oil safety and burner shut off valves, fuel oil pressure and

(d)   Drain oil connection and pumping system shall be provided in steam generator area.  (e)   Minimum turn down ratio of 4:1 shall be provided. Adequate number of position indicating  contacts for integration into burner management system (BMS) shall be provided.  (f)   The air fuel ratios around the burners shall ensure low NOx emission. The   Bidder   shall furnish  details of the system proposed to be adopted by him for NOx emission control.  5.12   BLOW DOWN SYSTEM  (a)   The blow down system shall consist of continuous blow - down (CBD) tank and intermittent  blow down (IBD) tank, all piping, headers, valves, supports and hangers, insulation, level  control system and instrumentation etc.  (b)   CBD tank shall be connected to IBD tank with CBD tank level control system complete with  auto level drain control valve. The steam space of CBD shall be connected to the deaerator  with NRV & isolation valves.  (c)   Safety relief valve shall be provided on the CBD tank. Anti erosion compensating wearing plates  shall be provided at the inlet of each tank. The tanks shall be provided with manhole openings  of suitable size with bolted and hinged doors. The vent of IBD ta nk shall be led to a safe  location.  (d)   All drain lines in steam & water side leading to the respective drain headers/IBD shall be  provided with two valves and the second valve shall be motor operated.  (e)   The continuous blow down line shall be provided with motorised isolation valve and motorized  inching type blow down valve. The intermittent and emergency blow down lines shall also be  provided with motorised isolation valve and motorized inching type blow   down valve.  5.13   SOOT BLOWING SYSTEM (IF PROVIDED)  (a)   Fully automatic, sequentially controlled, microprocessor based steam soot blowing system  would be provided. The system would have short retractable rotary wall blowers for the  furnace and long retractable rotary blowers for the super heater, economizer and   air  preheater.  (b)   The soot blowing system consist of motor operated steam soot blowers of the rotary and  retractable type, with drives, pressure reducing, isolation and relief valves, steam traps,  thermal drain valves, complete piping, support and hangers, insulation, motor   control centres  and soot blower instrumentation and control system. The system is of self - draining type.

(b)   The system shall be complete with phosphate preparation/metering tank, 2 x 100% dosing  plunger pumps complete with suction /   discharge valves, Y - type strainers, instrumentation,  dosing pump discharge header sectionalising valves and complete piping with isolating and  non - return valves, safety eye washers etc.  (c)   Complete system for High Pressure chemical dosing shall be skid mounted only and proper  spaces for the O & M shall be provided in & around the skid.  5.15   LOW PRESSURE CHEMICAL DOSING SYSTEM  (a)   The LP dosing system (O2 scavenger and Ammonia) shall be supplied to maintain the feed  water chemistry.  (b)   Each system shall be complete with preparation/metering tank, 2 x 100% dosing plunger  pumps complete with suction / discharge valves, Y - type strainers, instrumentation, dosing  pump discharge header sectionalising valves and complete piping with isolating a nd non -  return valves, safety eye washers etc.  (c)   Complete system for Low Pressure chemical dosing shall be skid mounted only and proper  spaces for the O&M shall be provided in & around the skid.  5.16   AUXILIARY COOLING WATER SYSTEM  (a)   The auxiliary cooling water system shall be supplied at one point to   each   B oiler . Local drains at outlet  side shall be provided near all the equipmen t. An air vent shall be provided at the top most  point in the boiler side. Adequate number of Pressure and temperature indicators shall be  provided.
--- Page 98 ---
Client   –   Hindalco Industries Ltd  Project   –   2X30 MW CGPP   TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR MAIN PLANT SYSTEM  TCE.13 833 A - ME - 6002 - 6 001, Rev - R 0   Page   96   of   342  5.17   STEAM TURBINE GENERATOR & AUXILIARIES  5.17.1   The steam turbine shall be of a proven design for the specified duty conditions. The steam turbine  shall meet the design criteria specified in IEC - 45 standard (latest Edition).  5.17.2   The steam turbine and accessories shall operate satisfactorily in the entire design frequency range  specified in   Attachment 1: Technical   data sheet ,   without exceeding the safe vibration limits and  acceptable noise level under all operating conditions.  5.17.3   The steam turbine and auxiliaries would be designed to operate as a base load station (2x 3 0 MW)  as also it shall be capable to operate at cyclic load variations. The turbine design will cover adequate  provisions for quick start - up and loading of the units to full load at a faster rate. In case of sudden  reduction in demand (load   throw off), the unit should get safely unloaded and stabilized for  operation at house load. During disturbances in the   sub - station, the unit shall be capable of  operating in an islanded mode catering to its own house load (about 5 to 6%) .   These disturbances  shall not cause tripping of steam generator.  5.17.4   The steam Turbine shall also be designed to operate continuously with turbine under VWO  condition (i.e. ,   output of 105% TGMCR ) .  5.17.5   The steam turbine generator shall be capable of safely operating and continuously delivering rated  output, under following operating conditions:  a)   With a frequency variation of ±5% over the rated system frequency of 50 Hz.  b)   Within ±10% of voltage variation of the rated generator voltage at maximum and minimum of  rated frequency range.  5.17.6   Each   steam   turbine unit shall   include the equipment, base/sole plates,   foundation bolts,  embedment’s, instrumentation and control equipment, electrical hardware and accessories, heat  resistant and special shielded cables where required, all piping, fitting s, valves and specialties,  hangers and supports, thermal and acoustic insulations etc. as specified and as required to make  the system complete and in line with the technical requirements of this   specification .  5.17.7   Each steam turbine shall be complete with steam strainers, turbine emergency stop and control  valves, interconnecting piping between emergency stop valve (steam strainer integral to ESV) and  turbine casing, Blow   out kit for steam blowing, turbine governing and protection system, solenoid  valve for remote   tripping.  5.17.8   Each   turbine will have   two no   internally controlled automatic extraction facility and quick closing  non - return valve as well as isolation valve shall be provided in the extraction piping. Adequate  safety relief valves and vent valves shall be provided in the extraction and exhaust pipi ng. The safety  valve and vent valve outlet shall be led to a safe location.  5.18   TURNING GEAR  The turning gear shall be electric motor driven and with   auto - engage & auto disengage  arrangement. A hand operated mechanical barring gear shall also be provided in addition to motor  driven turning gear which can be used in the   event of an emergency.  5.19   STEAM TURBINE GLAND AND GLAND STEAM SEALING SYSTEM

Design shall permit examination and replacement if necessary, of the  glands without lifting the upper half of the turbine casings.  b)   The system shall automatically control the pressure of the gland sealing steam under all  operating conditions through a set of control valves. During normal operation, the source of  sealing steam to be from the turbine. It shall include necessary speed / load / pressure / frequency  controllers, load limiter, speed / load changer. In case mechanical hydraulic  backup is not provided, adequate redundancies in the   governing   system shall be included to  ensure reliable and safe operation.  (b)   Each Steam turbine shall be equipped with separately actuated overspeed device called quick  acting emergency governor to trip the unit at 110% of rated speed in case speed governor fails  to limit the turbine overspeed. Client   –   Hindalco Industries Ltd  Project   –   2X30 MW CGPP   TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR MAIN PLANT SYSTEM  TCE.13 833 A - ME - 6002 - 6 001, Rev - R 0   Page   100   of   342  5.25   SURFACE CONDENSER AND ACCESSORIES  5.25.1   The Condenser shall be designed for 5% margin above the heat load   at the condition of   10 MW  gross power output of turbine   with zero process steam requirement.   Also,   5% tube plugging margin  with cleanliness factor of   90%   to be considered for condenser design . The condenser shall be  designed for a temperature   rise of   5 °C   with a design cooling water temperature of 3 4.6   o C .   The  condenser shall be provided with a neck to connect the turbine exhaust opening. The   condenser  unit shall be capable of condensing all the steam from the associated turbine over the entire load  range with all drains being dumped into the condenser, with the design cooling water inlet  temperature specified. The condensate temperature shall   not be less than the saturation  temperature corresponding to condenser backpressure.  5.25.2   The condenser shall be such that the condenser pressure, corresponding to sudden load throw - off  without bypass system at maximum condenser cooling water inlet temperature does not exceed  the turbine trip value for the condenser pressure. Further, the selec ted condenser shall not cause  unit capability loss at the maximum condenser inlet cooling water temperature.  5.25.3   The location and design of steam and water dumps to the condenser shall be in accordance with  ASME Standard TDP - 1, Part - 1.  5.25.4   The velocity of water in the condenser tubes shall not exceed 2.0 m/s. Adequate arrangement and  space shall be provided at the rear of the condenser to facilitate the removal of condenser tubes.  Also, turbine foundation columns shall be suitably placed to   enable easy operation of rear water  box door.  5.25.5   Hinged quick opening manhole covers shall be provided for easy access to the interior of water  boxes for inspection or maintenance. At least two manholes shall be provided in each water  box/reverse chamber. Suitable lifting lugs shall be provided for the w ater boxes.  5.25.6   Each waterbox shall be provided with a drain valve of adequate size at the bottom to enable the  front and rear waterbox and tubes to be emptied of water in not more than 30 minutes. Also, each  water box shall be provided with adequately sized vent line wit h two valves. One valve, immediately  after the tapping and the second valve at an accessible area near vent termination for frequent  operation.  5.25.7   Access manholes of adequate size shall be provided, one in each of the hotwell sections. Hotwell  connections to suction of condensate extraction pumps shall have anti - vortex baffling and the  screen mesh shall be made of SS 304.  5.25.8   A suitable means of access shall be provided into the condenser to permit inspection and cleaning  of confined areas. For this purpose, an access manhole of 600 mm x 600 mm size minimum shall  be provided on the shell above tubes.  5.25.9   The condenser hotwell shall be sized for minimum of three (3) minutes storage retention at  maximum flow. The above storage shall be from normal level to the low - low level of the hotwell.
--- Page 103 ---
Client   –   Hindalco Industries Ltd  Project   –   2X30 MW CGPP   TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR MAIN PLANT SYSTEM  TCE.13 833 A - ME - 6002 - 6 001, Rev - R 0   Page   101   of   342  The low   -   low level shall be 150 mm above the bottom of hot well. Connections shall be provided  to empty the hotwell and the emptying time from the normal operating level (to the station drains)  shall be about one hour or less.  5.25.10   The tube material shall be   as per datasheet in Attachment - 1 , manufactured and tested in  accordance with the approved standard. All tubes shall be of tested quality and the Bidder shall  have to submit the test certificate of the tubes. Each tube shall be eddy current tested for  soundness. The tubes shall be roller   expanded. The extent of tube expansion shall be established  by mock - up test and all expansion shall be done to the measured extent .  5.25.11   The tube support plates shall be arranged suitably so that no periodic vibration of tubes may be  induced by the running of the Turbine and the maximum span shall be limited such that vibration  induced by steam flow may not occur. Edges of tube holes with s upport plates shall be chamfered  and deburred to prevent tube damage.  5.26   Condenser Air Removal System (Steam Jet air Ejectors)  a.   The air removal system functions under two regimes: One during normal operation and the other  when raising vacuum on the turbine during start up.  b.   The condenser air removal system shall primarily consist of one (1) start up (hogging) ejector and  two (2x100%) main running ejectors with inter and after condenser.  c.   The start - up ejector shall be designed to lowest possible parameters to achieve vacuum as quickly  as possible during start up.  d.   The outlet piping of startup ejector shall be provided with a silencer and the piping shall be  terminated outside the building at a safe elevation.  e.   The ejector unit shall be designed, constructed and tested in conformance with the latest edition  of HEI standards. Each element shall be designed to handle the capacity recommended in the latest  edition of HEI standard to suit steam surface condenser.  f.   All necessary pipes, fittings, valves, flanges and instruments shall be provided by the Bidder to  complete the system.  5.27   Condensate Extraction Pumps and Accessories  a.   3 x 5 0% condensate extraction pumps of vertical canister type shall be provided   for each unit   to  pump the condensate from the condenser hotwell   through the steam jet air ejector, gland steam  condenser, to   condensate make up water storage tank .  b.   In order to ensure an acceptable availability between planned maintenance periods, components  of pumps shall not be made of materials which are degradable and/or which will wear and need  replacing within 40,000 hours with the exception of mechanical shaft   seal materials, which shall be  capable of operating for not less than 15,000 hours.  c.   The pump characteristic curve tolerances shall be limited to:
--- Page 104 ---
Client   –   Hindalco Industries Ltd  Project   –   2X30 MW CGPP   TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR MAIN PLANT SYSTEM  TCE.13 833 A - ME - 6002 - 6 001, Rev - R 0   Page   102   of   342  Sl. No.   Description   Plus(%)   Minus(%)  1.   Flow at guarantee point   0   0  2.   Head at guarantee point   3   0  3.   Efficiency at guarantee point   Unlimited   0  4.   Shut - off head   5   0  These tolerances shall be the permissible   variations between the conditions as proposed initially by  the Bidder and the design parameters demonstrated during acceptance tests.  d.   When pumps operate in parallel, the load   sharing deviation between them be limited to 5% at  flow capacities from   the design flow down to the minimum flow.  e.   The pump motor rating shall not be less than larger of the following:  i.   Sufficient to drive the pump through the entire range of run - out flow to shut - off conditions.  ii.   110% of pump design point rating.  iii.   115% of maximum power requirement of the pump.  f.   The suction specific speed for the pump shall be selected so   as to   ensure that pump is completely  protected against low   flow recirculation and consequential cavitation and without exceeding  specified vibration level over the entire operating range. Pump with suction specific speed value  higher than 11000 US units, not proven in practice, may be rejected.  g.   The construction shall be such as to allow removal of pump shaft and impeller without disturbing  the canister.  h.   The suction side vent of each canister shall be individually connected to the condenser. The  discharge side vent of each condensate extraction pump shall have its own solenoid valve operated,  together with break down orifice and be connected individually t o the condenser.  i.   The initial sealing water for the condensate extraction pumps shall be from the hotwell make - up  system, and during normal operation from the condensate extraction pump discharge. The transfer  from one sealing water source to another shall be automatic.  j.   The minimum recirculation system of the condensate extraction pump shall also meet the minimum  flow requirements of gland steam condenser.  k.   Pump shaft coupling shall be of flexible type and the thrust bearing shall be provided in the pump.  Pumps shall be fitted throughout with renewable wearing parts such as wearing rings, shaft sleeves,  stage sealing rings, etc.  l.   The suction valves shall be suitably sealed to avoid ingress of air into the condensate.  m.   The impeller shall be cast in one piece finished all over and carefully balanced both statically and  dynamically. Individual impeller shall be balanced in single plane. Entire rotor shall be balanced in  two planes. The assembled rotor shall also be dynami cally balanced.

Suitable automatic starting devices shall be provided to bring the stand - by pump into operation in  case of failure of operating pump or when the condensate header pressure drops below a  predetermined value. The following protections shall be provided for   the Condensate Extraction  Pump (CEP) as a minimum  i.   Condenser Hot well level ‘Low - Low’  ii.   Discharge pressure before Non Return Valve on the CEP pump   discharge is less than pre - set  value  iii.   CEP drive motor protections actuated  The   above - listed   protections are a minimum and additional protections if required shall be provided  to make the system complete during detailed engineering phase.  5.28   Make up water storage tanks and pumps  One (1) no make up   water storage tank for each unit shall be provided to store the TG cycle  condensate, return condensate from refinery plant   and shall be used as buffer storage tanks. Return  condensate shall be supplied at plant boundary with the quality indicated in this specification.  Suitable arrangements to be made to drain the return condensate at nearest drain pit in the event  of bad qua lity return condensate is received. DM water shall be used as cycle make up with level  control station at each tank inlet.   Suitable h eating arrangements to be provided on the tanks for  preheating the condensate before   transferring to deaerator s . The pegging steam shall be taken  from auxiliary steam header.   2 x 10 0% Makeup water pumps shall be provided to transfer the  condensate to   dearators.  5.29   FEED CYCLE EQUIPMENT AND ACCESSORIES  Deaerating Feed Water Heater  a.   One (1) no spray   cum tray   type constant pressure dearator for each unit shall be considered.   The  constant pressure deaerator shall be capable of satisfactory operation under boiler MCR conditions,  normal operation, condition of turbine trip, no condensate return from process and steam bypass  operation (full open) and lowest condenser pressure. Min imum condenser pressure shall not affect  deaerator performance in any manner.   The pegging steam to the deaerator shall be taken form  auxiliary steam header.  b.   Deaerator and storage tank shall be designed and constructed ASME Section VIII Div I code (latest  edition) for unfired pressure vessels .   The tray supports shall be rigid enough to withstand severe  vibrations that would be caused due to water. All materials used inside the deaerator such as trays,  fasteners, support rods, nozzles shall be of   SS. The sprayer shall be spring controlled spray valves  (variable flow area) which ensures fine atomization of condensate at all loads. The sprayer device  shall provide a self - regulated system in which disc/piston opening adopts automatically to the  chang ing hydraulic loads.  c.   Heating steam shall be introduced into the water stored in the feed water tank through a rake of  sparger steam pipes below the minimum water level.   The rake shall be suitably supported to
--- Page 106 ---
Client   –   Hindalco Industries Ltd  Project   –   2X30 MW CGPP   TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR MAIN PLANT SYSTEM  TCE.13 833 A - ME - 6002 - 6 001, Rev - R 0   Page   104   of   342  prevent vibration. Suitable means shall be provided to prevent back flow of water into steam pipes  in case there is any sudden drop in steam pressure. A separate heating steam rake to supply heating  steam during start - up shall be provided. These heating st eam pipes shall be suitably supported to  avoid   excessive   vibration   during   start - up,   normal   operation   and   transients.   Steam   pipe  configuration shall be such as to prevent entrainment of any heating steam into boiler feed pump  suction lines.  d.   Deaerator safety valve shall be provided on deaerator. The safety valve should of adequate relieving  capacity. The disc and spindles of the safety valve shall be made of 13% Cr. stainless steel.  e.   Manholes of minimum 500mm on deaerator and storage tank for access to and removal of internals  including trays shall be provided.  f.   The outlet pipes from the feed water storage tank shall be provided with   a vortex breaker and  project a minimum of 75 mm above the bottom of the storage tank so as to prevent sludge from  getting into the suction pipes of the boiler feed   pumps. The feed water storage tank shall be  provided with two manholes of minimum 500 mm size. In case of spray - tray type deaerator an  additional manhole with hinge shall be provided at a suitable location in the deaerator section.  g.   Low load recirculation flow and balancing device leak off flow systems from the boiler feed pumps  shall be led to the feed water storage tank.  h.   The vent from the continuous blow down tank (CBDT) shall also be led to the deaerator.  i.   The deaerator level shall be controlled by controlling the condensate inlet flow into deaerator  during normal conditions. The feed water storage tank shall have a minimum of 10 minutes storage  capacity of 100% BMCR between normal operating level and low - l ow level. The overflow and drain  from deaerator shall be led to the condenser through flash tank.  j.   The sprayer shall be designed to ensure fine atomization of condensate at all loads upto 110% of  the design flow.  k.   The maximum oxygen content of the feed water discharge from the Deaerator without chemical  dosing shall not exceed 7ppb at all loads and including the rated capacity. The carbon dioxide  content of the feed water discharge shall be nil.  l.   The deaerator shall be capable of deaerating all the incoming steam and condensate, HP heater  drain and DM make up water even when there is no condensate return from process at a rate to  provide boiler feed to match the boiler MCR requirements continuously .   It shall be designed to  avoid any stagnation pockets of non - condensable gases.  m.   The operation of deaerator shall be stable and free from vibration, water hammer when subjected  to pressure decay which may occur at full load trip out condition of the TG and during the interval  required for change over to the alternative steam source.  n.   Relief valves to be designed to relieve the largest capacity resulting from one of the following  conditions:

Extraction steam entering deaerator  b.   Condensate entering Deaerator  c.   Pegging steam quantity.  d.   Other flows entering deaerator.  o. The above listed are a minimum and additional provisions if required during detailed engineering  phase shall be provided to   make the system complete.  5.30   Boiler Feed Pumps  a.   The boiler feed pump group shall consist of 2 x 100% duty boiler feed pump sets   for each unit .  There shall be common suction header and common discharge header for all the BFPs of both Units.  The suction for the BFP pump shall be taken through strainers for the individual pump from  deaerating feed water heater. A boiler feed pump (BFP) set shall comprise of a main pump,
--- Page 108 ---
Client   –   Hindalco Industries Ltd  Project   –   2X30 MW CGPP   TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR MAIN PLANT SYSTEM  TCE.13 833 A - ME - 6002 - 6 001, Rev - R 0   Page   106   of   342  lubricating system comprising oil pumps, working and lube oil coolers, duplex filter, oil tank,  connected pipes, valves, etc. for the entire BFP system.  b.   The boiler feed pumps shall be of horizontal, multistage, constant speed centrifugal pump. A design  with both suction and discharge nozzles on top, pointing upwards shall be preferred.  c.   Location of deaerating feed water heater and offset of suction piping from pumps shall be such that  the margins on net positive suction head (NPSH) required by pump during normal and transient  conditions, would be as per Data Sheet and flashing/steaming i n suction lines is avoided under all  operating modes.  d.   If required, a pump warm - up system shall be included in case Bidder’s pump required the  same. In case Bidder does not recommend provision of same, the same shall be substantiated by  proven experience.   However rapid start - up of pump set shall be possible   from any operational  condition i.e. cold or partially warmed - up.  e.   The Bidder shall furnish calculations to justify the acceptability of the location of the deaerating  feed water heater with respect to margins over the required NPSH by the pump during normal and  transient conditions.   The transient condition would be duri ng a turbine trip with the boiler trip  condition or any other condition. The calculations shall have to be approved by Owner / Owner’s  representative.  f.   During normal operation the discharge valve of standby BFP set would be kept normally open. Each  BFP set as a whole shall be capable of withstanding the reverse rotational speeds which would  result from non - return valve failure under the most severe condit ion (i.e., remaining normally  working pump operating at full speed) and provided with a suitable instrumentation to detect  reverse rotation of pumps due to backflow of water after motor is tripped. It is not recommended  for the pump to be rotated in revers ed direction for longer duration (discharge   valve to be closed  immediately). However, the offered pump shall be suitable for reverse rotation and same shall not  be allowed to start or run during reverse rotation. Bidder shall provide reverse rotation detection  system to close discharge valve on det ection of same.  Pump characteristic curve test tolerances shall be limited to:  Sl. No.   Description   Plus(%)   Minus(%)  1.   Flow at guarantee point   0   0  2.   Head at guarantee point   3   0  3.   Efficiency   at   guarantee  point   Unlimited   0  4.   Shut - off head   5   0  These tolerances shall be the permissible variations between the conditions as proposed initially by  the Bidder and the   design parameters demonstrated during acceptance tests.  g.   The suction specific speed for the pump shall be selected so as to ensure that the pump is  completely protected against low flow recirculation and consequential cavitation.   The suction
--- Page 109 ---
Client   –   Hindalco Industries Ltd  Project   –   2X30 MW CGPP   TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR MAIN PLANT SYSTEM  TCE.13 833 A - ME - 6002 - 6 001, Rev - R 0   Page   107   of   342  specific speed shall not exceed the value specified in data sheet. The specified vibration level shall  not be exceeded over the entire operating range. The vibration level of the offered pump shall be  as per ISO 10816 - 7 zone A. The Bidder shall indicate a   reference list of installations where he has  supplied similar capacity pumps with the suction specific speed value equal or higher than that  offered by him. The impeller shall be designed for a minimum 40,000 hours operation.  h.   In the event the normal sound level exceeds the allowable level specified, acoustical treatment  features, subject to the Owner’s review and acceptance, shall be in the basic scope to achieve the  specified sound level.  i.   Dry running of the BFP is not recommended however, in case of power failure the pump and motor  shall be suitable till the coasting down of the pump to stand still condition.   Also, suitable dry run  protection features to be implement ed for BFP operation.  j.   However, the pump shall preferably be capable of running under   vapor   locked condition for a  sufficient duration without any damage during transient reduction in suction pressure well below  that required for cavitation free operation.  k.   The pump motor rating shall not be less than the larger of the following:  i.   Sufficient to drive the pump through the entire range of run - out flow to shut - off conditions.  ii.   110% of pump design point rating.  iii.   115% of maximum power requirement of the pump.  l.   Pump suction and discharge nozzles shall be provided with flange connections to standard ANSI B  16.5. Pump casing vent and drain connections with plugs and valves shall be provided, as required.  m.   All bearings shall be provided with a forced lubrication from a common oil system. The thrust  bearings shall be of tilting pad type and suitable arrangement shall be provided to adjust the  clearances. Also, the design of lubricating oil system should take   into account the oil requirements  of the motor wherever thrust bearings are fitted.   Bearings shall be easily accessible without  disturbing the pump. Each bearing housing shall have a drain plug. The bearing housing shall be  cooled with passivated DM wate r .  n.   A suitable axial thrust balancing arrangement preferably balancing drum & segmental pad type  thrust bearing shall be provided. The balance drum leak - off shall be lead back to pump suction. No  axial thrust shall be transferred to the electric motor at any c ondition of operation.  o.   Adequate space shall be provided between pump drain connections and base plate for installation  of minimum 15 mm drain piping. Pumps shall be supplied with suitable drainpipes or drain rim type  base plates with tapped drain connections.  p.   All BFP sets shall have individual lubrication system. The lubrication system shall include, but not  necessarily be limited to, a shaft driven oil pump, a spare full capacity electric motor driven oil  pump, all piping valves, return flow sight gauges, reli ef valves, oil coolers, pressure and temperature  switches, duplex or two simplex filters, pressure gauges, flow control devices, oil level indicators,

All necessary protection  required to protect the BFP, even if not included above shall be part of scope of supply.  5.31   DUST PRECIPITATION AND COLLECTION SYSTEM  a.   Each boiler   shall have two (2) flue gas streams and one electrostatic precipitator (ESP) shall be  provided for each stream of steam generator. The electrostatic precipitator shall be designed to  limit dust concentration to less than 30 mg/Nm3 with (N - 1) [where N = to tal number of fields) fields  in service for the complete load range of steam generator under all fuel firing conditions and as per  the analysis specified in   this specification . The ESP casing design pressure shall be compatible w ith  combustor design pressure.   ESP shall be complete with supporting structural frames, collecting  and discharge electrodes, shaft insulators, bush insulators, independent rapping system for  collecting and discharge electrodes with automatic rapping contr ols, high voltage transformers,  rectifiers, bus sections with sectionalising and grounding switches and controls, fly ash collecting  hoppers with accessories etc.   Bidder to implement   High Frequency Transformer Rectifier (HFTR)  and micropulse   technology in ESPs for better efficiency.   Bidder to elaborate in the Bid   other   energy  efficiency measures adopted for ESP.  b.   Access doors and platforms and corresponding interior walkways shall be provided on each side of  the casing before and after each of the fields in series at the bottom and midpoint elevations  of the collecting electrodes in order to facilitat e maintenance   and inspection of each of the  fields. Interior walkways shall be a minimum of 1 2 00 mm wide.  c.   The staircases shall be located in between both the streams of ESP, towards the Steam Generator  side. Staircase shall extend from zero meter floor.  d.   For personnel protection, key interlocks shall be provided to prevent the opening of any inspection  and/or access   doors   until   after de - energising   of   all   high   voltage   equipment   that   can   be  reached and/or made contact with, through the inspecti on and/or access door.  e.   Each hopper shall be provided with a conveniently located 150 mm (6 inch) pipe nipple fitted  with a quick removable hinged cap located near hopper outlets to facilitate emergency poking.  ESP casing shall rest on specially designed bearings whi ch will rest on supporting structures to allow  thermal expansion and other load variations.  f.   Penthouse/weather enclosures shall be ventilated by electric motor driven fans. The temperature  of transformer rectifier sets, controls etc., must be maintained within acceptable limits under all  operating conditions with maximum ambient temperature. The f ans   shall   be   wall mounted,  exhaust from inside the penthouse to the outside and be protected with rain louvers.  g.   The hoist to permit easy removal of each transformer - rectifier from its operating position to grade  level shall be provided.  h.   The rapping system shall be designed for selective remote   automatic or manual operation at  adjustable intervals. Necessary rapping arrangement with controls shall be provided for perforated

Heating elements   with auto cut provision  and vibrating pads shall be provided for ESP hoppers on all four sides for smooth flow of ash through  the hopper. High Frequency Transformer Rectifier (HFTR)   and micro - pulse transformer shall be provided by  Bidder.   Primary taps shall be included on the transformer. Transformer   shall have electrostatic  shielding between primary and secondary windings and shall have suitable shielding of the high  voltage coil to accomplish proper voltage division in the transf ormer when unit   is subjected  to   surge   conditions   encountered   in electrostatic fly ash precipitator application.  b.   Each output leg of each transformer - rectifier set shall serve one bus section and both bus sections  served by each transformer - rectifier set shall be in the same field.  c.   Transformer rectifier assemblies shall be mounted in a heavy gauge welded steel tank designed for  a minimum of 0.5 kg/cm2 test pressure and full vacuum  d.   All silicon rectifier cells shall be conservatively rated for compatibility with maximum circuit load  and demands requirements.  e.   In the event of arcing, the filter voltage shall be switched off for several half cycles to ensure  a satisfactory extinction of the arc. If dust bridging or metal short circuits occur in the precipitator,  the precipitator short circuit   current shall be limited to the rated current of the installation and  disconnected completely after the expiration of a given time.  f.   Supports and shafts shall be provided with heating elements with auto cut provision.   Supporting  insulators shall be located in   heated compartments that are maintained above the dew point of  the flue gas. They shall be located out of the dirty gas stream. These shall be protected from  weather by either a common top housing or individual c ompartments. A steel shroud below the  insulator may be provided which will further inhibit dust diffusion. Each compartment shall be  pressurised and ventilated by heated f iltered air furnished by one motor driven blower.  5.33   CONTROL CUBICLES  Automatic control cubicles shall be furnished for indoor mounting. The automatic controls shall have  full control from 10% of the power set rating to full rating.  The   control system should be capable of:  i.   Evaluating sparking by intensity and wave shape.  ii.   Regulate power at the threshold of sparkling.  iii.   Break a power arc within around ten -   (10) milli sec of its occurrence.
--- Page 113 ---
Client   –   Hindalco Industries Ltd  Project   –   2X30 MW CGPP   TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR MAIN PLANT SYSTEM  TCE.13 833 A - ME - 6002 - 6 001, Rev - R 0   Page   111   of   342  iv.   Automatically control the arc response so that power is restored within around one hundred  twenty (120) milli secs. And  v.   Modify the current wave shape permitting greater power input prior to reaching spark over  threshold.  Adequate numbers of indicating instruments and alarms shall be provided on the control cubicles.  Rapper control system for each bus section or pairs of bus sections served by a single transformer -  rectifier shall :  a)   Provide manual adjustments of the timing and frequency of operation and the rapping  intensity from the control cabinets,  b)   Provide rapper diagnostic system including miniature indicating lights, digital indicators,  etc.  5.34   ESP   Motors  Drive motors of the rapping mechanism for   collecting and discharge electrodes and fans shall  comprise 415V, 3 phase, 50 Hz, rated direct - on - line induction motors with IP - 6 5 enclosure.  In general, motors shall conform to Specification as specified elsewhere in this specification.  The storm water drain piping shall be provided up to the grade levels for ESP.  6.34.1   DAMPERS  a.   All necessary regulating and isolating dampers shall be provided for the efficient and convenient  operation of the steam generator. The   Bidder   shall furnish a list of all dampers, their purpose and  torque rating etc. together   with the details of actuators and   accessories for remote control.  Powered dampers shall also have provisions for manual operation during emergency / maintenance  along   with graduated   local   position indicator.  b.   The dampers shall be capable of effectively stopping the flow in the closed position and while in  full open position shall cause minimum pressure drop. Special attention shall be given to the  design of sealing arrangement of isolating d ampers to ensure that they provide a positive shut  off when closed. Open and Close positions shall be clearly marked on each damper.  c.   The dampers in flue - gas paths shall be so located that the build - up of grit behind   the damper  blades is reduced to a minimum.  d.   The attachment of all louvers to the damper spindles shall be by means of key fitting. All bearings  for spindles or damper operating gear shall unless approved to the contrary, be arranged for  efficient grease lubrication. Grease lines of coppe r/steel shall be run from all greasing points on  each damper to a convenient and   easily   accessible location adjacent   to   the respective damper  and terminated with suitable clamps and grease nipples on a steel frame to facilitate easy  lubrication.  e.   All dampers shall be arranged to facilitate   local manual operation from a gallery or floor level. The  operating gear shall be fitted with a graduated indicator and shall be designed such that the  damper may be retained in any position. The isolating dampers shall,   in addition be fi tted  with locking devices to permit locking in the fully open and shut positions.

Horizontal shafts shall be  supplied wherever possible.  j. Bidder to furnish  the required lifting arrangement for the following:  •   Turbine generator building shall be provided with electric overhead travelling (EOT)  cranes. Crane shall be provided with main and auxiliary hook.  •   Hoists with monorails and chain pulley blocks shall be provided for all buildings as  required for the entire plant.
--- Page 115 ---
Client   –   Hindalco Industries Ltd  Project   –   2X30 MW CGPP   TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR MAIN PLANT SYSTEM  TCE.13 833 A - ME - 6002 - 6 001, Rev - R 0   Page   113   of   342  c)   Crane /Hoists shall be rated for the heaviest component to be handled with the auxiliary hoist  rated for 20% of main hook capacity. Subsequently, the required capacity of EOTs shall be in  multiples of 5 metric tons.  6.35.2   CRANE DESIGN CONSIDERATION:  a)   The turbine generator building EOT crane shall be rated for the heaviest component to be handled.  The generator stator shall be erected using jacking method or using mobile cranes. The main crane  is used for maintenance of steam turbines and electric gener ators, generator coolers, lube oil units  and the boiler feed water pumps. The auxiliary hoist is used to handle smaller components more  rapidly.  b)   The height of lift for the Turbine Building EOT crane shall be based on lifting the tallest piece from  the operating floor to grade, with consideration of sufficient clearances for lifting over other  equipment on operating floor.  c)   The EOT cranes shall of the plate box girder construction. The crane structure shall be designed in  accordance with IS 807. Box girders shall be designed as not to permit accumulation of water or oil  inside them.  d)   The turbine building EOT crane shall be able to be operated both from cabin located on the crane  and pendant push button station hanging from the crane. Air conditioning with one no. 4.5 kg CO2  type fire extinguisher shall be provided in Cabin.  e)   10% of main speed shall be the creep speed and shall be provided for all motions.  f)   Access walkway with safety handrails shall be provided along the full span length of the Turbine  Building crane.  g)   The turbine generator building EOT crane main hook shall be of Ramshorn type. The auxiliary hoist  hook shall be of the shank type.  h)   The EOT cranes shall be designed to operate under overload test with 125% of rated load without  any permanent deformation. The EOT cranes are designed with a maximum deflection of 1/900 of  crane span at rated load.  i)   Incoming cable will be terminated at the isolating junction. The isolating junction box shall be  located 1000 mm above the ground on one   of the building columns below the runway conductors.  j)   The scope of supply shall include the minimum of the following:  i.   Crane steel structure frame complete with end carriage with travelling machineries.  ii.   Runway rails with associated fixtures comprising of fixing clamps, bolts   nuts, etc.,  iii.   Access ladders, stairs, walkways, platform and handrails.  iv.   Hoist mounted on a trolley including brakes, hooks, load blocks, wire ropes, electric overload  devices with flexible bumpers, slings and all necessary accessories including end stoppers,  track sweeps, buffers, etc.

Client   –   Hindalco Industries Ltd  Project   –   2X30 MW CGPP   TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR MAIN PLANT SYSTEM  TCE.13 833 A - ME - 6002 - 6 001, Rev - R 0   Page   114   of   342  v.   High tensile wire ropes and rope guides  vi.   Limit switches for all motions.  vii.   Lube oil or grease for initial charge/filling  viii.   All electrical accessories to make the equipment complete in all respects.  a)   Bridge Girders : The crane structure shall be designed in accordance with IS 807. The  b)   main girders of the bridge shall be either box type construction or solid  c)   web construction. The bridge girder shall have enough strength to carry  d)   the rated load without causing undue stress or deflection.  e)   4.2.2 Box girders shall be designed as not to permit accumulation of water or  f)   oil inside them. Hand holes or manholes shall be provided for ease of  g)   inspection and cleaning.  6.35.3   HOIST DESIGN CONSIDERATION:  a)   For the equipment   weighing above 1000kg or with a lift >10mtr, electrically operated type of hoists  would be provided. For the equipment weighing < 1000 kg or with a lift <10mtr, manually operated  hoists and trolleys would be provided.  b)   Design and construction throughout shall be based on ample safety factors to guard against  injurious strains and distortions. All hoist parts shall be designed with a minimum safety factor of  5. All parts shall be readily accessible for inspection, adjustm ent, and repair.  c)   Non - spinning wire rope shall be used on all hoists where the load is suspended by a single rope.  d)   Hooks shall be of forged steel supported on a ball or roller bearing. They shall rotate freely within  this bearing.  e)   All gears shall be forged, rolled or welded steel with cut teeth. All gearing shall be enclosed in dust  tight housing. All gearing shall operate in grease lubricant or oil as appropriate.  f)   Bearings shall be grease lubricated. Bearings shall be sealed to prevent leakage and all bearings  shall be protected against entry of dust. Bearings shall be grease tight. The equipment should be  arranged so that all points of lubrication are easily and sa fely accessible.  g)   The scope of supply shall include the minimum of the following:  i.   Hoist mounted on a trolley including brakes, single/plain forged steel hook supported on a ball  or roller bearing with catches/latches, load blocks, wire ropes, electric overload devices with  condition monitoring system, buffers and all necessary accessori es including end stoppers and  buffers.  ii.   Limit switches for all motions.  iii.   Complete motorized trolley.
--- Page 117 ---
Client   –   Hindalco Industries Ltd  Project   –   2X30 MW CGPP   TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR MAIN PLANT SYSTEM  TCE.13 833 A - ME - 6002 - 6 001, Rev - R 0   Page   115   of   342  iv.   Monorail with all accessories including clamps, hanger rods, bolts/nuts, washers, end  stoppers, track sweeps, safety grips, pads, etc.  v.   High tensile wire ropes and rope guides  vi.   One (1) No. isolating switch shall be provided for each electric hoist with rising cable enclosed  in conduit.  vii.   Lube oil or grease for initial charge/filling.  viii.   All electrical accessories to make the equipment complete in all respects.  6.35.4   Chain Pulley block design consideration:  a)   The   chain pulley block shall be a complete unit with travelling trolley, load chain, load chain wheel,  hand chain, hand chain wheel, necessary gearing, brakes for hoisting, hook, and other accessories  to make the equipment complete in all respects. The chain p ulley block shall be of worm or spur  gear type and shall have minimum factor of safety as five (5).  b)   The hand chains for the hoisting and traverse mechanism shall hang well clear of the hook and both  the chains shall be on the same side. The length of the hand chain shall be such that the lowest  point of the suspended loop shall hang 400 mm above the oper ating floor level. The hand chain  wheels shall be provided with roller type guarding to prevent snagging and fouling of the chain.  c)   Gears shall be cut from solid cast or forged steel blanks or shall be of stress - relieved welded steel  construction or built - up from steel billets and welded together to form a one - piece gear section.  Gears shall have proper lubrication arrangement and shal l preferably be of totally enclosed type.  d)   Shafts and axles shall be of forged steel and shall have ample strength, rigidity and adequate  bearing surface for intended duties. Shafts and axles shall be accurately machined and properly  supported.  e)   Brakes shall be of automatic type that shall stop and hold the load when hand - chain pull is released  and shall permit controlled lowering of the load when hand - chain pull is applied for lowering the  load.  f)   Ratchet and Pawl mechanism shall be provided to arrest the full load from lowering due to gravity.  The ratchet and pawl shall be of steel, hardened and tempered so as to attain required wear  resistance and toughness.  g)   The lifting hook shall be single hook type, forged, heat treated, rugged construction and provided  with a standard depress type safety latch. Lifting hook shall have swivels and operate on ball or  roller thrust bearings with hardened races.  5.36   AIR CONDITIONING AND VENTILATION SYSTEM  6.36.1   GENERAL
