They shall be provided with anti - vibration   pads (15 mm  thickness), internal illumination lamps operated by door switches, louvers, ventilation fans  with filters, thermostat - controlled space heaters, utility power sockets, drawing pocket,  lifting eye bolts, safety and signal earth bus, SS name and   tag plates, removable front and  rear doors and door locks. Cable entry from shall be from the bottom.  All DCS / PLC   cabinets shall be mounted in air - conditioned rooms. Ingress protection for  indoor mounting shall be IP - 42. Local panels mounted outdoor shall be IP - 65 and provided  with canopy.  Two coats of rust resistant primer and two coats of final painting shall be done to give a  durable matte finish. System cabinets shall house the racks, signal – conditioning modules, I/O  cards, processor cards and associated power supply units. Field cables   may be terminated in  distinct marshalling cabinets or at the rear of the system / I/O cabinet with I/O modules  mounted at the front. Pre - fabricated cables shall be provided from marshalling / terminal  blocks to the I/O racks.

Client   –   Hindalco Industries Ltd  Project   -   Aditya Alumina Refinery   TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR MAIN PLANT SYSTEM  TCE.13833A - ME - 6002 - 6001, Rev - R 0   Page   222   of   342  Network cabinet with glass door shall be provided to mount the various Ethernet switches  and GPS receiver, signal processor and power supply. The terminal blocks shall   be grouped based on  the type of signals and voltage levels. Interposing relays of different contact interrogation  voltages shall be separated by a barrier.  v)   Control Desk - Indoor located, unit control desk suitable for top mounting of HMIs shall be  provided. The panel shall be constructed with 2 mm thickness sheet steel, IP42 degree of  protection, 2 mm thick of cold rolled steel & doors, fluorescent lighting, b lower, louvers with  brass mesh (space heater with thermostatic control). The desk shall have lockable enclosures  for mounting the CPUs of the HMI and for storing O&M documentation etc. These shall be  provided below the desk level.  w)   EARTHING SYSTEM  All equipment containing an electrical signal or power supply shall be earthed for personnel  safety reasons and for minimizing electrical interference. This includes enclosures, panels,  cable armour, cable glands, cable duct/tray, conduit, etc. Design of t he earthing system shall  avoid the creation of earth loops caused by duplication of earthing paths. Particular attention  shall be paid to the isolation of cabinet earth bars within cabinets and their final earthing to  a point of zero potential.  Cable screens shall be electrically continuous throughout the cable run and shall be earthed  at one point only. This point is the earth bar in the cabinet in which the cable is connected.  Signal Earth{Field instrumentation (Circuit ground, shields, drain w ires etc.), LCP , Junction  Boxes} shall be connected to dedicated earth pit.  All panels shall be equipped with an earth bus securely fixed along the inside base of the  panels. Provision shall be made for future extension of the earth bus, if need arises. Panel  Earth for Panels,racks, consoles,shelters etc. shall be connected to Pla nt Electrical Earth pit .  Control system console/ panel earthing shall be as per DCS vendor recommendation.  x)   Control Valves - Multistage, anti – cavitations, Balanced, modulating, globe type, cage guided,  single ported, diaphragm type of actuator with hand wheel, SMART Pneumatic positioner, air  filter regulator, air lock device, solenoid valve as applicable, limit sw itches and position  transmitters completely tubed with junction box. Pneumatic positioner shall be suitable for  accepting 4 – 20mA DC signal. Pneumatic (SS316) tubing complete with accessories, fittings. Positioner has to be 2 – wire, 4 – 20  mA loop powered by the control system and capable of split ranging operation.  The SMART positioner shall be suitable for both single acting and double acting actuators.  The SMART positioner shall be fully modular in construction with Encapsulated printed wiring  board and pressure gauges inside the positioner cover to protect from tr ansit/site damage.  SMART positioner shall be a Double stage positoner. The first stage of the positioner shall be  typically a flapper – nozzle that serves as a high – gain pre – amplifier. This sensitivity shall be  maintained over a wide range of dynamic conditions. Second stage s hall be a power amplifier  that provides power to drive the actuator. Preferably this shall be a pneumatic relay. Spool  Driven type SMART positioners are not preferred due to Higher Dead Band and Poor  responsiveness. The SMART positioner shall have pressure   sensors to measure the pneumatic  outputs to the actuator  The control algorithm for the positioner shall use feedback signal from the motion of the  pneumatic relay beam instead of pressure feedback to minimize pneumatic related effects  and for stable and smooth response of the control valve. The SMART positioner   shall have  user adjustable tuning sets to identify the optimum tuning for the total valve assembly.  SMART Positioner with HART Communication facility shall communicate all the valve  diagnostics to DCS.The electrical housing shall be designed to meet IP65.

Wherever there is possibility of huge spillage of  caustic, MS painted cable trey shall be used.  7.7.6.3   Pre - fabricated cable trays shall be hot dip galvanized. The fasteners shall be electroplated. Cutting  of trays at site shall be minimized. As far as possible pre - fabricated bends shall be used.  7.7.6.4   Ladder trays shall be made from angles and flats.  7.7.6.5   The width of the cable trays shall in general be 150mm, 300 mm, 600 mm and 750 mm.  7.7.6.6   Perforated G.I. trays shall be used from junction boxes to individual instruments. They shall be  hot dip galvanized, made of at least 2.5 mm thick sheet. However if  required for optimization Branch trays within unit may have field bus cables combined with  instrument cables provided distance criteria is satisfied.  7.7.6.9   Two (Main and redundant) separate route shall be provided for PLC/DCS Data Highway  communication cable to ensure one mishap will not disrupt DCS communication . These cables  can run in communication trays (telephone). However if communication tray is not a vailable, than  cable can be laid in Fieldbus/Fire & Gas cable tray provided distance criteria is met (As per site  condition, it shall be planned accordingly).

T h e s e   a l a r m s   s h a ll   b e   t a k e n   i n   s e r i e s   w i th   i n d i c a t i on  on   D CS   g r a p h i c  7.6.8   UNINTERRUPTIBLE POWER SUPPLY (UPS) SYSTEM
--- Page 233 ---
Client   –   Hindalco Industries Ltd  Project   -   Aditya Alumina Refinery   TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR MAIN PLANT SYSTEM  TCE.13833A - ME - 6002 - 6001, Rev - R 0   Page   231   of   342  220V, 2x100% rated hot redundant UPS system operating in synchronised mode and with static  bypass to   regulated   supply shall be provided for complete plant for feeding the loads requiring  UPS supply such as Turbine and Boiler field instruments, DCS and other control systems. The DC  back - up duration is considered as 60 minutes. UPS shall be with super - maintenance by pass  facility. The system will comprise of 2x100% rated hot redundant UPS system (two sets of  converters, inverters & 2 X 100 % battery banks ) ope rating in synchronized mode and with static  bypass to regulated supply and AC Distribution Board. The system will have a selector switch. In  the `Normal' position of the selector switch, the UPS loads shall be supplied by two UPS, sharing  50% load each, w ith output of both UPS being automatically synchronized with each other. If one  UPS fails, the healthy UPS will supply the full 100% load by the operation of the static switch. In  the event of failure of both the UPS, the load shall be fed by the standby   regulated supply through  static switches.   When any one of the UPS is under mainte nance, the selector switch shall be  suitably changed such that the healthy UPS supplies 100% load. When only one UPS is feeding  the load, the output shall be synchronized w ith the frequency of the standby source.  The UPS system and associated ACDB (two incomer one bus coupler) shall be located in the  Central   Control   Building   for   respective   Switchgear   Buildings   for   locations   based   on   the  requirement.  7.6.9   TIME SYNCHRONISATION EQUIPMENT  Time synchronisation equipment shall be complete with all hardware and software to receive the  synchronizing signals from remote satellite and provide outputs to synchronize the plant wide all  programmable control systems to ensure a common time reference  The system shall be designed in a 100% redundant configuration and will include two master clock  systems  The master Clock shall be configured as Real Time Clock with display of time in 24 hrs format and  date and shall drive slave clocks located in different plant control buildings.  The offered master clock system shall be a fully wired panel consisting of the following minimum  items:  a   GPS Antenna  b   GPS Receiver  c   Signal Processing Unit  d   Local and Remote display units (slave clocks)   –   3 nos. (at least)  e   Power supply  f   All required cables, mounting accessories, cabinet for the processing electronics, etc
--- Page 234 ---
Client   –   Hindalco Industries Ltd  Project   -   Aditya Alumina Refinery   TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR MAIN PLANT SYSTEM  TCE.13833A - ME - 6002 - 6001, Rev - R 0   Page   232   of   342  The fully wired panel shall accommodate signal receiver, signal processing unit, power supply unit.  Technical features of the master clock system shall be as   follows:  a   GPS Antenna Type   : Helical  b   Signal Receiver   : Microprocessor based  c   Tracking method   : Code / Carrier tracking  d   Output data format   : NEMA 0183  e   Communication speed   : > 9600 BPS  f   Output rate   : every second  g   Signal Processing Unit   : Microprocessor based Digital type  h   Reference   : OCXO with stability of 1 PPM.  i   Time Accuracy   : 1 1 msec or better.  Time synchronizing signals shall be able to communicate through multiple protocols like IRIG - B,  NTP.  All the Slave clocks shall derive impulse from the Master clock. Each Slave clock shall be connected  to Master Clock by 2 pair copper wire with annealed, tinned, stranded Copper conductor, PVC  insulated, PVC sheathed, GI round wire armoured, overall FRLS P VC sheathed. It shall be ensured  that loss of any slave display unit does not affect the display of any other slave unit. Slave clocks  shall be weatherproof construction suitable for indoor or outdoor use.  Slave Clocks shall be digital type with updates at every second. The display shall be of six digits for  hours, minutes and seconds. Display element shall be red LED and the digit size shall be 100 mm  minimum for a viewing distance of 20 metre. Anti glare g lass cover shall be provided. Slave clocks  in control rooms shall be wall/panel mounting decorative type.  7.6.10   HART MANAGEMENT SYSTEM (HMS)  A dedicated standalone PC based HART Management system (HMS) shall be provided for  centralized configuration, maintenance, diagnostic and record keeping of all electronic smart  transmitters and smart valve positioners.HMS shall be complete with its server   & user PC, and  shall be Integral with DCS.  7.6.11   SPARES  Bidder to consider below spares as minimum while submitting the recommended spare list on  unit price basis

Sample Conditioning System  Sample conditioning system design shall meet with the requirements as specified in ASME PTC  19.11 - 1997. It shall receive and condition all samples as required by the respective analyzers  connected to the sample streams. This will include conditioning comp onents as specified herein  and shall cover the following:  GENERAL

Vendor to clea rly specify the maximum sample  temperature that can be handled by each analyser offered.  7.8.10. Steam and Water Analysis System (SWAS) Panels  a.   The SWAS panels shall have two sections namely sample conditioning section and analyser  section. The first section shall be the wet section of SWAS panels. The second section shall  be the dry section housing the analytical transmitters, signal conditioning   devices and other  requisite accessories and multi - stream analysers like silica, sodium, etc. All monitoring  instruments and controls for sampling system shall be located on the front & shall be  grouped accordingly to function in a logical and orderly fash ion. There shall be a general  progression of flow from top to   -   bottom and left - to - right to minimize sample line length,  purge times and material exposed to sample.  b.   Similarly, the conditioning components for each stream shall be grouped together  wherever possible.

These systems shall be capable of performing the required functions and shall be  complete in all respects. The system shall be capable of providing   on line reliability which meets  or exceeds all Codes and Standards that apply. The Facility uninterruptible power supply (UPS)  may be used to provide necessary power to achieve the required reliability factor  7.9.4   At a minimum continuous monitoring of below parameters shall be provided:  a)   NOX  b)   SOX  c)   PM  d)   CO  e)   CO2
--- Page 239 ---

Each analyzer shall be specific  for the measured gas without interferences by background or trace gas of other components. Only suitable alloys or Teflon components shall contact wet, dirty sample  gas. A moisture sensor downstream of the drier shall shutoff the samp le system to protect the  analyzers in the event of a moisture break through. Multi - stream switching shall be provided to  switch up to multiple streams through the sample system. Streams that are not sampled shall flow  continuously through a bypass to reduc e transport lag time when selected. Probes and sample  line not being sampled shall be back flushed periodically with high - pressure air  7.9.7   System shall have the capability for online automatic cleaning (using compressed air or otherwise)  of the sample extraction system and opacity monitoring system without dismantling of any of the  components  7.9.8   A means for calibrating all analyzer cells shall be provided by periodically introducing zero and  span gas or gas cells, manually and automatically. The equipment shall be mounted on a rack, completely   piped and wired with all field  sample tubing and electrical wiring connections terminating at bulkhead tubing fittings and  electrical junction boxes.  7.9.11   A rack for supporting span, zero and calibrating gas cylinders shall be furnished, located outside  the Analyzer room. Each cylinder shall be provided with a pressure   regulator and pressure gauges.  7.9.12   The rack shall provide storage space for clear separation and identification of in use, empty and  full gas cylinders.

ANNEXURE - II  1.0   This section covers the minimum requirements for the design and installation of a  Foundation Fieldbus system based on the 31.25 kbps physical layer per ISA S50.02/AG -  181/IEC61158. Specification shall be used in conjunction with the SPECIFI CATION OF  DISTRIBUTED CONTROL SYSTEM DCS. The Foundation Fieldbus portion covers:  Hardware associated with the FF H1 segment.  Operability issues associated with FF design.  1.1   A P P LI CA TI O N  For details refer clause 7.5.2.1  1.2   HARDWARE  A typical Fieldbus segment consists of the following components   -  a   Host system with controller, operator station, internal buses etc. & I/O  module to connect field bus segment i.e Fieldbus interface card   Redundant  b   Bulk Power Supply   -   Bulk VDC supply to Foundation Fieldbus Power Supplies  Redundant
--- Page 251 ---
Client   –   Hindalco Industries Ltd  Project   -   Aditya Alumina Refinery   TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR MAIN PLANT SYSTEM  TCE.13833A - ME - 6002 - 6001, Rev - R 0   Page   249   of   342  c   Foundation fieldbus power supply cum Conditioner   -   To feed required  power to each segment Redundant  d   Diagnostic Module   –   For each FF   Power Hub/Motherboard common for  group of segment  e   Surge suppressor at Power Conditioner & inlet of FF JB.  f   Main Fieldbus segment cable to field.  g   Juntion Box/ Device coupler.  h   T — Terminators.  i   Spur cable -   connecting main trunk cable to field instruments.  j   Fieldbus devices e.g., transmitters, transducers, positioner etc  1.3   HO ST   S Y S T EM   &   F I E L D   I N T E RF ACE   CA R D  a   As general practice, Control system in Fieldbus network is termed as  HOST. For description refer “SPECIFICATION OF DISTRIBUTED CONTROL  SYSTEM DCS  b   Field   interface for DCS will be through Redundant Fieldbus I/O module H1  Card.  1.4   BU L K   P O W E R   SUP P L Y   ( R E DUND A N T  a   Bulk Power supply BPS feeds 24 VDC to Foundation Fieldbus Power supplies  .  b   BPS shall be powered from UPS distribution panel by two separate,  independent UPS circuits.  c   over current protection shall be provided for each feed supplying power to  an individual or gang of FFPS.  d   It shall be possible to replace a failed power supply online .  1.5   FOUND A TI O N   F I E L DBU S   PO W ER S UPP L Y   ( FFPS )   W I T H   P O W ER   CONDI TI ON E R  a   Redundant Fieldbus power supply with power conditioner shall be provided  per   segment.  b   FFPS shall be fed by redundant 24 VDC Bulk power supply unit by two  independent fused circuit.  c   FFPS backplane shall have facility for connecting diagnostic module.  Diagnostic module shall be for each FF Power Hub/Motherboard. Vendor  shall provide suitable software/Hardware for segment diagnostics.   In  case diagnostic modules are interface d to system through dedicated   H1  interfaces   then   such   interfaces   shall   be   provided   in   addition   to  specified I/O requirement.
--- Page 252 ---
Client   –   Hindalco Industries Ltd  Project   -   Aditya Alumina Refinery   TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR MAIN PLANT SYSTEM  TCE.13833A - ME - 6002 - 6001, Rev - R 0   Page   250   of   342  d   Fault in any of redundant module shall be annunciated in Host System.  Common alarm for all FFPS in a cabinet may be used.  e   FFPS shall provide galvanic isolation for H1 segment.  1.6   TERMINATOR ,   J UN C TI O N   B O X,   D E V I CE   C OU P L ER   &   S U R G E   S UPP R ESS O R S  a   Each Fieldbus segment must   have exactly two terminators. The wiring  between the two terminators is defined as the trunk. All terminators  located in the field shall be installed in a junction box. No terminators  shall be installed in the FF devices. Repeaters shall   not be used.  b   A Junction Box/Device Coupler is a physical interface between a Trunk and  Spur, or a Trunk and a device. The number/type of device coupler in a JB is  decided by number of instrument connected to a junction box.  c   Surge protection device shall be provided in Trunk out from Power  Conditioner & incoming of JB.  1.7   N O   O F   D E V I C E S   P ER   S E G M EN T  1.7.1   No   o f   d e v i c e s   p er   s e g m en t are as below -  •   Type A :   Critical : One control loop & remaining monitoring loop (Max 6 Nos of  device per segment)  •   Type B: Non critical: Two control loops & remaining monitoring loop (Max 8  Nos of device per segment)  •   Type C: Monitoring loop: Max 8 Analog Input per segment  1.7.2   PID control : In DCS Controller  1.7.3   LAS capable : DCS FF H1 Interface card /Field Devices PRIMARY & SECONDARY LAS SHALL  RESIDE IN H1 CARD  1.7.4   Each Fieldbus segment shall support multiple backup Link Active schedule .  1.7.5   Segment Diagnostic Module : One per FF Power Hub/Motherboard Per group of  segment  1.7.6   Instrument Cable : ISA 50.02 “A” Type cable  1.7.7   Wiring Polarity :   + Signal = White  -   Signal = Black
--- Page 253 ---
Client   –   Hindalco Industries Ltd  Project   -   Aditya Alumina Refinery   TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR MAIN PLANT SYSTEM  TCE.13833A - ME - 6002 - 6001, Rev - R 0   Page   251   of   342  Drain/Shield = Bare tinned   copper conductor  1.7.8   Wiring polarity shall   be maintained throughout the segment design and installation.  1.7.9   Square root extraction : Field device  1.8   S E G M EN T   D E S I G N   P H ILO S OPH Y  T h e   nu m b e r   o f   p o ss i b l e   d e v i c e s   c o nn e c t e d   t o   o n e   F i e l db u s   s e g m e n t   s h a l l   b e   li m i t e d   b y  t h e   li m i t a t i o n s   o f   F F   a n d   t h e   l a y o u t   o f   t h e   p l a n t .  1.9   S E G M E N T   D E S I G N   P O I N T S :  1.9.1   All control i.e. PID, Split range, Cascade, interlock shall reside in Host controller.  1.9.2   Loop execution time: As per DCS specifications  1.9.3   Segment topology -   Fieldbus installation shall use tree - , individual spur - , combination  topology, but  daisy chain topology i.e. routing the trunk directly through each device shall not to be used.  1.9.4   The following criteria shall also to be considered during segment design:  a   Type of loop i.e process critical, time critical, Non Critical loop.  b   Area classification & concept applied for Hazardous area.  c   Cable length of segment cables trunks" and "spurs".  d   "Bus software load", limitations of signal transfer rates on the bus and  number of usable function  e   Voltage drop   - Fieldbus devices may be powered either from the segment  bus, or locally powered, depending on the device design.  f   Circuit analysis shall   be   carried   out for each   Fieldbus   segment   to  determine the operating voltage at each device. The calculated voltage at  the device shall exceed the devices minimum voltage   rating   by 4 volts,  6e.g. Minimum Vdc requi red by device = 9 volts, therefore the calculated  minimum voltage seen at the device shall be 13 Vdc. The   calculated  voltage   at   each   device   shall   be   shown   on   the   Instrument Segment  Diagram Power consumption of the individua l devices  g   The total current draw from all devices on the segment must not exceed the  rating of FFPS Foundation Fieldbus Power Supply. The segment design must  take into account.
--- Page 254 ---
Client   –   Hindalco Industries Ltd  Project   -   Aditya Alumina Refinery   TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR MAIN PLANT SYSTEM  TCE.13833A - ME - 6002 - 6001, Rev - R 0   Page   252   of   342  •   Total device quiescent current draw + 10mA extra for data  transmission  •   One spur short circuit fault i.e. ~50 mA additional current draw.  •   25%   additional   current   load   over   and   above   that  calculated  1.9.5   All final control elements shall have configured fail safe on loss of segment  communication.  1.9.6   For Fieldbus Foundation, 4 pair cable shall be used between control system & Field.  Out of 4 pair 3 pair shall be used & balance one pair shall be considered as spare. Hence  there can be 3 Junction Box associated to this multipair. On similar lines,the fou rth port  of every FF card shall be spare  1.10   H O S T   RE Q U I RE M E N T S  1.10.1   Ho st   F F   I n t e r op e r a b i l it y  a   All FF Host systems shall have completed host interoperability support  testing at foundation Fieldbus facilities .FF host systems shall support all  features specified in the HOST.  b   A letter of conformance to the Host interoperability system test shall be  provided to verify test completion and feature support.  c   The Host system supplier shall have an interoperability test setup to  ensure   that all third party devices   used in   the project have been  successfully tested and certified with their system  d   As far as possible, each type & make of instrument will be forwarded for Host  interoperability test during FAT.  e   For Instruments not available during FAT, control system supplier shall  establish communication   at   site.   It   will   be   control   system   suppliers  responsibility   for   establishing   communication   with   “FF”   certified  instruments.  1.11   HO ST C ON F I GU R A TI O N  a   FF   Host   must   have   configuration   tool   capable   of   on   line   &   offline  configuration. The configuration   tool shall have multi - user & multi - instance  apability  b   Internal mirror or shadow function blocks used by control systems to map FF  function blocks to internal proprietary function blocks must be completely  transparent   to   the Configurator   and   operator.   Single,   unique   and
--- Page 255 ---
Client   –   Hindalco Industries Ltd  Project   -   Aditya Alumina Refinery   TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR MAIN PLANT SYSTEM  TCE.13833A - ME - 6002 - 6001, Rev - R 0   Page   253   of   342  independent function block and parameter tag names must be used for  both   configuration   and   operation.   Duplicate   shadow   blocks   /  parameters   with   tag names different from FF block / parameters are not  acceptable.  c   Vendor shall indicate host configuration tool capability in offer.  d   Host shall have auto addressing facility i.e. downloading addresses to device  from DCS.  1.12   HO ST   F F   F EA T U R E   I N T E G R A T I O N  All host FF functions, including engineering, configuration, maintenance, and operational  display functions, shall be integrated into a single, seamless host system.Engineering,  configuration, maintenance   and   operational   features   shall   apply   consistently   and  seamlessly to conventional analog/discrete,Smart HART IO,bus based IO,FF etc.Separate  software   tools,   displays   or   procedures   -   specific   for   FF   and   different   from  conventional are not acceptable.  1.13   HO S T - T O - D E V I CE   R E V I S I O N   D O W N L O AD   C A P A B I L IT Y  Hosts shall have the capability to download software revisions to foundation Field bus  devices.  1.14   Ho st   F F   O p e r a t i n g   T oo l s - FF   f e a t u r e s   r e q u i r e d   f o r   i n t e g r a t i o n   i n c l u d e :  a   FF function block operation, including use of data quality, status, windup  and bad value indication and mode switching, must be supported by,  and transparently integrated   into   the   control   system   operation   and  operating displays .Differences in operation or displays between FF devices  or loops and conventional loops are not acceptable.  b   FF process alarms and device alerts must be supported by, and integrated  into the control   system.   Differences between   conventional and FF alarm  management and alarm displays are not acceptable.  c   FF trend data via trend objects shall be supported by the host control  system   and transparently integrated into real - time and historic trends  displays.  1.15   .   Ho st   C o m m i ss i o n i n g   a n d   m a i n t e n a n ce   F un c ti on s
--- Page 256 ---
Client   –   Hindalco Industries Ltd  Project   -   Aditya Alumina Refinery   TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR MAIN PLANT SYSTEM  TCE.13833A - ME - 6002 - 6001, Rev - R 0   Page   254   of   342  a   The Host shall be capable of commissioning, setup, and maintaining all FF  devices. The function must be integrated into the Host and available from  Host workstations. The following functions shall be supported.  •   Add a new FF device to a segment. Add a future FF device to a  segment through use of placeholder templates.  •   Move   FF devices   from / between   offline,   spare,   standby,  commissioned,   and mismatch   states   and   manage   all   address  changes   transparently.   Manual address changes shall not be  required.  •   Simple and complex commissioning functions including transmitter  range changes, zeroing, and control valve positioner setup.  •   Support for DD methods and menu‘s wizards for all maintenance  functions to walk technicians through the necessary maintenance  procedures.  •   Provide specific maintenance displays, organized in a logical manner,  for all FF devices using English language descriptors and definitions  with   access   to all parameters.   Screens shall not use lists of FF  function block parameters.  b   Vendor shall provide simulation software for segment checking  c   It is the responsibility of system vendor to communicate and calibrate any  make of FF transmitters registered with Foundation of FF and maintain these  devices from their PRM.  1.16   A sset   m a n a g e m e n t   s y s t e m s  Asset management   systems,   separate   from the host operator / engineering  workstations, may   be   used   to   manage   and   display   real - time   and   historical  diagnostic   &   maintenance   information.   However,   it   may   not   replace  commission ing   and   maintenance   function   integrated into the host as described  above.  1.17   Spare   P h il o s oph y  Spare   philosophy shall   be   as per guidelines   given   DCS specification document  however it shall cover following points in addition. The spare capacity of FIM shall take  in to for VCRs, FF function block i.e.  •   Min 40% spare capacity for no of VCR’s per segment calculation to be  based on VCR’s per device.  •   Min 40% spare FF function blocks per controller.

T h e F i e l d b u s   d e v i c e   s h a ll   b e  c o nn e c t e d   t o   th e   t e r m i n a l   b l o c k   d e s i g n a t e d   f o r   t h e   f i e l d   w i r i n g .  1.18.3   The following tests shall be conducted  a   A complete functional test shall be conducted for one - of - each  FF device used on this project i.e. Both third - party and control  system vendor products.This test will include, but is not limited  to, plug - and - play   interconnectivity   to host   system; configured  fail - safe operation, verify access to all device function blocks, and  actual device   operation, 6e.g. stroke valves / MOV‘s, simulate  process inputs for transmitters, etc.7  b   The test shall include configuration, calibration checks and  setup for each type of FF device. Examples are changing RTD  sensor types, configuring and calibration check of transmitter  span,   zeroing   P & DP   transmitters, zer oing   elevation   on   DP  level transmitters,   setup   & calibration of new positioner on  a control valve, MOV setup, AOV setup etc. The intent of  this   requirement   is   to   verify   the   ease   of   access   to  calib ration wizards and setup procedures via the host system.  c   All calibration and setup procedures, for each device, shall be  demonstrated to Client/. Each procedure shall be documented  in detail by the vendor and approved by Client.

The test  shall verify that automatic fail over shall not cause an upset, i.e.  I/O signal bumps, loss of operator view,   mode changes etc..  All FF interface cards and FFPS shall be tested. Client shall  approve the fail - over test procedures. Client   –   Hindalco Industries Ltd  Project   -   Aditya Alumina Refinery   TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR MAIN PLANT SYSTEM  TCE.13833A - ME - 6002 - 6001, Rev - R 0   Page   268   of   342  8.0   CIVIL, STRUCTURAL & ARCHITECTURAL WORKS  8.1   BTG ISLAND  •   The intent of the specification is for Civil, Architectural & Structural steel erection works for  BTG island area   excluding   Chimney. The works are to be executed within the boundaries of  proposed 2x 30 MW   CGPP .  •   The technical specification covers the technical requirements for the supply / Construction /  installation of complete Civil / Structural   works as applicable. The works shall have to be carried  out below and above ground level .   Broad scope of work is specified herein in subsequent  clauses.  •   The work to be performed under this specification consists of planning, design, engineering  including all incidental works not   shown or specified but reasonably implied or necessary for  the engineering of the present scope of work all in strict accordance with the specifications,  including revisions and amendments thereto as may be required.  •   The   scope of work   under this part of specification consists of design, preparation of detailed  drawings and documents for all structural steel building / structures, preparation of GA  drawings & layout drawings under the scope of contract, all in strict accordance with the  specifications, including revisions and amendments thereto as may be required during the  execution of the work   in the BTG island area excluding chimney . Preparation of fabrication  drawings shall be in the scope of the CONTRACTOR.  •   Supply, Fabrication & Erection of   t he technological structures like EOT Crane for TG, other  crane & hoists for BTG auxiliaries ,   Boiler, ESP, Duct and Duct supports etc. shall be in the  Bidder’s scope.  •   Contractor shall ensure the correctness of details in the fabrication drawings and submit to  Owner   to proceed with fabrication/ erection.   All inserts and anchor bolts required as per  Contractor’s design drawings shall be supplied by the   OWNER . Contractor shall also check and  supervise the   correctness of placement of inserts/anchor bolts during construction before  concreting.  •   D esign   &   preparation of   Good   for   Construction   (GFC)   Drawings for Civil RCC   works   in the BTG  island area   will be   done by   OWNER/ OW NER’s engineer . Contractor to provide the necessary  loading data   ( including   Foundation loading data for each equipment)   for all the   structures ,  Steel tanks, pumps,   equipment   etc.   The necessary   baseplates, shim plates   etc.   required ,   shall  be supplied by   the   Contractor. Grouting works shall be   done   by   Contractor under the  supervision of   the   OW NER . However, if any special grouts required for specific reasons /  functional requirement of the equipment / tanks the same shall be   provided by Contractor .
--- Page 271 ---
Client   –   Hindalco Industries Ltd  Project   -   Aditya Alumina Refinery   TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR MAIN PLANT SYSTEM  TCE.13833A - ME - 6002 - 6001, Rev - R 0   Page   269   of   342  •   The scope of supply, fabrication and erection of steel structure   (excluding technological  structures)   above ground (other than RCC works) shall be in   OWNER ’ s   scope . The design of  such steel structures shall be as per   relevant IS codes for the worst combination of forces such  as bending moment, axial force, shear force and torsion. Basic Wind speed and seismic zone  of the project site shall be as per project information attached with this specification. Wind  and seismic   forces shall not be considered to act simultaneously. Criticality of erection /  maintenance loads shall also be checked separately in combination with other simultaneously  occurring loads for possible design loading.  •   For all building and   structures, necessary layout and details are to be developed by the  Contractor keeping in view the statutory & functional requirements of the plant and facilities  and providing enough space and access for operation, use and maintenance. Certain minimum  req uirements are indicated in this specification for guidance purpose only.  •   The bidder shall inspect the site, examine, and obtain all information required and satisfy  himself regarding   the site conditions necessary for detail engineering . Ignorance of the site  conditions shall not be accepted by the   Owner   as basis for any claim for compensation or  extension of time.  •   The submission of a bid by the Bidder will be construed as evidence that such an examination  was made and any later claims / disputes regarding   to the   price quoted shall not be entertained  or considered by the   Owner   on account of ignorance of prevailing site conditions.  •   SURVEY DATA   -   It is the responsibility of the   Bidder   to verify the various features on his own  before submission of bid.  8.1.1   STATUTORY REQUIREMENT  The contractor shall comply with the latest applicable statutory rules.   Contractor   shall obtain  approval of Civil / Structural/ Architectural drawings from concerned authorities before starting  construction   activities .  8.1.2   DOCUMENTS TO BE SUBMITTED BY   BI DDER   WITH PERTAINING TO WORKS  (a)   Layout drawing   locating all buildings, structures, facilities roads, temporary site office, etc. with  their plan dimensions   based on the latest Plot Plan.  (b)   A report on foundation proposed for various structures, buildings and facilities based on the  geotechnical investigation   data furnished by   Owner . Allowable safe bearing capacity for open  foundation, depth of foundation, need for pile foundations, type, length and capacity of piles,  if piles are proposed, soil improvement if any required, special precaution against aggressive  soil etc., shall also   be covered in the report.

The scope also  includes all other buildings, structures and works necessary which are not specifically mentioned  here but required for   engineering under   present scope of work   are deemed to be included in the  scope of the Bidder. The works shall conform to high standards of design and engineering .   Design  and   engineering   shall conform in every respect to all international standards / local regulations  governing such works .  8.1.4   BRIEF DESCRIPTION OF   BUILDING AND STRUCTURES  STEAM TURBINE GENERATOR BUILDING  i.   Turbine building framing shall be of structural steel with moment connection framing in the  transverse direction and bracing in the longitudinal direction.  ii.   Service and maintenance bays will not have any intermediate floors; however, a walkway with  handrails shall be given along the wall at the operating floor level to observe the TG erection  operation. Floors shall be provided as per Mechanical requirements.   All floors in the TG  building shall have RCC floors over steel framing.  iii.   Crane capacity and crane rail level shall be fixed based on the equipment to be lifted and the  method of lifting generator stator. At crane girder top flange level, a crane walkway shall be  provided in line with Factory rules. Access shall be provided to c rane walkway through staircase  from operating floor in addition to cage ladder at two ends. The bottom level of roof framing  will be decided by the crane clearance required duly taking into account clearance required for  mounting light fixtures. Roofing sh all consist of in - situ RCC slab of minimum 50 mm thickness  from top of trough of metal deck laid to a slope of 1 in 100, constructed over permanently
--- Page 274 ---
Client   –   Hindalco Industries Ltd  Project   -   Aditya Alumina Refinery   TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR MAIN PLANT SYSTEM  TCE.13833A - ME - 6002 - 6001, Rev - R 0   Page   272   of   342  colour coated galvanised M.S trough metal sheet of approved profile supported on steel  purlins and trusses.  iv.   Cladding shall be of brick wall supported on steel wall beams, up to a height of 3000 mm above  ground level. Walls in front of transformers shall be of adequate thickness and height to satisfy  “fire rating” as per NFPA/ TAC regulations. Cladding beyond the   above level shall be with  permanent colour coated, sandwiched, insulated metal sheets.  v.   Windows shall be in anodised aluminium framework using 6 mm thick wired glass. In areas  where windows are fixed to metal cladding, windows shall be fixed type and in accessible areas  sliding windows shall be provided. Doors on external walls shall be doubl e plate flush steel  doors. For equipment entry into the service bay specially designed steel sliding, cum folding  rolling shutters shall be provided with appropriate operating mechanism. Provision shall be  made for the easy removal of the condenser tubes b y providing a rolling shutter/removable  cladding as required.  vi.   Staircases protected on all sides with fireproof enclosure shall be provided to satisfy NFPA  regulations or local authority guidelines. All the doors leading to the inside of the powerhouse  from staircase shall be automatically closing fireproof doors sati sfying NFPA regulations.  Access to the staircase shall be given to T.G. building roof. A Passenger lift shall also be  provided in the Steam turbine building.  vii.   Roof shall be provided with treatment using high solid content liquid applied urethane based  elastomeric membrane DFT 1500 microns, as per ASTMC836 and C898. Cement concrete tiles  / clay tile over cement mortar bed shall be provided over waterproofed area   to a width of 1000  mm where movement of personnel is anticipated. UPVC pipes min 100 Dia NB conforming to  standards shall be provided to drain off the rainwater from roof. They shall be suitably hidden  from the facade.  viii.   The minimum thickness of structural RCC floor shall be 150 mm above the steel floor beams  flange for the intermediate floors with 50 mm floor finish which will be applied later for  conventional shuttering system or 175mm above   s teel floor beams flange if troughed type  permanent shuttering is used.  ix.   All the intermediate floors shall be provided with proper drainage. A catch pit with C.I. grills  shall be provided near the internal column in a regular pattern and the floor shall be sloped  towards the catch pit by varying the thickness of the bedding con crete for the floor finish.  Catch pits shall be at the same coordinate for all floor levels. UPVC pipe shall be provided to  carry the drainage down. The pipes shall be clamped on to the intermediate column. Sumps  shall be provided on the ground floor to re ceive the water. Proper drainage arrangement shall  be provided on the ground floor by means of RCC gravity channels with M.S. grating on top.
--- Page 275 ---
Client   –   Hindalco Industries Ltd  Project   -   Aditya Alumina Refinery   TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR MAIN PLANT SYSTEM  TCE.13833A - ME - 6002 - 6001, Rev - R 0   Page   273   of   342  The drainage shall be led to separate sumps from where the water shall be pumped to plant  drainage arrangement. Around equipment liable to frequent drainage, concrete kerbs shall be  provided to isolate the area and water shall be led through sump and chann els to the floor  drainage system. Oil water separator shall be provided as required to isolate oil from the  drainage water where the water is mixed with oil.  x.   In the operating floor, the design live load shall be painted on the floors prominently showing  the extent of the area where such loading is permitted.  xi.   Wherever openings are provided in the floor for handling of equipment using EOT cranes such  openings shall be covered with gratings provided over removable steel beams.  xii.   Expansion joint (if required) shall be provided in the building to satisfy the requirements of  codes.  xiii.   Foundations for major equipment like generator and boiler feed pump shall be mounted on  properly designed vibration isolation system if so, specified in the Mechanical section of this  specification. However, the foundation shall be isolated from the buildi ng framework.  xiv.   All structural steel work shall be given paint as per painting specification attached elsewhere.  ELECTRICAL SWITCH GEAR CUM CONTROL ROOM  i.   The control building shall be an RCC/steel framed structure, aesthetically designed with in -  situ RCC slabs and elevator for approaching all the floors. Services housed on the various  floors shall be as per the enclosed GA drawings. Most of the areas except   for the area on the  ground floor shall be air conditioned and shall be provided with a false ceiling. The false  ceiling in the conference room, control room and computer room shall be of colour coated  aluminium ceiling system and in other areas false ceil ing may be of gypsum board.  ii.   Cladding shall be of brick wall with plastering and painting  iii.   Main entrance to control room shall be through aluminium glazed double leaf swing door of  adequate size and one single panel door shall also be provided. Separate rolling shutter entry  shall be planned for panel movement with a hatch and lifting beam. Main   door to switch gear  room shall be steel sliding door having adequate size to admit switchgear. There shall be a  minimum of two fireproof doors to the switch gear room. No windows shall be provided for  switchgear rooms and control rooms.
--- Page 276 ---
Client   –   Hindalco Industries Ltd  Project   -   Aditya Alumina Refinery   TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR MAIN PLANT SYSTEM  TCE.13833A - ME - 6002 - 6001, Rev - R 0   Page   274   of   342  iv.   An auxiliary transformer yard with fencing and gate shall be provided adjoining the building.  Building cladding adjoining transformer yard shall be made sufficiently thick to satisfy fire  rating as per CEA/TAC/NFPA regulations.  v.   Staircase area shall be protected from fire as per NBC/ TAC/NFPA regulations.  vi.   Partition between control room and adjoining rooms shall be of glazed aluminium with 300  mm high brick wall at bottom for toe protection. Doors shall be single leaf glazed aluminium  doors for all cabins. For movement of panels suitably sized double leaf al uminium glazed  doors shall be provided. In other area cladding shall be of brick wall. Battery room shall be  provided with PVC doors in PVC framing.  vii.   Toilets shall be provided at the switchgear room and control room levels. Toilet floors shall  be sunk, and floor provided with damp proofing treatment and shaft shall be provided to  route the soil pipe. All openings in the floor for switchgear and other pa nels shall be sealed  with fireproof material after cables are connected.  viii.   False flooring shall be provided in the control room, electronic cubicle room and the  computer room. The floor slab in these areas shall be sunk.  STEAM GENERATOR / ESP / DUCT SUPPORTING STRUCTURE  i.   Criteria for designing the steel structure is described elsewhere in this volume  ii.   Types of foundation for the structures shall be based on the subsoil condition and  recommendation based on detailed geotechnical investigation. In addition to foundations for  structure other major foundations in the area those for PA/SA/ID fan foundations.   The above  foundation shall be designed with Vibration isolation system .  BOILER AND BUNKER BAY FOR COAL, LIMESTONE & BED MATERIALS  i.   The framing shall be of structural steel. This shall be designed as a moment connection  framing in the transverse   direction and braced in the longitudinal direction. This structure  primarily supports bunkers ,   feeders and tripper arrangement .  ii.   This shall be a steel framed structure with bracing in the longitudinal direction as well as at  gable ends. Roofing shall be permanently colour coated galvanised trough shaped sheeting  1.0 mm thick and side cladding shall be of similar sheeting but 0.6 mm   thick. Bottom 3000  mm high cladding shall be of brick/ masonry . The rainwater gutter shall match the sheeting  provided and RW down take pipe shall be UPVC pipe conforming to standards.  iii.   The extent of the feeder floor shall be decided by the Bidder based on maintenance  requirements. The feeder floor will be of RCC supported on structural steel framework.
--- Page 277 ---
Client   –   Hindalco Industries Ltd  Project   -   Aditya Alumina Refinery   TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR MAIN PLANT SYSTEM  TCE.13833A - ME - 6002 - 6001, Rev - R 0   Page   275   of   342  Handrails shall be provided on all the sides. A steel staircase shall be provided for access to  the feeder floor from the ground floor. Access shall be provided to the feeder floor from the  boiler elevator.  iv.   Framework for supporting the bunker shall be provided above the feeder floor based on  geometry of the hopper.  v.   The bunker feeding conveying floor shall be immediately above the bunker. This floor shall  be of RCC supported on structural steel framework.  vi.   The Bunker roof shall be of RCC supported on permanently colour coated metal sheeting over  structural steel framework. Alternatively, tripper roof can be of permanent colour coated  metal deck sheet over structural steel framework. The tripper roof supports   bunker  ventilation / dust supporting structure. An RCC parapet or steel handrail shall be provided for  the tripper roof. An access stair shall be provided from tripper floor to roof in each unit.  vii.   In addition to the above, framing arrangement for structural steel platform at various levels  around the bunker shall be provided with proper access ladder, for poking, striking and  attending to air cannons.  viii.   Bunker feeding floor shall be given access through Boiler staircase / elevators.  ix.   Bunker supporting structure is normally left uncladded up to the bottom of tripper floor. For  tripper floor up to 3000 mm above finished tripper floor level, brick wall shall be provided.  Above this level permanent colour coated galvanised trough sheet cla dding shall be provided.  Fixed steel windows with 6 mm thick wired glass shall be provided in the tripper house as per  requirements for conveyor gallery.  x.   Roof slab shall be sloped to one side to permit easy drainage. Roof shall be provided with  treatment using high solid content liquid applied urethane based elastomeric membrane DFT  1500 microns, as per ASTMC836 and C898. Cement concrete tiles / clay tile o ver cement  mortar bed shall be provided over   waterproofed   area to a width of 1000 mm where  movement of personnel is anticipated. Rainwater down take pipes of UPVC conforming  Standards shall be provided.  xi.   All structural steel work shall be given paint as per painting specification attached elsewhere.  STEAM GENERATOR ELEVATOR SUPPORTING STRUCTURE  i.   Capacity and number of the elevators shall be as stipulated elsewhere. The elevator  supporting structure shall be of   structural steel braced in both directions. Plan bracings shall
--- Page 278 ---
Client   –   Hindalco Industries Ltd  Project   -   Aditya Alumina Refinery   TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR MAIN PLANT SYSTEM  TCE.13833A - ME - 6002 - 6001, Rev - R 0   Page   276   of   342  be provided at all the landing locations. Where landing locations are far apart, plan bracings  shall be provided at least at every 8 m center.  ii.   The elevator shaft shall be constructed with colour coated insulation sandwiched metal  cladding system supported on structural steel work. Lift pit shall be of RCC. Ground floor  entry shall be kept about 500 mm above general graded / paved level with a ram p for  approach. A sump for drainage and Aluminium ladder shall be provided in the lift pit.  iii.   The machine room floor shall be of RCC with 300 mm kerb wall all round. Roof also shall be  of RCC construction with permanently colour coated galvanised MS trough metal sheet  decking. Roof shall be sloped to one side for drainage purpose.  iv.   Side cladding for the machine room shall be of sandwiched metal cladding as specified for lift  cage. Adequate window shall be provided for admitting natural light. Adequate / designed  monorail with equipment hatch in the machine room floor shall be provide d to lift heavy  machinery. Steel staircase shall be given to the M/C room from last landing. Landing levels  are generally governed by the floor levels in the Boiler structure. In addition, landing shall be  provided at feeder floor and tripper floor levels.   Structural steel walkway shall be provided  from the elevator landing platform to these floors.  v.   Windows in the M/C room shall be of steel glazed type using 6 mm thick wired glass. Door  shall be of flush welded hollow steel type. All structural steel work shall be painted as per  specification described elsewhere.  STEAM GENERATOR AREA PAVING  i.   The entire steam generator area from the Electrical bay up to the end of chimney shall be  provided with RCC paving over rubble soling.  ii.   Paving shall be carefully planned such that maintenance access roads minimum 4 m wide to  all major equipment are available. This area shall be designed for heavy loads similar to  design of roads. Such approach road shall be provided to the S.G. elevator al so to enable  handling of equipment at higher level during maintenance. Such roadways shall be clearly  identified with kerbs / painting. All drain / trench covers in these areas shall be designed to  withstand truck loading.  iii.   The drainage of the entire S T G area shall be properly planned. A network of RCC drains with  RCC cover with slotted holes or MS grills shall be provided covering the entire area which  leads the storm water to the peripheral drains, which in turn will lead to plant drainage  system. Leak ed process water and water with ash/coal contamination shall be suitably  segregated and led to pits from where it shall be pumped to ETP. Where open drains are not

All structural steel  columns base plate are below paving level, it shall be provided with encasi ng to a height of  minimum 200 mm above paving level. The maximum paved level in S.G. area shall be kept a  minimum 100 mm below the finished ground floor level in Turbine building area.  8.1.5   GENERAL REQUIREMENT OF BUILDING / STRUCTURES  a)   The design of buildings, structures etc. shall take into account requirements for operation and  maintenance of all equipment and its   users. The buildings shall have good architectural  features. The surrounding areas shall be properly micro levelled and graded.  b)   ARCHITECTURAL CONCEPT FOR BUILDINGS  The architectural design concept of buildings shall be evolved considering the functional,  technical and other requirements for efficient operation, ensuring comfortable working  environment for personnel, satisfying the aesthetic requirements. Special care   shall be taken  to provide elegance and aesthetics, with effective use of appropriate treatment, materials,  fittings and finishes.   To achieve the above objective   Bidder   shall employ a qualified architect  to carry out all designs and hold all other architec tural responsibilities for the project.  The   Bidder   shall obtain and be conversant with all laws, by - laws and regulations of local and  Statutory Bodies as applicable to the project. The architectural concept evolved shall also take  care of these requirements. The   Bidder   shall provide the drawings and documents for such  statutory approvals.  c)   ROOF ACCESS  All roofs shall be provided with access through a staircase/ cage ladder. A minimum 1000 mm  wide access path shall be provided with tiles to approach equipment on the roof.  d)   PLATFORMS AND WALKWAYS  Platforms, stairways, and ladders shall be   provided to permit personnel access to   equipment  and components. Components that require access to perform operations, surveillance,  maintenance, or adjustment shall generally be accessible from a permanent floor or platform.  Other components with less frequent access shall be generally accesse d with the use of mobile  ladders, scaffolding, or other temporary means. Platforms and/or walkways shall connect pits  and low - lying areas with the main functional levels of the Plant.  Platforms and walkways shall be a minimum of 900   mm wide. All platforms shall have kick  plate of 100mm high x 6mm thick for protection for personnel around periphery.   It   shall be
--- Page 280 ---
Client   –   Hindalco Industries Ltd  Project   -   Aditya Alumina Refinery   TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR MAIN PLANT SYSTEM  TCE.13833A - ME - 6002 - 6001, Rev - R 0   Page   278   of   342  adequately sized and supported for personnel and material loadings as necessary. The  platforms and walkways details for major equipment as per   manufacturer’s   design.  A continuous walkway minimum 750mm wide and at least 500 mm wide at column locations  shall be provided along the crane girder level with handrails on both sides of the building.  Approach to EOT   crane shall be ensured by Cage ladder or staircase.  e)   STEEL STAIRS  All steel staircases, except fire escape staircases, shall normally have minimum clear width  (back to back of stringer) of 1000 mm, minimum 250mm tread width and maximum riser of  180mm. Number of riser in   each   flight shall not exceed 15. Channels (minimum MC200) shall  be provided as stair stringers. Treads shall be of Chequered plate/grating, with suitable nosing,  and   spaced equally.  All stair   treads   shall be galvanized steel grating , 25 mm x 6mm   load bearing bar, cast abrasive  nosing.  Fire   escape staircases shall be provided as per NFPA guidelines.  f)   STEEL LADDERS  Ladders shall be provided to platforms, walkways, instruments and equipment which do not  require frequent access. Ladders shall preferably be vertical and its angle with vertical shall  not exceed 45 deg. Ladders shall be of minimum 450 mm clear width with   65mm x 12mm with  20mm diameter rungs spaced at 300 mm (maximum).  Safety cages shall be provided for vertical ladders as per OSHA standards and cages shall be  tied into the handrails of lower levels. The top step of vertical ladders shall be level with the  platform and the horizontal gap between the top rung of the ladde r and the platform shall not  exceed 75mm.
--- Page 281 ---
Client   –   Hindalco Industries Ltd  Project   -   Aditya Alumina Refinery   TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR MAIN PLANT SYSTEM  TCE.13833A - ME - 6002 - 6001, Rev - R 0   Page   279   of   342  g)   RCC STAIRS  All stairs shall have a maximum riser of 180 mm and a minimum tread of 250 mm. However,  for service building, admin building, and other public buildings riser shall be limited to 150 mm  and tread width of 300 mm. Minimum width of stairs shall be 1250 mm ge nerally. All stairs  normally shall have not more than 15 risers in one flight. Aluminium angle nosing with  minimum 50X25X3 angle or PVC nosing shall be provided for edge protection of RCC stairs.  Fire escape staircase shall be as per the local fire regulat ions authority/ TAC/ NFPA guidelines.  h)   HANDRAILS  Handrails shall be provided at appropriate places to ensure safety e.g. around all floors/roof  openings, projections / balconies, walkways, platforms, steel stairs etc.  All handrails shall be of 32 mm nominal bore MS pipes as per international standards  galvanised using 750 gm/sq. m of zinc. Handrails for platforms, walkways and projections shall  be a two - rail system with the top rail 1000 mm above the walkway surface and   the  intermediate rail 450 mm below the top rail. Handrail post spacing shall be limited to 1500  mm as far as possible but can be proportioned to the length of the protected horizontal  opening. In such a case spacing shall not exceed 1800 mm center to cent er of posts. Handrails  shall be shop fabricated for specific locations and bolted to the erected structural steel. For  platforms at an elevation of more than 30 m, three rail system with top rail at 1500 mm shall  be adopted. 100 mm wide and 6 mm thick toe   guard shall be provided at landings and flat  locations.  For RCC stairs, handrails with 20 mm square MS bar balustrade with suitable MS flat &  aluminium / Teakwood handrail shall be provided in general unless specifically mentioned  elsewhere in the specification.  i)   EDGE PROTECTION  Wherever possible around floor openings an RCC kerb of 100 mm wide 150 mm high shall be  provided. All concrete edges, where breakage of concrete corner is expected, shall be provided  with angles at least L 50x50x6 with lugs for edge protection e.g. all rou nd the cut -  outs/openings in floor slab, edges of drains supporting grating covers, edges of RCC cable/pipe  trenches supporting covers, edges of manholes supporting covers and supporting edges of  precast covers etc.  j)   GRATING  Grating will be of welded rectangular steel bar type with min. thickness 32mm with 32mm x  5mm main bars on not more than 30mm bar spacing and 100mm cross bar pitch. Heavy duty  grating shall be used for areas with vehicle and forklift access. All grating sh all be hot dipped

Areas exposed to chemical attack shall have Fiberglass Reinforced Polyester (FRP)  grating with FR gray grit top.  Note: Cutting/welding of grating banding bars on site will not be allowed except on   unforeseen  cases which shall be discussed with quality team and subject to Owner's   approval.  k)   ANCHOR BOLTS AND INSERT PLATES  Anchor bolts shall be designed for working stress, in tension and shear, for embedded length  of the anchor bolts and pipe sleeves. Shear and crushing strength of concrete shall also be  checked.  Insert plates shall be designed / checked for shear and bending moment. All lugs shall be  checked for tension. Bond strength of concrete shall also be checked. Lugs using steel bars  shall preferably be fillet welded to   the plate to transfer full strength of the lug. Preferably lugs  shall be of square bar type.  l)   VERTICAL HEADROOMS  All accessible areas shall be provided with minimum clear headroom as   indicated in Chapter -  4, General Requirement.  m)   EXPANSION/CONSTRUCTION JOINTS  Expansion and construction joints shall be provided wherever required. All expansion and  construction joints of water retaining structures in RCC shall be made watertight using PVC  ribbed water stops with central bulb. However, kicker type (externally plac ed) PVC water stops  may be used for the base slabs and in other areas where it is required to facilitate concreting.  The minimum thickness of PVC water stops shall be 6 mm and minimum width shall be 225  mm. At other joints these shall be 150 mm wide.  Two - part poly sulphide sealant conforming to international standards or any other sealing  material as approved by   Owner /   Owner ’s Engineer   shall be used for sealing of joints in contact  with water. For other cases, bitumen sealing compound can be used. Preformed bitumen  impregnated fiber board conforming to standards shall be used as joint filler.  n)   BRICK MASONRY AND PARAPET WALL  All masonry works shall be designed in accordance with international codes and regulations as  applicable. Structural design of load bearing, and non - load bearing walls constructed with solid  or perforated burnt clay bricks or fly ash bricks shall be in acc ordance with criteria specified by  applicable codes and standards.  Thickness of wall shall be as per   Indian   standard code of practice.

Client   –   Hindalco Industries Ltd  Project   -   Aditya Alumina Refinery   TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR MAIN PLANT SYSTEM  TCE.13833A - ME - 6002 - 6001, Rev - R 0   Page   281   of   342  Damp proofing course shall be provided at plinth level as per standards.  All up stands and parapet walls on roof shall be of RCC construction, minimum height of  parapet walls shall be 900 mm and thickness 125 mm  o)   FLOOR DRAINAGE  For all   buildings   and areas, suitable arrangements for draining out water collected from  equipment   blow downs, leakage, floor washing, firefighting, etc. shall be provided on each  floor. Gully traps, inspection pits, collecting pits etc. shall be located suitably and designed  considering flow volume, easy access, maintenance and safety.  All   drains   inside   the building shall have minimum 40 mm thick grating covers. In areas where  heavy equipment loads would be coming, precast RCC covers shall be provided in place of steel  grating. These drains shall lead the water to drain sump.  Garland   drains   shall be   provided   around all buildings to receive the drainage water from roof  and floor and lead them to the plant storm water drainage system.  p)   ROOF DRAINAGE  Roof drainage system shall be provided for quick and efficient draining of rainwater from roof  to avoid seepage and damage to roof. The runoff gradient for the roof shall not be less than 1  in 100. The gradient shall be made by screed concrete of 50 mm min imum thickness. Roof  drainage   system shall consist of roof drain heads, rainwater down take pipes and fixtures. The  system shall be designed to handle design requirements for the specific site and shall be in  accordance to stipulations of international sta ndards. Roof drains shall conduct water to storm  drains through down take pipes.  Rain water   down   take pipes shall be UPVC pipe   conforming   to standards.

All cable   vaults   shall be located above ground level i.e. cable vaults shall not be provided as  basements in the buildings.  Finished   floor   levels of Boiler area / transformer area yard paving shall be kept 100 mm lower  than the finished floor level of turbine building.  u)   FIRE AND SAFETY REQUIREMENTS  All the applicable statutory rules pertaining to the country, Fire safety rules of Tariff Advisory  Committee,   Water   act of Pollution Control Boards, Explosives act etc., and stipulations of other  relevant statutory authorities shall be taken into consideration at the time of design.  Provisions of safety, health and welfare according to Factories act shall be complied with  design   stage . These shall include provision of continuous walkway (minimum 750 mm wide)  along crane - girder at crane girder level on both sides of the building, comfortable approach to  EOT crane cabin, railings, fire escape, locker room for workmen, pantry, toilets, re st room etc.

All masonry firewalls  shall be minimum 345 mm thick and RCC firewall shall be minimum 200 mm thick.  8.1.6   LOADS AND LOAD COMBINATIONS  In general, different loading conditions have to be considered for the design of the plant and other  structures. Following loads shall be considered in the design:  1)   Dead load (DL)  2)   Live load (LL)  3)   Dust load  4)   Wind load (WL)  5)   Seismic load (SL)  6)   Dynamic load  7)   Impact Load (IL)  8)   Equipment loads (EL)  a.   Equipment Dead Load (ELe)  b.   Equipment Operating Load (ELo)  c.   Equipment Test Load (ELt)  9)   Construction load  10)   Thermal Load/Friction load  11)   Bundle Pull Load (if any)  12)   EOT Crane, Monorail load (if any)  13)   Earth pressure  14)   Liquid pressure  15)   Pipe Loads  16)   Vibration Load  Permanent/Dead Loads (DL)  Dead load is the self - weight of the structure and the   weight of all materials permanently fastened  there to or supported on the structure thereby, such as fireproofing, finishes, pipes, insulations and  walkways, weight of empty equipment, ducts, trays and vessels. Dead loads shall be considered as  specified i n I.S. 875 (Part 1). Unit weight to be considered for various materials shall be as follows:

Live loads shall be uniformly distributed/concentrically applied over the horizontal projection of the  specified area. Distributed live load is applied to all areas except directly under equipment. Collateral  load accounts for piping and miscellaneous util ities and is applied to all areas. Roof Dust Loads  Floor dust loads:   Uniformly distributed load of 1 kN/m2 will be considered for areas where there is  likelihood of accumulation of dust on floors.  Roof Dust Loads : Dust load will be considered in a selective manner depending on the project  requirement. Dust load of min. 0.5 kN/m2 on roof will be considered in areas where there is  possibility of accumulation of dust on roof. Dust load will be taken over and above th e uniform live  loads specified in IS Codes.  Wind Load (WL)  The structures shall be designed to withstand the forces of wind pressure, assumed in any horizontal  direction with no allowance for the effect of shielding by other adjacent structures, in accordance  with the appropriate provisions of IS: 875 (Part 3). Seismic Load (SL)  All structures & foundations shall be designed to resist the effects of earthquakes in accordance with  IS 1893 (Part 1) for infrastructure building such as canteen etc. and IS 1893 (Part 4) for main plants,  pipe rack, chimney, compressor building, Substati on etc. and for elevated/ground supported liquid  tank IS 1893 (Part 2). All structures shall be designed for Design Basis Earthquake (DBE). Detailed  dynamic analysis shall be done based on the category of the structure.  Seismic Parameters:  Seismic design forces shall be determined based upon the   following parameters. Damping ratio coefficient shall be as per Cl 7.2.4 of IS1893 - 1:2016 as follows:  The value of damping shall be taken as 5 % percent of critical damping irrespective of the material  of construction, namely steel, reinforced concrete, masonry, or a combination thereof of these  three basic materials.  Design acceleration due to earthquake:  Horizontal acceleration values of the spectra are obtained from IS: 1893 Part 4. The horizontal  seismic co - efficient Ah = (Z/2) *(I/R)*(Sa/g). Seismic parameters Z, I, R and Sa/g are obtained as per  IS1893(Part 4):2015.  Combination of responses due to multi component seismic acceleration:  Since lateral load resisting elements are oriented along two mutually orthogonal horizontal  directions, the structures shall be designed for two component earthquakes (One horizontal and  vertical). For Horizontal earthquake load, structure shall be designe d for the effects due to full  design earthquake load in one horizontal direction at a time, and not in both directions  simultaneously.  When lateral load resisting elements are not oriented along mutually orthogonal horizontal  directions, Structure shall be designed as per Clause No.6.3.2.2. I.S. 1893 Part.1.  Effect due to vertical earthquake shaking shall be considered as per clause No. 6.3.3.1 IS 1893 (Part  1); the design vertical force shall be calculated for vertical ground motion as detailed in clause No.  6.4.6 IS 1893 (Part 1).
