--- Page 291 ---
Client   –   Hindalco Industries Ltd  Project   -   Aditya Alumina Refinery   TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR MAIN PLANT SYSTEM  TCE.13833A - ME - 6002 - 6001, Rev - R 0   Page   289   of   342  When both horizontal and vertical seismic forces are considered, load combination specified in  Clause No.6.3.4 IS 1893 (Part 1) shall be considered.  When response from the three earthquake components is to be considered (three directional  earthquake ground shaking), the response due to each component may be combined using the  assumption that when maximum response from one component occurs, the response s from other  two components are 30% each of their maximum. All possible combinations of three components  including variations in sign shall be considered. Refer clause no. 6.3.4.1 IS 1893 (Part 1) for set of  combinations of earthquake load effect.  Storey Drift Limitation:  The storey drifts in any storey due to the minimum specified design lateral force, with partial load  factor of 1.0 shall not exceed 0.004 times the storey height as per IS 1893 (Part 1), clause no. 7.11.1.  Regular and Irregular Configurations:  Buildings with simple regular geometry and uniformly distributed mass and stiffness in plan and in  elevation, suffer very less damage than building with irregular configurations. All efforts shall be  made to eliminate irregularities by modifying architectu ral planning and structural configurations.  Limits on irregularities and special requirement given in Table 5 and 6 as per IS 1893 (Part 1) clause  7.1 should be followed.  Increase in permissible stresses:  When earthquake forces are included, the allowable bearing pressure in soils shall be increased as  per Table 1 of IS 1893 (Part1), depending upon type of foundation and the type of soil. For  determining type of soil for this purpose, soil shall be classifi ed in 4 types as given in Table 2 of IS  1893 (Part1).  Hydro dynamic forces on liquid retaining structures:  Hydro dynamic forces exerted by liquid on tank wall during earthquake shall be considered in the  analysis in addition to hydro static forces. These hydro dynamic forces, namely convective and  impulsive forces are evaluated with the help of spring mass mode l of tanks. For load combination  with seismic load, the amount of liquid considered in the tank shall be normal liquid level under  service condition only. For tank full as well as empty conditions, tank shall be analysed as per IS  1893 (Part 2).  DYNAMIC AND VIBRATION LOADS  Vibration load means vibration force by heavy machinery   i.e. TG, Mill, PA, SA & ID Fans,   surge load  of catalyst, etc. which act on the structure. Rotating parts shall be considered as vibrating mass. For  rotating machinery,   the Manufacturer’s   design criteria shall be considered.
--- Page 292 ---
Client   –   Hindalco Industries Ltd  Project   -   Aditya Alumina Refinery   TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR MAIN PLANT SYSTEM  TCE.13833A - ME - 6002 - 6001, Rev - R 0   Page   290   of   342  PREVENTION FROM VIBRATION BASED ON DYNAMIC ANALYSIS  The foundation and structure of reciprocating machine and/or equipment having provable  vibration shall be designed to prevent vibration and resonance through dynamic analysis. The  design shall be in accordance with DIN 4024 or equivalent standard, and/or b y the limitations  stipulated by the equipment manufacturer.  The foundation for rotary machine without remarkable vibration such as less than 200 kW and  minor sized reciprocating machine, etc., may be designed in accordance with the following static  and experimental method:  ➢   The weight of foundation concrete of minor sized reciprocating machine shall be more than 5.0  times of total machine weight with motor.  The weight of foundation concrete of centrifugal pump, etc. shall be more than 3.0 times of the  total machine weight with motor.  Vibratory Loads:   If equipment which causes vibration is supported on floors the effect of vibration  will be considered together with the direct loads. In the absence of impact data from the equipment  manufacturers design of supports for vibratory equipment may be made as   per the following  recommendations wherever applicable:  Motor and similar balanced rotating equipment:  -   Vertical impact   -   25% of the weight of the equipment  Resonance in Structure :   Structures supporting equipment like motor and similar balanced rotating  machines, reciprocating machines, jaw/cone crushers, roll crushers, ball mills, draft fans, pumps,  vibrating screens and feeders, conveyer galleries, etc. will be designed to avoid re sonant  frequencies.  Structures supporting vibratory/reciprocating equipment will be designed so as to obviate  occurrence of resonance. The ratio of applied frequency to natural frequency will not lie within the  range 0.7 to 1.5.  Notional   Horizontal Forces:  All practical structures contain imperfections such as lack of verticality and straightness of  members. To take into account of this, the lateral load resisting system of all structures should be  capable of resisting notional horizontal forces with a minim um of 0.5% of the factored dead and  imposed loads applied at the same level as the vertical loads. A minimum notional lateral pressure  of 0.5 kN/m2 will be used if this gives a higher lateral load than 0.5% of factored dead and imposed  load. This pressure   should be applied to the enclosing elevation of the structure. No further partial  load factor need be applied. The notional horizontal forces will be assumed to act in any one
--- Page 293 ---
Client   –   Hindalco Industries Ltd  Project   -   Aditya Alumina Refinery   TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR MAIN PLANT SYSTEM  TCE.13833A - ME - 6002 - 6001, Rev - R 0   Page   291   of   342  direction at a time and will be applied at each roof and floor level or their equivalent. The notional  horizontal forces need not be applied when considering overturning, pattern loads, in combination  with other applied horizontal loads or with temperature   effects. They need not be taken to  contribute to the net reactions at the foundations.   If the specified loads from overhead  travelling cranes already include significant horizontal loads, the vertical crane loads need  not be included when calculating notional horizontal forces.  Equipment Load (EL):  Equipment Loads can be divided in following types:  ➢   Empty  ➢   Operating  ➢   Test  ➢   Exchanger loading  Equipment loads along with attached piping and platform shall be taken as given in mechanical  loading data.  Equipment dead load (ELe):  This shall mean the weight of equipment during erection and exclude the weight of internal fluids,  solids within equipment, platforms, insulation and piping attached to the equipment. Equipment  empty load shall be considered as per the details provided by   the equipment manufacturer.  Equipment operating load (ELo):  This shall mean the weight of equipment during normal operating conditions including the weight  of internal fluids, solids within equipment and all materials permanently attached to the equipment  such as platforms, piping and insulation.  Equipment test load (ELt):  This shall mean the weight of equipment, piping during hydrostatic testing after erection /  installation   including   the weight of water   within   the   equipment piping   and   all   materials  permanently attached to equipment such as platforms, insulations and piping . Equipment test load  shall be considered as per the equipment manufacturer’s data.  Bundle Pull Loads  The bundle pull design load need not exceed the total weight of the exchanger. Bundle pull load  need not be combined with wind or earthquake loads. Total bundle pull shall be considered on fixed  pedestal alone. (Note: Unless otherwise not given by mechanic al loading data sheet.)
--- Page 294 ---
Client   –   Hindalco Industries Ltd  Project   -   Aditya Alumina Refinery   TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR MAIN PLANT SYSTEM  TCE.13833A - ME - 6002 - 6001, Rev - R 0   Page   292   of   342  Bundle Pull shall mean the force needed to remove the tube bundle of heat exchanger. The pulling  force shall be applied to a fixed point along the centre line of the bundle even when jacks are  provided on the exchanger.  Total bundle forces for exchangers mounted at grade on concrete pedestals shall be resisted by the  fixed support only. However, to maintain uniformity of sizing and reinforcement both pedestals  shall be kept same.  Pulling force for exchangers mounted on the structure shall be applied to the framed structure and  foundation when externally pulled.  Earth Pressure (EP)  Earth pressure at rest, acting on basement, trenches and pits shall be calculated from the following  equations:
--- Page 295 ---
Client   –   Hindalco Industries Ltd  Project   -   Aditya Alumina Refinery   TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR MAIN PLANT SYSTEM  TCE.13833A - ME - 6002 - 6001, Rev - R 0   Page   293   of   342  Active and   passive earth pressure acting on retaining wall shall be calculated from Coulomb’s  or Rankin’s earth pressure equation.  Liquid Pressure (LP)  Liquid pressure shall be calculated by using the following equation:  Load for Pipe and Cable Racks  For designing the pipe rack superstructure and foundation the following loads shall be considered.  •   Vertical loading:  The vertical loading shall be as per the inputs provided by the piping, electrical & instrumentation  discipline. In calculating the actual weight of pipe, the class of pipe, material content and insulation,  if any, shall be taken into consideration. Insula tion density shall be taken as per the inputs from  piping discipline. In case of gas/steam carrying pipes, the material content shall be taken as 1/3rd
--- Page 296 ---
Client   –   Hindalco Industries Ltd  Project   -   Aditya Alumina Refinery   TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR MAIN PLANT SYSTEM  TCE.13833A - ME - 6002 - 6001, Rev - R 0   Page   294   of   342  volume of pipe filled with water. The total actual weight thus calculated shall then be divided by  the actual extent of the span covered by the pipes to get the uniform distributed load per unit  length of the span. To obtain the design uniformly distribute d load over the entire span, the above  load shall be assumed to be spread over the entire span.  However, minimum loading for any pipe rack shall not be less than 1.25kN/m 2 . In case, the  calculated loading is higher than 1.25 kN/m 2 , this shall be rounded off to the nearest multiple of  0.25 (i.e. 1.50, 1.75, 2.0 kN/m 2 )  In absence of data, pipe operating loads (other than gas & steam pipes) up to 300mm diameter  shall be considered as 0.8kN/m 2   (empty weight) & 1.7 kN/m 2   as water filled weight. Beyond 300mm  diameter individual point loads as per piping inputs shall be considered.  Where consideration of uplift or system stability due to wind or seismic load is required, 60% of the  design gravity loads as an "all pipes empty" load condition shall be considered.  •   Friction force:  Friction loads caused by hot lines sliding across the pipe support   during start up and shutdown are  assumed to be partially resisted through friction by nearby cold lines. Therefore, in order to provide  for a nominal imbalance of friction loads acting on a pipe support, a resultant longitudinal friction  load is applied at   each level.  If thermal loads (such as produced by temperature changes in piping, equipment and structures)  results in friction between equipment and supports (exchangers) or piping and supports, the friction  force shall be taken as the operating load on the support mu ltiplied by the applicable friction  coefficient μ given in the following table:  Surfaces   Friction coefficient (μ)  PTFE (Teflon) to PTFE (Teflon)   0.10  Stainless steel to PTFE (Teflon)   0.10  Steel to steel   0.30  Steel to Concrete   0.35  If the friction coefficient is unknown, a friction coefficient of   0.30   shall be considered.  •   Anchor and guide force (thermal load):
--- Page 297 ---
Client   –   Hindalco Industries Ltd  Project   -   Aditya Alumina Refinery   TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR MAIN PLANT SYSTEM  TCE.13833A - ME - 6002 - 6001, Rev - R 0   Page   295   of   342  The Anchor or Guide Forces in longitudinal and transverse directions shall be as per piping inputs.  Steel beams   subjected to above - mentioned horizontal anchor forces shall be designed for torsion  arising from the anchor forces.  Alternately suitable framing arrangement shall be made to eliminate torsional effect on steel beam.  In the absence of loads provided by Pipe Stress & Support group, 7.5% and 10% of the pipe total  weight shall be used as anchor load and guide load respectively acting at midspan of each bent.  Pipe rack designs shall be rechecked, when actual pipe loads, fr iction and anchor loads are known  from Plant Design & Piping group.  •   Cable Tray/Ducts and Walkway Loads:  The estimated actual load from electrical trays and instrumentation ducts shall be considered at  the specified locations, together with walkways, if provided. A minimum vertical cable tray load of  1.0 kN/m 2   per tray layer shall be used for electrical/instrument trays.  •   Wind Loads:  To calculate wind load on the pipes in the transverse direction of pipe rack, a projected height equal  to (diameter of largest pipe including   insulation (m) +10% of width of pipe rack (m)) shall be  considered. This projected height multiplied by the spacing of the portals shall be considered the  effective area for calculating the wind load on each portal. The wind load in   transverse direction  shall be considered as point loads acting at the ends of the transverse beams.  Wind load on structural members shall be calculated in addition to the wind loads on the pipes as  mentioned in the above point.  Force coefficient, “Cf” for wind load on pipes shall be considered as 0.7  Wind load on cable trays in transverse direction shall be calculated considering a projected height  of (Height of tray (m) + 10% of the width of the pipe rack (m)). The force co - efficient,” Cf” for the  cable trays shall be considered as 2.0.  •   Vibration Load:  Frames, structures and foundations for machinery or equipment causing the vibrations shall be  designed to limit vibrations to an acceptable level. It shall be designed such that whether they are  independent or part of the building, it shall not only safely   carry the loads for such items but also  prevent resonance. Further, refer machine/ equipment foundation design criteria.
--- Page 298 ---
Client   –   Hindalco Industries Ltd  Project   -   Aditya Alumina Refinery   TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR MAIN PLANT SYSTEM  TCE.13833A - ME - 6002 - 6001, Rev - R 0   Page   296   of   342  •   Temperature Load  Maximum ambient temperature 46.6 degree centigrade and minimum ambient temperature 3  degree centigrade. Temperature analysis is required for the RCC structures having length longer  than 45 m (as per clause 19.5.1 of IS456 :2000) without expansion joint and   180m for steel  structures without expansion joint (Cl. 3.10.3 of IS 800).  •   Traffic Loads  The design of slabs on grade and other structural elements shall consider the effects of vehicle  loads. Slabs on grade accessible to heavy traffic such as large earthmoving machinery, shall as a  minimum be designed for an axle load of 450 kN. Slabs on grad e where access is limited from large  machines shall be designed for an axle load of 100 kN. Construction loading shall be carefully  considered and allowed for in terms of design load as well as in considerations of age (strength) of  concrete subjected to t hese loads.  Bollards, fencing and other access limiting structures shall be installed for operational security, and  control of construction access. Impact loads shall be assessed for specific risks and operational  criteria. A commonly adopted design load of 25% of veh icle weight is recommended in the absence  of other information.  In storage areas forklifts with a minimum total weight of 7.0 t and in   areas only affected by forklift  loads in case of repair purposes a minimum total weight of 3.5 t shall be considered. Traffic loads  are tabulated below.  Description   In storage areas  Permissible total weight   70 kN  Nominal pay load   25 kN  Static load   per axle   65 kN  Track width   1.0 m  Overall width   1.2 m  Overall length   3.4 m  Bearing area per wheel   0.2 mx 0.2 m  Uniformly distributed equivalent load   20 kN/m²  Horizontal impacts on load - bearing columns and walls of buildings which are exposed to   the danger  of collision with road vehicles:  •   Impact by truck : Horizontal loads shall be 100 kN at a height of 1.2 m above ground.
--- Page 299 ---
Client   –   Hindalco Industries Ltd  Project   -   Aditya Alumina Refinery   TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR MAIN PLANT SYSTEM  TCE.13833A - ME - 6002 - 6001, Rev - R 0   Page   297   of   342  •   Impact by a fork - lift truck : Horizontal loads shall be 5 times the permissible total weight at  height of 0.75 m above ground.  The impact load need not be taken into consideration for the calculation of the foundations.  •   Thermal Loads  Thermal expansion and contraction as a result of operational or ambient factors can result in  restraint forces acting on the structure. These   restraint forces are commonly due to the friction  between the supporting structure and equipment and piping. The frictional restraint loading shall  be assessed for the operational loads on the support, multiplied by an appropriate friction factor.  Friction   factors to be adopted for design purposes shall be as follows:  •   Uncorroded steel to steel 0.30  •   Steel to concrete 0.35  •   Teflon to Teflon 0.10  •   Spillage Loads  The design loading shall take account of potential spillage from conveyors and feeders where these  are installed. The design shall allow for spillage loads of 5 kPa unless a higher value is required by  the approved Design Standard. Walkways and platforms s ubject to spillage loads shall be designed  for the live load or the spillage load, whichever is the greater.  •   Other Loads  Following   loads   shall   also be considered in the design:  ➢   Pressure loads, such as resulting from the use of expansion joints in piping.  ➢   Dynamic loads, such as resulting from rotating equipment and from surging fluids in piping  and equipment. Data for performing dynamic calculations shall be obtained from equipment  vendor.  •   Miscellaneous Loads  Following   possible additional loads shall also be considered in the design:  ➢   Loads related to reaction and resistance forces of   compensators.  ➢   Loads related to nozzle forces from piping on vessels and equipment.  ➢   Loads resulting from non - uniform foundation settlement.  ➢   Loads related to collected leaked out liquids.  ➢   Buoyancy due to groundwater or leaked out liquids.

For loading conditions applied to two or more items of equipment supported by a common structure,  variable effects such as weight of contents, wind load (when considering shielding) and thermal  reactions of piping shall be based on compatible conditions.  When hydrostatic pressure testing of equipment is required at site and more than one vessel, etc. is  supported by one structure, the structure shall be designed on the basis that only one vessel will be  tested at any one time, and that the others will be e mpty.  Maintenance loads shall be considered in design, but shall be limited to reasonable combination of  operations, i.e.  a)   In designing a structure with more than one maintenance load, consideration shall be  limited to one crane or trolley load.  b)   In designing skeleton frame - type structures, maintenance loads shall be combined with  reduced wind effects, i.e. 50% wind. Client   –   Hindalco Industries Ltd  Project   -   Aditya Alumina Refinery   TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR MAIN PLANT SYSTEM  TCE.13833A - ME - 6002 - 6001, Rev - R 0   Page   308   of   342  ➢   The above design criterion may be omitted for centrifugal pumps and fans and other minor  rotating equipment weighing less than 1 ton or if the mass of the rotating parts is less than  1/100th of the mass of foundation installed directly on concrete foundati on provided that  the weight of foundation is not less than 3 times of the equipment weight. In such cases,  dynamic analysis is not necessary.  Machine foundations shall satisfy the following requirements:  a)   Minimum reinforcement as per requirements of IS: 2974 (part 4) shall be provided unless  required otherwise by design.  b)   All units of the foundation system, except foundation raft shall be provided with symmetric  reinforcement on opposite faces, even if not required by design considerations.  c)   No common raft shall be designed for set of machines.  d)   The soil stress below foundations under dead loads shall not exceed 80% of the allowable  soil bearing capacity, or safe load on pile, for static loading. The eccentricity of load and  foundation area shall be less than 5% for block foundation and 3% of fram e foundation.  Design Of Structural Steel  Specific Design Data  The design of structural steelwork shall incorporate loading conditions applicable to the specified  equipment being supported. Temporary loading conditions that may occur during equipment  erection shall also be considered.
--- Page 311 ---
Client   –   Hindalco Industries Ltd  Project   -   Aditya Alumina Refinery   TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR MAIN PLANT SYSTEM  TCE.13833A - ME - 6002 - 6001, Rev - R 0   Page   309   of   342  Type of construction  a)   This design basis is directed mainly to the use in structures of hot rolled steel sections, plates  and tubular shapes.  b)   Fabrication of structural steelwork by shop welding is preferred. Field connections shall be  bolted as far as possible.  Material  Structural Steel:  Rolled steel sections and plates up to 20mm thickness will be semi killed/killed, and conform to  IS - 2062:2006 Grade E - 250(Fe - 410W), Quality A. Plates above 20 mm thickness will be killed and  conform to IS2062:2006 Grade E - 250(Fe - 410W), Quality B.  Higher grade steel [E300 / E350] can also be adopted for economy in design, meeting  serviceability requirements. UB/ UC/ IPE/WPB sections, now rolled in India, will also be used in  addition to Indian Sections [MB/MC]  Chequered plates will conform to IS:3502/ E - 250 - A, IS - 2062:2006. All plates 40mm thick and  above used in crane girders and similar dynamically loaded structures will be of UT grade. Plates  below 40mm thick for such application will be ultrasonically tested . Crane rails will conform to IS:  3443. Steel Tubes will conform to grade Yst 310 of IS: 1161. Hollow steel sections will be hot  formed and will conform to Grade Yst 310 of IS: 4923. All structural steel will be free from rust,  scales, laminations, cracks,   fissures and other surface defects.  To minimise wastage, wide plates (2000mm to 2500mm wide) are preferable for crane girders  over 2000 mm depth.  Abrasion resistant steel liner plates will be TISCRAL upto 12mm thick and SAIL HARD for more than  12mm thick.  Bolts And Nuts:  All bolts and nuts will conform to IS:1363 or IS:1364 as applicable. Nuts will have property class  conforming to IS:1367 compatible to the property class of the mating bolts. High strength  structural bolts and nuts will conform to IS: 3757 and IS:6623 resp ectively.  Washers:  Plain washers will conform to IS:2016 unless otherwise specified. Each nut will be provided with one  washer. Taper washers conforming to IS:5372 and IS:5374 will be used for channels (MC) and I - beams
--- Page 312 ---
Client   –   Hindalco Industries Ltd  Project   -   Aditya Alumina Refinery   TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR MAIN PLANT SYSTEM  TCE.13833A - ME - 6002 - 6001, Rev - R 0   Page   310   of   342  (MB) respectively. Washers for high strength structural bolts and nuts will conform to IS:6649. An  additional double coil helical spring washer conforming to IS:6755 will be provided for bolts carrying  dynamic or fluctuating loads and those in direct tensi on.  Roof And Side Sheeting:  In general roofs and walls of structural buildings will be covered with pre - colour coated galvanized  profiled sheets of 0.5 TCT and matching FRP translucent or polycarbonate transparent sheets of  minimum 2mm thickness on walls. Additional FRP/polycarbonate   sheets of minimum 2mm thickness  on roofs matching with the profile of the pre - colour coated sheets, will be used for buildings. Suitably  designed safety guard, made of structural steel will be used below FRP/polycarbonate sheet on roof  in order to avoid a ccident due to breakage of FRP/polycarbonate sheets during roof maintenance.  Roof and side walls of conveyor galleries will be covered (as per process requirement) with Pre -  Colour Coated sheets with a provision of gap of 300 mm below roof. Side cladding will be terminated  at 150 mm from top of floor level for ventilation.  Adequate provision for natural light inside conveyor gallery will be made through side walls by  providing translucent sheets (FRP sheets as per IS: 12866 - latest). Every sixth sheet on side wall will  be FRP sheet of thickness 3mm and will be staggered on op posite wall.  Minimum Thickness of Metal:  The minimum thickness of structural steel elements will be in accordance with IS 800 and subject to  following minimum requirements.  Gusset plate   :   8 mm  Base plate   :   20 mm  Stiffener plate for base plates   :   8 mm  Flange plates for plate girder   :   20 mm  Web plates for plate girder   :   10 mm  Stiffeners for webs of plate girder   :   8 mm  Bracing member   :   ISA 50 x 50 x 6  Handrail post   :   32NB (medium)  HR top rail   :   32NB (medium)  HR mid - rail   :   32NB (Medium)  HR toe guard   :   FS 75 x 6 mm  Chequered plates   :   6 mm o/p  Grating flats   :   5 mm  Minimum thickness of structural members other than gratings and chequered plate directly  exposed to weather and inaccessible for painting and maintenance will be 10 mm.

Client   –   Hindalco Industries Ltd  Project   -   Aditya Alumina Refinery   TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR MAIN PLANT SYSTEM  TCE.13833A - ME - 6002 - 6001, Rev - R 0   Page   311   of   342  Following norms may be adopted while selecting the thickness of gussets in braced and latticed  members.  The selection of thickness of gusset shall be based on following guidelines  Sl No   Unfactored force in member - P kN   Gusset Thickness  1   P   ≤   200 kN   :   8 mm  2   200 kN<P ≤ 400 kN   :   10 mm  3   400 kN   ≤ P   ≤ 750kN   :   12 mm  4   750 kN   ≤ P   ≤ 1000kN   :   16mm  5   P   ≥ 1000kN   :   20mm  Sealed tubes and sealed hollow box sections used for external construction exposed to weather will  not be thinner than   4mm and for construction not exposed to weather will not be thinner than  3.2mm. Where structures are not readily accessible for maintenance the minimum thickness will be  5mm.  Structural Design  a)   The structural design shall be in accordance with IS 800 by limit state design. While designing  the structure, attention should be given to Section 12 of the IS 800 dealing with design and  detailing for earthquake loads.  b)   The structural column and connections shall be checked for additional load combinations  with seismic load as given in Cl. 12.2.3 of IS 800.  c)   For special concentrically braced frames (SCBF) the compressive and tensile strength of  bracing member should comply with Cl. 12.8.2.3 and Cl. 12.8.2.4 of IS 800.  d)   Top support beams of pipe rack frames shall be framed into the columns, with the columns  extended above the top of the beam so that stubs are provided where future expansion is  required. The length of the stub shall not be less than 250 mm.  e)   Clearance from Piping and Equipment: Framing members, including column and bracing,  should clear vessels, piping, and / or related attachments, insulation and supports by a  minimum 75 mm both vertically and horizontally.  f)   Plan bracing members which are part of seismic load resisting system (SLRS) shall be plastic  sections. Tee section (or cut Tee section from I - section) shall be preferred for plan bracings.  Bracings which are not part of SLRS need not be plastic sections. F or definition and details of  SLRS refer to Section 1 of Annexure - 1. Tee section Interaction ratio shall be limited to 0.7 to  accommodate the effect of additional moment due to eccentricity.
--- Page 314 ---
Client   –   Hindalco Industries Ltd  Project   -   Aditya Alumina Refinery   TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR MAIN PLANT SYSTEM  TCE.13833A - ME - 6002 - 6001, Rev - R 0   Page   312   of   342  g)   A - Bracings or inverted A - Bracings are to be avoided. When A - bracings or inverted A - bracings  are provided, they shall be designed as per AISC 341. As per code, when these bracings are  provided, zipper columns or strengthening of horizontal beam will be requ ired.  h)   Unbraced length of the X - brace in each orthogonal direction may be taken as half the entire  length of the brace provided the two segments of each diagonal are col - linear and there is  flexural continuity at the bracing intersection.  CONNECTIONS  Welding will be used for shop fabrication and joints.   For site connections, welding or high  strength bolts will be used, except in few cases for shear connections of lighter members or  removable beam connections where bolted joints may be adopted e.g. pu rlins, side girts etc.  A minimum of two bolts per connection will be used. IS: 816 and IS: 9595 will be followed for  welding of structures.  All normal bolted connections will have bolts of minimum 16 mm dia. Erection bolts will be black  bolts of minimum 16 mm dia.  The minimum size of fillet weld will be 6 mm.   Main structural elements will be welded  continuously.  Connections for structural steel members will be designed to withstand the following minimum  forces, or the actual force as per analysis. Actual design forces will be indicated in the design  drawing. Client   –   Hindalco Industries Ltd  Project   -   Aditya Alumina Refinery   TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR MAIN PLANT SYSTEM  TCE.13833A - ME - 6002 - 6001, Rev - R 0   Page   315   of   342  PAINTING OF SEAL PLATES, STEEL DOORS & WINDOWS, VENTILATORS, LOUVERS, ROLLING  SHUTTERS, GRATINGS & HANDRAILS  (i)   Primer:   -   Red oxide zinc chromate primer conforming to IS 2074, (minimum 2x35=70 micron  DFT)  (ii)   Finish (Top) coat:   -   Synthetic Enamel Paint (conforming to IS 2933) (minimum 2x25= 50 micron  DFT)  ➢   All rust and scales shall be removed by scrapping/rubbing by emery paper or by brushing with  steel wire brushes. All dust and dirt shall be thoroughly wiped away from the surface. If the surface  is wet, it shall be dried before priming coat is undertaken.  ➢   All gratings shall be cleaned to Sa 2 ½ finish of Swedish standard SIS   -   05   -   5900 and shall be hot  dip galvanised at the rate of 610 Gms / Sq. m. All handrails and ladders shall be galvanised at the rate  of 610 Gms / Sq.m as per IS : 4736.  PAINTING OF STEEL SURFACES EMBEDDED IN CONCRETE  ➢   For the portion of Steel surfaces embedded in Concrete, the surface shall be prepared by Manual  Cleaning and provided with Primer Coat of Chlorinated Rubber based Zinc Phosphate Primer of  Minimum 50 Micron Dry Film Thickness (DFT).  ➢   All threaded and other surfaces of foundation bolts and its materials and during execution of  civil works, the dried film of coating shall be removed using organic solvents  GUIDELINES FOR STAIRCASE, LADDERS, HANDRAIL & GRATING  ➢   Steel staircases for main approaches to operating platforms shall have channels provided as  stringers with minimum clear width of 700mm and slope not exceeding 45 0 . Treads shall be minimum  300mm wide made of grating (with suitable nosing) spaced equally to restrict the riser between 150  to 200mm. Staircase head room height shall be minimum 2.1m.  ➢   Ladders shall be provided with safety cages when the top of the ladder is more than 3.0 m above  the landing level. Safety cages shall start 2.1 metres above the lower landing level. Ladders shall be  of 450mm clear width with 20mm diameter MS rungs spaced a t 300mm (maximum). Ladders shall  preferably be vertical. In no case shall the angle with the vertical exceed five degrees.  ➢   MS Gratings shall be minimum 25 mm deep and maximum span of grating shall be 1200mm.  Deflection shall not be more than 6mm or span/200, whichever is lesser.  ➢   For RC staircases, tread shall be 300mm without nosing and riser shall be 150mm. Staircase head  room height shall be minimum 2.1m.
--- Page 318 ---
Client   –   Hindalco Industries Ltd  Project   -   Aditya Alumina Refinery   TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR MAIN PLANT SYSTEM  TCE.13833A - ME - 6002 - 6001, Rev - R 0   Page   316   of   342  ➢   Maximum number of steps per flight shall not be more than 12. Only in exceptional cases, the  maximum number of risers may be limited to 15.  ➢   Tread and Risers shall not be varied within a flight.  ➢   To the extent possible same rise and tread shall be maintained for all the flights in the staircase.  ➢   The stairway shall be cleared of pipe, ducts and cable routing.  ➢   Stair landing width shall not be less than width of stairs.  ➢   In case of steel grating for steps, deflection due to point load at edge shall be checked  particularly for parallel type grating.  ➢   Providing risers in landing area shall be avoided.  8.1.8   Standard Drawings for structural steel  Standard drawings will be provided to the successful bidder for their final detail design and  drawings.  8.1.9   Standard Drawings for concrete works  Standard   drawings will be provided to the successful bidder for their final detail design  and drawings.  8.1.10   Conflict between codes and standards and specification and vendor drawings/ documents  Criteria whichever is stringent to be followed

All such functional tests shall be documented to provide a “base line” condition of  the Power Facility for use of the Owner’s O&M staff. At the same t ime, a record of any design or  construction defects or deficiencies that need to be   corrected shall be made. The applicable Air Emissions Testing Procedure will be used in the determination of test  results. In the event that such testing indicates a greater amount of air emissions than the  guaranteed amounts the Contractor shall sub mit a plan and schedule to Owner for approval,  outlining the corrective measures necessary to bring the air emissions in compliance with the  guaranteed values. Contractor may then proceed with the required Performance Guarantee
--- Page 324 ---
Client   –   Hindalco Industries Ltd  Project   -   Aditya Alumina Refinery   TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR MAIN PLANT SYSTEM  TCE.13833A - ME - 6002 - 6001, Rev - R 0   Page   322   of   342  Tests. The testing may proceed provided that the air emissions do not exceed the levels achieved  during the Emissions Test and that all the equipment in the unit or plant (as the case may be) shall  be operated as described in   the applicable testing procedures during such testing.  9.4   RELIABILITY RUN  9.4.1   After the   functional tests , the plant will be on reliability operation. During the reliability   run , the  Bidder will be allowed to make minor adjustments as may be necessary, provided that such  adjustments do not interfere with or prevent the commercial use of the plant or result in  significant reduction of output or increase of the heat rate of the uni ts.  9.4.2   The duration of the 'Reliability   Run ' of each unit will be spread over a period of seven (7) days at  full load or any other load intimated by the Owner/ Owner’s Engineer   based on process plant  demand on automatic mode and without any interruption attributable to Bidder.  9.4.3   Should any failure (other than of an entirely minor nature) due to or arising out of faulty design,  materials, or workmanship (but not otherwise) occur in any item of the plant, sufficient to prevent  commercial use of the plant, the reliability test period   of seven (7) days will recommence for that  item after the defect has been remedied by the manufacturer. The onus of proving that any failure  is not due to faulty design, materials and workmanship will lie with the Bidder.  9.4.4   A ‘reliability   run ’ report comprising observations and recordings of various parameters measured  in respect of the ‘reliability   run ’ will be prepared and submitted to the Owner /   Owner’s Engineer .  The report will have recordings of all details of interruptions that occurred, adjustments made,  and any repairs done during the ‘reliability operation’.  9.4.5   Should any failure or interruption in any portion of the tests due to or arising from faulty design,  materials, workmanship, omissions, incorrect erection, or inadequate instructions by the Bidder’s  supervisors, sufficient to prevent safe commercial use of   the plant occur, the reliability   run   test at  the particular load will be considered void and the reliability test will recommence after the Bidder  has remedied the cause of the defect.  9.4.6   The ‘reliability   run ’ will be considered successful, provided that each item of plant can meet the  above requirements.  9.4.7   Upon the completion of ‘reliability   run ’, as soon as practicable, or at such time as may be  otherwise agreed to by the parties concerned, the Bidder shall notify in writing to the   Owner   /  Owner’s Engineer   that the plant is ready for performance tests.  9.4.8   At the end of the reliability test run, the performance guarantee test shall be conducted by the  Bidder and the performance report shall be submitted to the Owner.
--- Page 325 ---
Client   –   Hindalco Industries Ltd  Project   -   Aditya Alumina Refinery   TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR MAIN PLANT SYSTEM  TCE.13833A - ME - 6002 - 6001, Rev - R 0   Page   323   of   342  9.4.9   Before taking over of the plant by the Owner, performance related parameters shall be optimally  tuned by the Contractor to the satisfaction of Owner and thereby, all the system and auto control  loops shall run satisfactorily for at least one month at varyi ng loads without disturbing any  adjustment. Any assistance required from the system OEM shall be taken for the satisfactory  functioning of the system. During this period, supervision and maintenance of the system shall be  the scope and responsibility of Co ntractor.  9.5   PERFORMANCE TESTS  9.5.1   Following the successful completion of the   reliability test   of each unit, the Performance Tests shall  be performed   (for each boiler at a time)   described herein and any further tests required by the  Owner to demonstrate that the performance of the Power Facility and the units comply with the  requirements specified in   performance guarantee   Schedul e.  9.5.2   The performance test for each unit shall be done in line ASME PTC.  9.5.3   The performance test will be conducted at site by the Bidder in presence of Owner/ Owner’s  Engineer . The tests will be commenced after the 'Plant'/'Equipment' has attained stable operation  at the successful completion of   functional test .  9.5.4   The performance test results will be corrected based on correction curves furnished by the Bidder  for variation in the specified test conditions from that of the specified design conditions. The  Bidder   will   furnish   all   necessary   performance   correction   curv es   to   be   ratified   by   the  Owner/Owner.  9.5.5   Bidder will furnish procedures for all site performance tests six (6) months in advance and these  procedures will be sent to Owner/ Owner’s Engineer   for approval.  9.5.6   The Owner reserves his right to appoint an independent inspector at his own cost as his  representative to discuss the test programme, to approve the instrumentation, to witness the  tests and to analyse the test results.  9.5.7   The performance tests will be carried out to prove the guarantees and the unit characteristics as  indicated below on the basis of principles enumerated herein. The purpose of the performance  tests is not only to check whether the plant meets the guaranteed   performances but also provide  a full mass, heat and energy balance for the whole plant which will serve as reference to evaluate  the plant performance in future over the plant life.  9.5.8   The performance test procedure, including the definition of the calculation method to be used,  the instrumentation to be installed, the instrument accuracy classes, the areas of responsibility  and the items which specifically require preparation and agreem ent will be submitted by the  Bidder for review and approval by the Owner /   Owner’s Engineer   during detail engineering phase.  Schematics   identifying   the   guarantee test   instrumentation will   be submitted   along   with
--- Page 326 ---
Client   –   Hindalco Industries Ltd  Project   -   Aditya Alumina Refinery   TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR MAIN PLANT SYSTEM  TCE.13833A - ME - 6002 - 6001, Rev - R 0   Page   324   of   342  procedure. It will be ensured that necessary test points and spool pieces are installed during the  detail engineering phase and also identified in process and instrumentation drawings.  9.5.9   The performance test instruments will be of precision type with instrument accuracy limits as  required and defined in the applicable performance test codes such that measurement  uncertainty does not exceed the values agreed to by the Bidder in the Schedule   of Performance  Guarantees.  9.5.10   All test instrumentation for the performance tests as required will be supplied by the Bidder on  loan basis. Data loggers on loan basis will be used extensively for the performance tests. All costs  associated with the supply, calibration, installation and   return of the test instrumentation / data  loggers / computers etc. are deemed to have been included in the contract price. The test will be  in accordance with those specified or as per agreed performance test codes. Batch calibration will  not be accepted.  9.5.11   The performance test results will be reported as computed from the performance test  observations with corrections for site conditions, variations in fuel, etc. and test conditions.  9.5.12   Reporting of Test Results  i.   Within two weeks after the conclusion of the performance test and unit characteristics  tests, the Bidder will submit a test report to the Owner/ Owner’s Engineer :  a.   In the case of a performance test, whether the unit passed or failed such test,  accompanied by sufficient test data and calculations to demonstrate the level of  performance attained with respect to each of the tested parameters.  b.   In case of a unit characteristics tests, the level of performance achieved with respect  to the desired levels of performance.  ii.   The report(s) will include as a minimum, the following:  a.   Description of the test procedures  b.   Standards that were used  c.   Instrumentation details and   calibration  d.   Full schematic diagrams with indication of instrument test location and identification  tag of same.  e.   Test logs and summary of test readings used for performance calculations  f.   Full set of correction curves  g.   Computation of test results.

Required  4.02  Boiler Protection System /Burner Management System i.e protection &  interlocks shall be TMR system or SIL 3 certified System. The SIL 3  certified safety system shall be of the same family as of DCS and shall  reside on the plant data highway along with the DCS controllers. Required  4.06   Smart soot blower control (if required) shall be PLC based intelligent control  system.   Required  4.07  Dedicated microprocessor / PLC based system for the ESPs as described  under the Electrical section of the specification. Required  4.09   Sequence of Events Recording system integral to the DCS and safety  system with 1 ms resolution or better shall be provided   Required  4.10  Wherever the same process parameter is used for both the safety system  and the DCS, the corresponding field sensors shall be connected to the  safety system and the input signal to the DCS shall come as hardwired  outputs from the safety system to the DCS  Required  4.11  The DCS and safety systems shall be provided unit-wise and independent  set of controllers of these systems shall be provided for equipment /  systems common to both units  Required  4.12  The DCS based control system shall be interfaced to the GIS and AIS  SCADA system. DETAILS   UNIT   Bidder's Response   TCE/HIL Response   Bidder's Response   TCE/HIL Response  4.0   Total dynamic head (TDH) at design  m  While establishing the design TDH the following shall be  considered :  Discharge Pressure:  a) Make up water storage tank operating pressure.  b) Static discharge head from the condensate pump discharge  nozzle centerline to the centerline of Make up water storage  tank inlet nozzle.  c) Pressure loss across the gland steam condenser (GSC) &  ejector condenser  d) Pressure loss through the  piping system  Suction Pressure:  The total suction pressure is equal to the sum of the condenser  pressure and the static head, minus the pressure drop in the  piping system from the condenser outlet to the centerline of  suction nozzle of pump including entrance loss at the condenser  hotwell outlet. The condenser pressure is considered as per the  heat and mass balance diagram. The static suction head is the  difference between the Low-Low Level in the hotwell and the  centerline of suction nozzle of CEP, at the design condensate  pump suction temperature.  An additional 10% margin over the calculated friction loss shall  be considered for friction loss  calculation.  5.0   Minimum flow per pump   m3/h   Not less than twenty five (25) percent  6.0   Best efficiency point of pump   %   Shall be at 100% TG MCR (Guarranty case)  7.0   Shut-off pressure   bar  Between 115% and 130% of pump design total dynamic head of  pump.  8.0   NPSH available (min.)  m  Shall be based on condenser hotwell lowest level and total head  loss in the suction line considering the condenser operating  pressure and vapour pressure of the fluid  9.0   NPSH required at 3% head break  m  Shall not be greater than 80% of NPSH available over the entire  range of operation from minimum flow to pump runout and it  should be min 1 m lesser than the NPSH available.  10.0   Pump efficieny at design point   %   By Bidder  11.0   Motor rating   kW   By Bidder  12.0   Acceptable noise level at all operating conditions   Not greater than 85 dBA at 1.0m from the outline of TCE FORM NO. 329 R 7 FILE NAME: F - 329 - Rev - R 7 . docx  ISSUE  R6  1.0   SCOPE  This specification covers the general design, preparation of general arrangement  & detailed fabrication drawings for review and approval, supply of materials,  fabrication, stage - wise inspection, final inspection, testing, packing, forwarding,  transportation and delivery at site of welded unfired pressure vessels.  2.0   CODES AND STANDARDS  2.1   The design, materials, fabrication, inspection, testing and performance of welded  unfired pressure   vessels shall comply with all currently applicable design codes  and standards, statutes, regulations and safety codes in the locality where the  equipment is to be installed. The equipment shall also conform to the latest  applicable Indian or equivalent sta ndards. Other international standards are also  acceptable, if these are established to be equal, or superior to the listed standards.  Nothing in this specification shall be construed to relieve the VENDOR of this  responsibility.  2.2   The   codes   and   standards   men tioned   in   the   various   specifications   and  requirements specified in the enquiry document shall be latest as on the day of  award of contract   of the works unless otherwise specified. Contractor shall be  responsible to inform to the Consultant / Owner in case   of any revision / re -  affirmation / amendment in the relevant codes and standards after the date of  award   of   contract   within   30   days   of   the   issue   of   such   revision   /   re -  affirm/amendment of the code / standard. Consultant / Owner may approve use of  the earlie r code / standard if the revisions do not materially affect the statutory  requirements of the project or does not impact safety practices. Any cost impact  arising out of such revisions shall be mutually agreed.  2.3   If statutory approvals   like IBR, PED, U stamp ing etc.   are specified in data sheet A,  the VENDOR shall be responsible for obtaining all approvals on design, materials,  stage - wise inspection, final inspection and testing of the vessel.  3.0   MATERIALS  3.1   All materials used in the fabrication of pressure vessels   shall be as specified in the  Mechanical Data Sheets (MDS). Equivalent materials are acceptable only when  approved by the PURCHASER in writing.  3.2   All plates shall be ultrasonically tested as follows;  (a)   For nominal thickness 20 mm and higher when used for   fabrication of dished  ends  (b)   For nominal thickness 40 mm and higher when used for fabrication of shells  (c)   For nominal thickness 50 mm and higher when used for blind flanges  (d)   All thicknesses, when used for body flanges  3.3   The VENDOR shall ensure that code mandator y requirements of impact testing  are met in accordance with the minimum design temperatures specified in the data  sheets.
--- Page 536 ---
SPEC.NO.  TCE.M4 - 102 - 01  TATA CONSULTING ENGINEERS LIMITED   SECTION:   D  WELDED UNFIRED PRESSURE VESSELS   SHEET   2   OF   9  ISSUE  R6  FILE NAME: F - 329 - Rev - R7.docx   TCE FORM NO. 329 R7  3.4   The VENDOR shall ensure that code mandatory requirements of PWHT (Post  Weld Heat Treatment), solution annealing etc are met.  3.5   The extent of radiography shall be as specified   i n mechanical data sheet. In no  case shall the radiographic examination shall be less than that specified in the  code. However, spot radiography is the minimum requirement for all vessels.  3.6   All the supplementa ry requirements for the material as mentioned in the MDS and  elsewhere in the tender documents shall be complied with .  3.7   Any non - pressure parts such as cleats,   reinforcement (R/F)   pads at support lugs  or legs, wear plates at saddles and pads at pipe supports etc., welded directly on  to the pressure parts, shall be of the same material as the pressure parts.  3.8   Reinforcement pads at nozzles and other load bearing locations shall be of   the  same material and nominal thickness as the pressure part.  3.9   Material for skirt in case of alloy steel vessels and columns shall be the same as  that of pressure part at least for a minimum length of 500 mm from bottom tan - line.  The rest of the skirt part   shall be of Carbon steel.  3.10   ALLOY STEEL PROTECTIVE LINING AND CLADDING  3.10.1   Lining is defined as loose strip lining and bush lining. Cladding may be by weld  deposit overlay or explosion bonding or roll bonding.  3.10.2   Clad plate or lining shall conform to the following   requirements;  (a)   Clad plate thickness shall be minimum 3 mm, unless specified otherwise.  (b)   Clad plate made either by the explosion bonding process or reduction roll  bonding process is acceptable.  (c)   Clad plate shall meet the ASME code requirements of SA263, SA264,   SA265  including bond test or AD - Merkblatter W - 8.   The shear bond test shall be  executed as per SA264, Figure 1.Ultrasonic inspection shall be carried out  in accordance with SA 578, acceptance level S - 6.  (d)   For   ensuring that cladding thickness of reduction roll - bonded clad plates is  not less than 3 mm, each plate shall be inspected at the mill using a "coating  gauge". Plates shall be scanned on at least three edges and one across the  width of the plate.   Thickn ess shall be plotted at points not more than 600  mm apart. The VENDOR shall furnish a mill certificate, showing an outline of  the plate and the points at which readings were taken together with the  results.  (e)   Instead of lining, weld deposit overlay may also   be considered.  (f)   At weld deposit overlay or clad - restoring welds, at least the same chemical  composition shall be present at 3 mm depth, to be analysed by the welding  procedure qualification in advance.  (g)   Weld deposit over - lay and clad - restoring welds shall b e dye penetrant tested  and shall be crack - free.  (h)   Weld deposit overlay and clad - restoring welds as a minimum, shall be  qualified as per ASME Section IX.
--- Page 537 ---
SPEC.NO.  TCE.M4 - 102 - 01  TATA CONSULTING ENGINEERS LIMITED   SECTION:   D  WELDED UNFIRED PRESSURE VESSELS   SHEET   3   OF   9  ISSUE  R6  FILE NAME: F - 329 - Rev - R7.docx   TCE FORM NO. 329 R7  3.10.3   Alloy steel lining shall not be used when cladding is specified. Alloy steel lining,  when specified, shal l be so designed that the distance between attachments does  not exceed 150 mm.  3.10.4   The minimum required thickness of pressure parts shall exclude cladding or lining.  3.10.5   Cladding or lining requirements discussed in this specification are using low and  high alloy s teels only. For cladding or lining using non - ferrous and other materials,  when specified, the VENDOR shall furnish on his drawings all details, including  Welding Procedure Specification (WPS), for review and approval.  4.0   MINIMUM THICKNESS  4.1   The thicknesses of   the pressure parts as shown in the data sheets or drawings are  the minimum acceptable thicknesses after manufacture, even though the code  may permit a lesser thickness. The minimum acceptable thickness includes  specified corrosion allowance.  4.2   The   VENDOR   sha ll   be   responsible   for   proper   selection   of   plate   thickness  considering the fabrication and forming allowances, mill under - tolerances etc., to  meet the minimum thickness requirements as specified.  5.0   SHELLS  Shell courses shall be of the largest possible dimens ions to minimise the number  of   circumferential and longitudinal   weld seams and   minimise   wastage of material  in case the material is free issue to the VENDOR.  6.0   HEADS AND REDUCING SECTIONS  6.1   Heads shall be of 2:1 ellipsoidal or equivalent ellipsoidal type (crow n radius R =  0.9D, knuckle radius r = 0.17D), unless specified otherwise, where D is the inside  diameter of the shell.  6.2   Reducing sections or conical bottoms shall have a knuckle radius of r = 0.06D,  unless specified otherwise. However, knuckle radius shall   not be less than 50 mm.  6.3   Dished ends shall be seamless or 100% radiographed when made from more than  one piece.  6.4   In case of mounded or mounted bullets the heads shall be hemispherical and shall  be   made of   crown and petal. All the weld joints shall be 100%   radiographed.  7.0   AGITATOR ASSEMBLY  7.1   Agitator assembly shall be complete with drive motor, mechanical seal with seal  housing, bearings, gear box, agitator shaft with impellers, foot - step bearing with  support, rigid coupling between agitator shaft and gear box   output shaft with spool  piece, flexible couplings between gear box and drive motor, suitable base plate  and supporting structure for agitator drive arrangement and necessary fasteners  etc. so as to make it complete in all respects.  7.2   Drive motor and gear box   shall be mounted on a common base frame and duly  coupled and aligned through flexible couplings.
--- Page 538 ---
SPEC.NO.  TCE.M4 - 102 - 01  TATA CONSULTING ENGINEERS LIMITED   SECTION:   D  WELDED UNFIRED PRESSURE VESSELS   SHEET   4   OF   9  ISSUE  R6  FILE NAME: F - 329 - Rev - R7.docx   TCE FORM NO. 329 R7  7.3   Agitator shaft and gear box output shaft shall be coupled through a rigid coupling  with split ring locking arrangement to ensure that agitator shaft does not   fall into  the vessel during operation.  7.4   Agitator, shaft and impeller assembly shall be statically and dynamically balanced.  7.5   The VENDOR shall obtain necessary approvals from the seal manufacturer  regarding arrangement of assembly and application of API cooli ng plan etc. for the  mechanical seal.  7.6   The gear box shall be rated for heavy duty agitator service, continuously working  for 24 hours a day and shall be suitable to withstand static and dynamic loads and  bending moment on the gear box due to agitator shaft.  7.7   Drive motor and gear box unit shall be provided with a suitable lifting eye hook.  7.8   There shall be no oil spillage from gear box on to the vessel.  7.9   Base frame shall be provided with suitable lifting lugs suitably designed for lifting  complete agitator assemb ly.  8.0   NOZZLES AND MANHOLES  8.1   Nozzles made from pipe shall be of seamless construction.  8.2   Nozzles fabricated from plates are acceptable for sizes larger than 250 mm NB in  case of carbon and low alloy steels and 150 mm NB in case of high alloy steels.  However,   this is to be done with prior approval from the PURCHASER and subject  to all welds being 100% radiographed.  8.3   All nozzle sizes and quantities indicated in the data sheets are tentative and for  bid purpose only. Final sizes and quantities are subject to confi rmation during  review of fabrication drawings and issuance of nozzle orientation drawings. The  BIDDER shall include and confirm in his BID, that changes, to the extent of one  size lower or higher for each nozzle and quantities plus or minus one number for  each size, are acceptable at no extra cost to the PURCHASER.  8.4   All nozzles with butt - welding end connections shall have edge preparation as per  ANSI B16.25 suitable for connected piping inside diameter. Such nozzles shall  have 100 mm extra stub length with   pipe caps for hydro test purpose.  8.5   All nozzles of sizes 40 mm NB and below shall be gusseted as per the companion  specification TCE.M4 - 102 - 34.  8.6   All openings in pressure parts shall be compensated for the area of opening as per  code. Minimum width of pad sha ll be 50 mm.  8.7   All load bearing areas in pressure parts shall be verified for buckling and local  stresses. Adequate reinforcement shall be provided as necessary.  8.8   Nozzle and manhole necks shall be flush ed   with the inside surface of vessel,  unless specified ot herwise.   Inside edges of   manholes shall be smooth and  rounded - off with a radius of 3 mm.  8.9   Nozzle projection shall be 150 mm for 6” & below and 250 mm for larger nozzles.
--- Page 539 ---
SPEC.NO.  TCE.M4 - 102 - 01  TATA CONSULTING ENGINEERS LIMITED   SECTION:   D  WELDED UNFIRED PRESSURE VESSELS   SHEET   5   OF   9  ISSUE  R6  FILE NAME: F - 329 - Rev - R7.docx   TCE FORM NO. 329 R7  8.10   Local   stress   analysis for loads due to piping and supported equipment etc. shall  be carried out for all equipment   as per WRC 107 and 297 as applicable . Critical  equipment shall be analysed by using finite element analysis method (FEM) in  case the design code does not   have such provisions.  8.11   VENDOR shall provide in his general arrangement drawing of the pressure vessel  the allowable nozzle loads of all the nozzles for which piping is connected and the  equipment is mounted (Agitator, heat exchanger etc).  9.0   FLANGES  9.1   Unless spe cified otherwise, all nozzles shall be provided with rated flanges only,  from   consideration   of   interchangeability,   as   per   the   dimensional   standards  indicated in the data sheets.  9.2   Bolt holes shall straddle the main vessel axes, unless specified otherwise.  9.3   All flanges on nozzles shall be of forged construction with integral hubs. Plate or  plate type forged flanges are not acceptable in place of forged flanges with integral  hubs.  9.4   All slip - on type flanges of sizes larger than 50 mm NB and girth flanges shall b e  provided with adequate venting arrangement during welding.  9.5   Girth flanges, if made   from   multiple plates, shall be maximum of three segments.  All welds shall be 100% radio graphed. Pre - heating and post weld heat treatment  shall be carried out as per code.  9.6   Companion flanges specified with alloy steel liner shall be supplied with 100 mm  long alloy steel stub ends with butt - welding edge preparation to suit connected pipe  thickness.  9.7   When companion flanges are of weld ed   neck type, the same shall suit bore of  co nnected pipe. However, this information shall be provided by the PURCHASER,  during review of drawings.  9.8   Blind flanges and covers intended for inspection or access openings shall be  provided with adequate arrangement to facilitate opening and closing withou t  undue effort.  9.9   Unless specified otherwise, seating surfaces for non - metallic gaskets shall have  stock or serrated finish. For other types of gaskets, the type of finish shall be  furnished by the VENDOR on the drawings for review and approval.  9.10   When flang es specified are of tongue and groove or male and female type, groove  or female face shall be on the nozzle flanges and tongue or male face on the  companion flanges.  9.11   The type of flange (Slip on, Weld Neck, Tongue & Groove, Male - Female or ring  type joint) a nd rating of flange shall be as specified in the data sheet and shall be  complied with by the BIDDER.  10.0   GASKETS  10.1   Gasket shall conform to ASME B16.20 and ASME B16.21.
--- Page 540 ---
SPEC.NO.  TCE.M4 - 102 - 01  TATA CONSULTING ENGINEERS LIMITED   SECTION:   D  WELDED UNFIRED PRESSURE VESSELS   SHEET   6   OF   9  ISSUE  R6  FILE NAME: F - 329 - Rev - R7.docx   TCE FORM NO. 329 R7  10.2   Service gasket supplied shall not be used for hydrostatic testing. Gasket used for  hydrostatic test shall be same as that of service gasket.  11.0   SUPPORTS  11.1   All skirt supports shall have at least one access opening and adequate vent holes  at the top of skirt.  11.2   Skirt shall be provided with adequate holding and climbing rungs inside, above and  bel ow the skirt opening. Details shall be furnished by the VENDOR on the drawings  for review and approval.  11.3   Unless otherwise specified, corrosion allowance of 1 mm shall be considered for  skirt.  11.4   VENDOR shall supply the template of base plate (fixing the bolt   circle diameter,  no.   of   bolt   holes and bolt   diameter)   prior to the construction   of   equipment  foundation.  11.5   BIDDER to include the load cell cover plate in his bid for equipment mounted on  load cells.  11.6   If fire proofing of the skirt is specified, the studs   shall be welded to the skirt   inside  as well as outside   as indicated in mechanical Data sheet.  12.0   MISCELLANEOUS  12.1   The sizing of vessel internals shall be such that all removable parts can be   passing  through manholes.  12.2   Wherever applicable and unless specified oth erwise in data sheets all support  rings, bolting bar for internals such as trays, tower packing’s, down comers etc.  shall be in the scope of the VENDOR.  12.3   Internal baffles, tray support beams of other internals spanning a chord or diameter  of the vessel shal l be provided with a means for allowing differential expansion  between the part and the vessel shell.  12.4   Unless specified otherwise, baffles or weir plates shall not be welded directly to the  shell or dished end of the vessel. These shall be bolted to the sup port attachments  which are welded to the shell or dished end.  12.5   The BIDDER shall include two (2) numbers stainless steel name plates in his  scope. This requirement is exclusive of manufacturer's name plate. The details on  name plate shall be furnished duri ng review of fabrication drawings.  12.6   Lifting lugs and tailing shall be provided for all vessels. For heavy and tall vessels ,  the lifting and tailing lugs shall be provided based on the approved methodologies.  Lifting lugs shall be designed with impact factor   of 2. Details are subject to review  and approval. The local stresses on shell or dish ends arising out of the lifting lugs  shall also be submitted for review / approval.  12.7   Earthing cleats as per companion specification TCE.M4 - 102 - 43 shall be provided  at t he approved locations.  12.8   All reinforcement pads at nozzles, supports etc. shall be provided with a tell - tale  hole at the bottom - most location, which shall be tested with air at 1.0 kg/cm²(g) and
--- Page 541 ---
SPEC.NO.  TCE.M4 - 102 - 01  TATA CONSULTING ENGINEERS LIMITED   SECTION:   D  WELDED UNFIRED PRESSURE VESSELS   SHEET   7   OF   9  ISSUE  R6  FILE NAME: F - 329 - Rev - R7.docx   TCE FORM NO. 329 R7  checked with soap solution for leakages if any. Such tell - tale   holes shall be plugged  with either hard grease or cap after satisfactory testing.  12.9   For insulated or fire proofed vessels, insulation or fire proofing supports as per  enclosed companion specification shall be provided by the VENDOR.  12.10   Wherever platforms and la dders are specified in the data sheets, necessary  supports shall be provided by the VENDOR as per details which shall be made  available during review of drawings.  12.11   Where materials are “free issue” as indicated in the enquiry specification or data  sheet, the   BIDDER shall furnish along with the technical BID, a firm Bill of Materials  (BOM) of all “free issue” items and indicate cutting and wastage allowances both  accountable and unaccountable.  12.12   Nitrogen blanketing shall be provided for the critical vessels that   are being  transported though high seas.  12.13   Vendor to perform transportation saddle analysis for long / tall vessels.  13.0   ANCHOR BOLTS  Anchor bolts are not included in the scope of supply of the VENDOR, unless  specified otherwise in the data sheets.  14.0   FABRICATION  14.1   All welders, WPS and Procedure Qualification Records (PQR) shall meet the  requirements of the specified code irrespective of pressure service or non - pressure  service.  14.2   Before welding, edges shall be checked in order to ascertain the absence of  flaking, irregularities or serious defects.  14.3   Stainless steel and high alloy steel fabrication shall have independent set of tools,  grinding wheels, rollers and wire brushes etc.  14.4   Welding shall be executed by qualified welders only and for the qualified posi tions  only.  14.5   Butt - welds shall have full penetration. Single sided welds shall be chipped back to  sound metal and re - welded from the other side. Wherever chipping is not possible,  root run shall be by TIG. Backing strip shall not be used without prior appr oval.  14.6   Openings,   reinforcing   pads   and   attachments   shall   clear   longitudinal   and  circumferential weld seams as per the stipulations of the design code.  14.7   Circumferential welds shall be situated 100 mm above or below the support rings  and packing support rings m eant for supporting internals, if specified.  14.8   Circumferential and longitudinal weld seams shall clear saddles and support lugs.  Longitudinal seams, in case of horizontal vessels, shall be at least 150 mm above  saddle wear plate.  14.9   Nozzle   and   reinforcement   pa ds,   where   applicable,   shall   be   set - in   type   and  attached to the vessel by full penetration welds.

SPEC.NO.  TCE.M4 - 102 - 01  TATA CONSULTING ENGINEERS LIMITED   SECTION:   D  WELDED UNFIRED PRESSURE VESSELS   SHEET   8   OF   9  ISSUE  R6  FILE NAME: F - 329 - Rev - R7.docx   TCE FORM NO. 329 R7  14.10   Skirt to vessel heads joints shall have continuous flat - faced weld matching with  outer diameter of the vessel. The width of the weld shall be equal to skirt th ickness  and height of weld shall be twice the width.  14.11   All welds shall be de - burred, free from under cuts, overlap ridges and valleys.  14.12   No welding shall be carried out after stress relieving and/or post weld heat  treatment and hydro testing of the vessel. All   flange faces shall be suitably  protected against oxidation during heat treatment.  15.0   INSPECTION  15.1   VENDOR shall submit the quality assurance plan (QAP) for review and approval  of the purchaser or his authorised inspection agency.  15.2   All vessels shall be offered   for inspection to purchaser or his authorised inspector  as per the approved QAP  15.3   Inspector shall have free access to all workshops of contractors & sub - contractors.  15.4   Inspection shall be carried out during material procurement, fit - up, fabrication and  before delivery and for sub ordered materials, if any.  16.0   HYDROSTATIC TEST  16.1   Hydrostatic test shall be conducted at pressure mentioned in mechanical data  sheet after complete   fabrication and post weld heat treatment. After hydrostatic  testing, vessel shall be thoroughly   cleaned and   dried.  16.2   Clean potable water shall be used for hydro - test. Sea water shall not   be   used.  16.3   During hydro - test, care shall be taken to avoid local stresse s in shell from  exceeding 90% of the yield strength of material.  16.4   Hydro - test of the tall   vessels   ca n   be performed in horizontal position provided the  static head of the tall vessel shall be added to the hydro - test pressure.  17.0   FIELD ERECTION  17.1   Where size or   shape of vessel makes it impossible to transport it in one piece for  erection by others, the VENDOR shall transport the largest shop fabricated  sections suitable for shipment and ease of handling for field erection and assembly.  Fabrication and erection of   the vessel shall be completed at the site by the  VENDOR.  17.2   All pieces shall be shop fit - up into sections and each section shall be fit to adjacent  section and all pieces shall be match marked.  17.3   The width of permissible gap during fit - up shall be in accordanc e with approved  drawings and welding procedures and shall generally be 3 mm maximum with a  tolerance of plus 0.8 mm to minus 0.0 mm.  17.4   Flame cut edges shall be ground by the VENDOR as required to remove slag,  detrimental discoloration and non - uniformity of e dges.  17.5   All radiographic requirements for welds completed in shop shall be made by the  VENDOR before the parts of section leave the shop.

Subsequently, all such surfaces shall be painted with  tw o coats of red oxide primer.  19.0   ESSENTIAL AND RECOMMENDED SPARES  19.1   The BIDDER shall include following essential spares in his scope of supply;  (a)   Two (2) gaskets as spares for each nozzle  (b)   Three (3) gaskets as spares for body or girth flanges  (c)   10% spare fasteners of   each size and length. However, the minimum  quantity shall be four (4) for each size and length.  19.2   In addition to essential spares as indicated in para 19.1, the BIDDER may  recommend additional spares considered as necessary by him, for three (3) year  troubl e - free operation.  19.3   The   BIDDER   shall   include   essential   spares   as   indicated   in   para   19.1   and  recommended   spares   as   considered   necessary   by   him,   in   TCE.M4 - 993 - 01  “Schedule of Essential and Recommended Spares”.   Total value of   such spares  shall not be included in the quoted price for supply of equipment. This value shall  be considered   for   BID evaluation.

HEALTH & SAFETY GUIDELINES FOR CONTRACTORS  2  1. P URPOSE  This manual sets out   Hindalco Industries Ltd   ( HIL ) expectations, in areas of Health and Safety (H&S),  of all contractors of   HIL   as defined in the scope and provides guidance in how these expectations can  be met.  The contractor shall meet these H&S require ments when conducting work under contract to or on  behalf of any entities related to or affiliated with   HIL . These requirements do not supersede any other  requirements, such as those described in applicable contracts between   HIL   and the contractor or in  th e applicable national or state laws and/or regulations but are intended to complement them.  •   To align with the   HIL   H&S Policy,   HIL   requires that its contractors:  •   Comply with all applicable H&S laws,   regulations,   and industry standards  •   Comply with all H&S requirements contained in the contract between the applicable   HIL   Entity  and this manual. In the event of a conflict between the terms and conditions of any such  contract and this document, the terms and conditions of such contract shal l prevail.  Contractors are responsible for the health and safety of their employees, its subcontractors (where  applicable), members of the public and others who may be affected by its activities, and for the safe  and environmentally acceptable performance   of their work.  HIL   believes in “ Z ero   Harm ” with respect to health and safety while performing work for   HIL .   All  contractors   and   their   subcontractors   are   required   to   strictly   observe   all   health   and   safety  requirements   o f   HIL ’s requirements when working i n   HIL ’s   P remises or on behalf of   HIL   or its  customers. This requirement extends to all levels of subcontracting done by the contractor.  Circumventing safety procedures and not utilizing required safety equipment or personal protective  equipment (PPE) will   not be tolerated. The consequences for contractors that fail to observe safety  requirements will be disciplinary in nature up to and including termination of the contract. A breach  of safety requirements will be deemed to be a breach of contract with   HIL .  Contractors shall ensure that H&S hazards and risks are properly identified, assessed,   controlled,   and  evaluated prior to any work begins. Only applicably competent / certified persons may perform the  specified activities  2. S COPE :  The requirements outlin ed in this document apply to all HIL contractors and can be used as a guidance  document for other works / services including, but not limited to, construction activities, equipment  installation or de - installation, maintenance, or repair activities for or o n behalf of HIL.  A.   Operations and Safety Regulations  •   The Contractor shall be solely responsible for the adequacy, stability, and safety of its operations  on the Site. The Contractor shall, prior to the commencement of Works at the Site, prepare and  submit a comprehensive set of safety regulations in connection with its operations at the Site. The  Contractor shall comply with and shall ensure that all its employees, agents and Sub - Contractors  of any tier engaged in work at the Site comply with the pro visions of such safety regulations.  •   Ensure   accident - free   working at all sites   -   No fatalit y or LTA   MTC, RWC and FAC   at any site  associated   with a vision to achieve Zero harm.
--- Page 573 ---
HEALTH & SAFETY GUIDELINES FOR CONTRACTORS  3  •   All jobs as defined in the contract within the company premises or   associated with company related  work is made/carried out in a manner that the risk to Health, Safety and Environment is adequately  addressed.  •   Compliance of all the local regulations (Acts, Rules & IS specifications) pertaining to Health -   Safety  & Environme nt including employment & conditions of services. Compliance of Health Safety  Environment standards & practices of   HIL .  B.   Safety Code of Conduct  Con t r a c tors   h a v e   to   f o l l o w   1 0 0 %   s a f e t y   n o r m s   d u r i ng   the   c o u rs e   of   c o n tr a c t   as   p e r   “ S a f e t y  C o de   o f   Con d u c t”   i n   HIL   pre m i s e s .   B r i ef   g u i d e l i n e s   o n   sa f e t y   c o d e   of   c o n d u c t   are   a p p e nd ed   b e l o w -  •   Contractor shall deploy qualified (with qualification indicated) safety officer / supervisor as  mandated in safety plan  •   Contractor shall provide/ensure site   orientation / safety induction   Job specific   training to its   each  workman prior to deploying them on the job  •   Contractor shall conduct weekly safety meeting, daily   toolbox   meetings, and monthly gate  meetings as per requirement of the safety plan and submit t he proof there   of on regular basis  •   Contractor shall ensure proper housekeeping at the work sites, segregation of waste and disposing  them at prior identified location(s)  •   Contractor shall thoroughly assess the work scope, properly understand the hazards and   risks  associated with the job and identify risk mitigation measures, submit information about the  measures and employ them while executing the job(s)  •   Contractor shall devise safety plan for work scope involved which includes but not limited to proper  acce ss, proper exits. Assembly point, storage areas, waste disposal, movement of manpower &  equipment, inspection schedules of plant & machineries, rigging plans etc  •   Contractor shall ensure deployment of qualified, skilled & competent persons.  •   Contractor shall   provide / submit work method statement   before start of the Job  •   Contractor shall ensure that his work force engaged in critical activities like confined space entry,  work at height, hot work / welding, rigging etc are medically / physically fit to carry o ut the job,  certified to that effect by medical practitioner and are specially trained to carry out these jobs  safely.  •   Contractor shall ensure that his workforce will use proper scaffold or other suitable work place  access & egress devices and will not emp loy any unsafe means like temporary work plat forms,  damage tools & equipment’s,   cable trays etc.  •   Contractor shall ensure that his entire workforce will be provided with work required PPE of good  quality (approved by/as per specifications provided by Hinda lco) and ensure their strict uses by  entire workforce.  •   Contractor shall comply with   all   safety requirements communicated to him / his representatives &  workforce in safety induction / training, contract agreement, in safety plan, safety manual etc.  •   Contrac tor shall provide detailed breakup of his estimate for compliance with safety requirements.  •   The contractor shall   c o m p l y   r e q u i r e m e n t   of   s a f e t y   work   p e r m i ts   of   HIL   as   a p p l i c a b l e,   a nd   no  work   s h al l   be   carried out without safety work permit failing which   penalty will be imposed.  •   The Contractor shall compensate the Company for loss or liability suffered or incurred on account  of defaults or non - compliance to safety guidelines by the Contractor.
