Hindalco Industries Limited  Electrical Safety Standard   Document No.   HI L/ O HS/STD / ES/01  Effective Date   19.03.2020  Review Date   19.03.2022  Page   13   of 58  6 .2.2   Safety certification of welding machines :  Annexure - 2 used for carrying out audit of equipment used in electrical department.  Key Principle for safe operation :  1.   All welding machines   used at the site shall be pre - inspected by concerned site supervisor and  authorized for their safe use.  2.   Use all PPE as per approved by Hot work committee during work.  3.   Each welding machine must be connected to input power circuit designed for welding work   only,  which is protected by ELCB/RCCB.  4.   Transport it in a secure manner & Store it in a careful manner when not in use.  5.   Earthing Returns:  a.   Welding current should preferably be returned to the welding m/c by a single cable from the  work to the welding m/c.  b.   Pi pelines containing gases or inflammable liquids or conduits carrying E & I conductors shall not  be used for a ground return circuit.  c.   Earth return should be given as close to the job as possible.  6.   The return cable to be bolted properly.  7.   The frame or case of   electric hot work machines shall be grounded. Ground connections shall  be mechanically strong and electrically adequate for the required current.  8.   Use double insulated insulating Holder.  9.   Always keep the hands and the body dry during welding testing job.  10.   Re check & confirm that the   Electrical   circuit has been switched off / Isolated   after check - up .  11.   Know the location of safety devices such as fire extinguishers.  12.   All transformer type welding machine may be discourage for use in order to eliminating risk of  an ac cidental instantaneous peak of current that may damage the other vulnerable electronic  parts, cable etc. and in turn cause life risk to welders.  13.   All three phase welding machine incoming power cable should be minimum 4 core cable except  for Pot room.

For P ot room, 3 core power cable is ok if the machine is working in non - earthing zone (Room -  A, Room - B and basement area), A specific Tag (For Pot room only) on such machine is to be  painted.  15.   All three phase Welding M/c incoming power cable should be maximum 15   m without any joints.  Procedure for certification:  1. (as per location defined above)  4.   After inspection and testing is successful the machine will be released to the respectiv e owner  with a certificate/ test report.  5. A centralized record to be maintained at E & I workshop of all the machines. Procedure for certification:  1.   Reports to the concern electrical department for any damages to any electrical outlet   for  rectification.  2.   Ensure healthiness of two point earthing to the container. If it was damaged, report the same  to concern electrical maintenance team.  3.   Ensure healthiness of ELCB/RCCB and testing being carried out periodically as scheduled. If  it was not   done, report the same to concern electrical maintenance team.  4.   Do not use loose wire for further temporary connection.  5.   Do not connect any appliances without plug top.  6.   No joint in cable of connecting appliances.  7.   Always keep the hands dry when connecting any   appliances.  8.   Recheck & confirm that the circuit has been switched off, prior to take any connection.  9.   No fabrication work is allowed to electrically power portable cabin, metallic container until  approved by concerned electrical maintenance team.  10.   Do not conn ect heavy electrical load equipment to the portable cabin, metallic container.  11.   Do not provide any permanent extra connection to the portable cabin which may be required  by user.
--- Page 614 ---
Hindalco Industries Limited  Electrical Safety Standard   Document No.   HI L/ O HS/STD / ES/01  Effective Date   19.03.2020  Review Date   19.03.2022  Page   16   of 58  12.   The electric fittings of the cabins are designed for minimal vibration exposur e. When higher  loads are given, appropriate measures must be taken according to the national technical  regulations (and/or checks of the plug or screw contacts).  13.   Do not place the material at top of the portable cabin/ container having electrical installat ion.  14.   If water is coming inside, inform electrical maintenance team to cut the power.  15.   All conduits used in the container shall be of PVC/ HDPE material.  6.2.4   Safety Check - up of Hand tools :  Annexure - 4 used for carrying out audit of equipment used in electrical department.  Key Principle for safe operation :  1.   Tools in defective condition must not be used for maintenance   activity.  2.   Hand tools that are broken or require repair should be kept separately and labelled with a  warning notice " DANGER!   DO NOT USE FOR   WORK!”  3.   Never carry tools in your pocket.  4.   Hand tools should not be used for purposes other than they are designed for . Their material  strength is always designed according to the nature of work to be performed. Tools being  misused may lead to fracture, causing danger to persons.  5.   Never leave power tools in work area.  6.   One should concentrate on the job when using a hand too l.  7.   Playing with hand tools should be strictly prohibited.  8.   Select the right tools for right work.  9.   To ensure that tool is in good condition & keep it is in safe condition.  10.   When using a tool in overhead place, care should be taken to avoid any slipping or fa lling of  tools. This may cause an injury to the person working below.  11.   Hand tools should be inspected every time before use.
--- Page 615 ---
Hindalco Industries Limited  Electrical Safety Standard   Document No.   HI L/ O HS/STD / ES/01  Effective Date   19.03.2020  Review Date   19.03.2022  Page   17   of 58  12.   Special care should be paid to the cleanliness of tools. Blunt cutting edge or deformed  working part should be redressed.  13.   Hand tool s should be systematically kept or stored at tool - rack or toolbox after use. Those  having sharp corners or edges should be protected by sheaths before they are stored.  14.   Every hand tool should be kept in an orderly manner at a workroom or toolbox designated   for  the purpose.  15.   Periodic examination, repair and maintenance of hand tools should be carried out only by  persons who are experienced and competent.  16.   Hand tools should only be carried to work area in a proper toolbox or with the use of a tool -  belt.  17.   Special   care should be taken to prevent other persons gaining access to the work area where  long handle tools are being operated. When necessary, the work area should be fenced off  to prevent unauthorized entry.  18.   Before working, work pieces should be checked for pr otruding metal parts that may cause  damage to hand tools.  19.   Precautions should be taken when working on or near electrical conductors.  20.   Hand tools should be operated in correct posture and strength.  21.   Proper steps and procedures should be followed when operatin g a hand tool, e.g. the face of  the hammer head instead of the peen should be used for hammering nails; the handle of a  spanner should not be hammered or extended by tubes for applying greater strength in  screwing of bolts or nuts.  22.   When hand tools with sha rp corners or edges are used, their direction of movement should  be away from the body.  23.   Suitable personal protective equipment such as helmets, aprons or gloves should be used  when necessary.  24.   When flying fragments, particles or noise are generated during   the operation of hand tools,  suitable personal protective equipment, e.g., goggles, masks or ear - muffs that conform to  safety standards should be worn.  25.   The operator should use clamps to secure a work piece that is liable to move into a stable  position.
--- Page 616 ---
Hindalco Industries Limited  Electrical Safety Standard   Document No.   HI L/ O HS/STD / ES/01  Effective Date   19.03.2020  Review Date   19.03.2022  Page   18   of 58  26.   Mai ntain all hand tools and similar equipment in top - notch working condition, whether they  belong to you or the company.  27.   Store tools with sharp edges so that they cannot cause injury or damage.  28.   Do not carry tools with sharp edges in your pocket.  29.   Do not leave   tools lying around where they could create an obstruction or a hazard, such as  causing a person to trip.  30.   Clean, oil, or adjust machinery only when it is not in motion.  31.   Keep tools and accessories clean, sharp, and correctly oiled.  32.   Keep impact tools such as   drift pins, wedges, and chisels free of mushroom   Heads .  33.   Select the appropriate hand tool for each specific task, and then properly use it, as it has  been designed. For example, never use a wrench as a hammer or a screwdriver for   prying.  34.   Only operate tools   within the rate limits.  35.   Never try to increase a tool’s capacity   with bypasses   or other modifications.  36.   Never attempt to bypass the   manufacturers   installed safety devices.  37.   For following specific jobs, the person should wear requisite PPE as per the unit   safe  practices     Energy meter/Other meter readings     Protection relay settings     Battery bank electrolyte checking & filling     Lift Testing     Testing of zero speed   switch  6.2.5   Audit of electrical installations:   Audit of electrical installation is being carried out as  per CE A   rule 2010.   Attachment of checklist is attached in annexure - 5.  6.2.6   Earthing   standard:   Minimum conductor size for earthing is mentioned in annexure - 6.  General notes:
--- Page 617 ---
Hindalco Industries Limited  Electrical Safety Standard   Document No.   HI L/ O HS/STD / ES/01  Effective Date   19.03.2020  Review Date   19.03.2022  Page   19   of 58     Earthing shall be done in accordance with the requirements laid down in specification for  Earthing Installation IS: 3043.     The distance between the two electrodes shall not be less than 2 times the length of  electrode.     Overall earth grid shall be made with 75 x 10 mm GI strip.   ( The design of earth grid  depend upon the System Fault current & operating time)       GI earth plates wherever provided shall be connected to the grid with two runs of 40 x 5  mm GI strips.     All the el ectrical trays shall be earthed suitably at an interval of 30m (Approx.) by tapping  40 x 5 mm GI strip from main grid and connected to the tray support/base plate.     Joints and   tapping   in the main earth strip shall be made by welding only. Exposed welded  jo int shall be given two coats of metallic primer and then a coat of zinc based paint.     Underground joints shall be given two coats of bitumen compound and shall be wrapped  with bitumen impregnated hessian tape.     Cable armour in case of armoured cables shall   be earthed through the cable glands.     50 x 6 mm GI strip shall be used for making the underground earth grid for street lighting  poles and flood lighting masts     Earth resistance should be checked at least once in a year, preferably in dry seasons.  1.   HT Motors   -   11KV, 6.6 KV and 690V  Frame of All HT Motors shall be earthed at two visible points. Motor phase side Power  Terminal Box (PSTB) shall also be earthed at two points.  2.   Transformer:  Transformer body shall be earthed by two separate and distinct connections with the grid.     The neutral of every transformer/generator shall be earthed by connecting it to not less than  two separate and distinct connections to the separate earthed pits, whi ch may be further  connected to the earthed grid.     The Neutral of the transformer must be insulated.     Jumper sizes for earthing transformer access ories as mentioned in Annexure - 7 .

Hindalco Industries Limited  Electrical Safety Standard   Document No.   HI L/ O HS/STD / ES/01  Effective Date   19.03.2020  Review Date   19.03.2022  Page   20   of 58  3.   415V Motors  Frame of All 415V Motors of any size shall be earthed at t wo visible points. However, Power  terminal box is not required to be earthed for any size of 415v Motor. Frame of Actuator motors  and Soot Blower motors shall also be earthed at two points. Earthing should be done at  designated place.  4 . Power Junction Bo xes  All Power Junction Boxes shall be earthed at two visible points. Earthing should be done at  designated place.  5 . Three Phase Receptacle Box  All three phase receptacle boxes shall be earthed at two visible points. Earthing should be done at  designated place.  6 . Single Phase Receptacle Box  All three phase receptacle boxes shall be earthed at two visible points. Earthing should be done at  desig nated place.  7.   Single   Phase Extension Board  Mobile single phase extension Boards shall be earthed inside the Box through incoming power  cable. One spare core shall be provided for this purpose. This will be earthed at one point only.  Alternatively, local earthing can also be done at one point only.   Earthing should be done at  designated place.  8 . Mobile three phase extension Board  Mobile single phase extension Boards shall be earthed inside the Box through incoming power  cable. One spare core shall be provided for this purpose. This will be earthed at one point only.  Alternatively, local earthing can also be done at one point only.   Earthing should be done at  designated place.  9 . Welding Machines  Welding machines shall be earthed through incoming cable inside the machine. One spare core  shall be provided for this purpose. Welding machines are required to be earthed at one point only .  Alternatively, if earth is not provided through cable it will be earthed through crocodile clamps  locally at one point only. Earthing should be done at designated place.  10 . Portable Electrical Tools
--- Page 619 ---
Hindalco Industries Limited  Electrical Safety Standard   Document No.   HI L/ O HS/STD / ES/01  Effective Date   19.03.2020  Review Date   19.03.2022  Page   21   of 58  Portable electrical hand held tools are not required   to be earthed. However, it is must to ensure  that such tools are double insulated. For double insulated electrically operated hand tools, 2 core  cable can be used. For others (non -   double insulated) 3 core cable must be used.  11 . Electrical Panels  Elec trical panels are required to be earthed at two visible points. All doors and movable parts shall  be earthed using flexible copper connection to the fixed frame of the panel/ JB.  Foundation bolt of equipment should not be used for earthing. Earthing should   be done at  designated place.  12 . Hoist and Crane  Rails of Hoist and crane shall be earthed at two points (both ends). Earthing should be done by  welding on rail of size 50x6 sq . mm GI strip.  The two rails should be connected by a flexible jumper with ri veted or bolting system to make  complete rail system connected electrically.  13 . MLDB   (Main Lighting Distribution Board)  MLDB shall be earthed at two visible points. Foundation bolt of equipment should not be used for  earthing. Earthing should be done at   designated place.  14 . LDB   (Lighting Distribution Board)  LDB shall be earthed at two visible points. Foundation bolt of equipment should not be used for  earthing. Earthing should be done at designated place.  15 . Light Fittings  Light fittings along with Control Gear Box shall be earthed via spare core of incoming cable.  Earthing should be done at designated place.  16 . Street Light Pole  All type of lighting pole shall be earthed at two points by connecting to the plant grid. Fou ndation  bolt of equipment should not be used for earthing. Earthing should be done at designated place.  17 . HIGH MAST AND FLOOD LIGHT POLE  Pole shall be earthed at two points by connecting to the plant grid. Foundation bolt of equipment  should not be used   for earthing. Earthing should be done at designated place.  18 . Steel Structure Earthing  Steel structure should be earth at every 24 Meters by welding.  19 . Steel Pipe Rack Earthing

However, valve and f lange should be earthed  with flexible copper cable.  20 . Tank, Vessel and Sphere Earthing  All tank and vessel shall be earthed at two visible points.  21 . RCC Chimney Earthing     For Chimney up to 1.5 Diameter at Top, 1 no. air terminal and 1 no. down cond uctor shall  be provided.     For chimney more than 1.5 diameter at top, 2 no. air terminal and 2 no. down conductor  shall be provided.     A bonding ring of 20x3 mm GI strip welded to air terminals and reinforcement bars of  chimney.     Clamp at interval of 500   mm   using GI spacer and saddles.  22. Lightning Arrester (LA)   Earthing     For   LA earthing, earth connection to the earthing strip shall be short and direct without any  loop .  23. Building   Earthing     For   multi storied buildings (Apartment blocks), lightning arreste r spike with insulated down  conductors are provided. Also these are connected to the earthing network.  6.2.7   Other standard which is applicable and maintained as per IE rules  1   Unit shall have electrical safety procedures that are easily accessible and   up - to - date. These  procedures shall be developed using a risk - assessment process and shall contain the  safe practices necessary to identify and manage the electrical hazard exposure. In addition  to internal operations, these procedures should co ver normal and emergency operations  associated with the electrical supply utility.  2   Electrical safety procedures shall be reviewed and reauthorized at intervals not to exceed  three years and shall be consistent with local regulations and the requirements of   this  standard.  3   As per IE rules all sub - stations should have minimum two exits with self - glowing signage.  4   W hen working on or near energized electrical equipment is that no work should be performed  within the prohibited approach boundary.
--- Page 621 ---
Hindalco Industries Limited  Electrical Safety Standard   Document No.   HI L/ O HS/STD / ES/01  Effective Date   19.03.2020  Review Date   19.03.2022  Page   23   of 58  5   Risk controls th at help achieve this goal shall include equipment and hazard assessment,  limitation of work activities based on levels of risk, and the need for a higher level of formal  authorization and work permits for work with higher levels of risk.  6   Sites should demo nstrate a commitment to minimizing exposure of working on or near  uninsulated or unguarded electrical circuits and conductors energized >50 V, including the  contact of tools or any part of the body, regardless of the PPE used.  7   As per electrical rules, all   sub standards should have minimum 2 exit.  8   Unit has implemented Zero Energy (LOTOTO) work permit system.  9   Test before   Touch   (Use LDL Method) , including verification of the test instrument before and  after the test.  10   Practices to help ensure identification, co mmunication, and understanding of the limits of the  safe work   boundary.   Personnel should know the safe work boundary to avoid electrical  hazards.   Techniques   to   enhance   communication   and   understanding   of   the   safe   work  boundary should include, but not be li mited to, temporary signs, locking doors and covers on  adjacent compartments, barricades, job walk - through, a description of the safe work boundary  in job plans,  11   Identification   of   ownership   and   control   of   hazardous   energy   during   construction,  commissioning ,   turnover   to   operations,   maintenance,   and   other   situations   in   which  control responsibility may change.  12   Warning and Danger label must be placed on all electrical equipment more than 230 volt.  13   For high voltage equipment label must be present as per   NECR Article 110.16 .   OSHA  guideline and NFPA 70E.  6.2.8   Battery Safety:  PRECAUTION DURING SHIFTING:  1.   Do not place or drop metal objects on top of the battery.  2.   Remove rings, wristwatch, loose coins   from the pockets and articles of clothing with metal  parts that might come in contact with the battery terminals.  3.   Do not smoke in the battery room as it is a fire hazard.  4.   Keep the ventilation cap always closed, it may cause an accident to shifting personne l.
--- Page 622 ---
Hindalco Industries Limited  Electrical Safety Standard   Document No.   HI L/ O HS/STD / ES/01  Effective Date   19.03.2020  Review Date   19.03.2022  Page   24   of 58  5.   Use proper shifting vehicle/ arrangement to shift the battery from one place to another  place.  6.   After shifting, install as per OEM installation manual.  STORAGE OF NEW BATTERY IN FILLED AND CHARGED CONDITION:  1.   A filled and charged battery can be stored for   a period of one year.  2.   Store the battery in a clean, dry and cool location.  3.   Do not smoke in the battery room as it is a fire hazard.  4.   Cover the battery with a sheet or plastic or similar protective cover to keep out water and  shield against direct sunlight.  5.   Do not store other material on top of the battery.  STORAGE OF NEW BATTERY IN DRY AND DISCHARGED CONDITION:  1.   A dry and discharged battery can be stored for a period of around ten years.  2.   Store the battery in a clean, dry and cool location.  3.   Do not smoke in t he battery room as it is a fire hazard.  4.   Cover the battery with a sheet or plastic or similar protective cover to keep out water and  shield against direct sunlight.  5.   Do not store other material on top of the battery.  6.   Do not remove the plastic film sealing th e flip top vent.  7.   After removing from the storage, follow the instructions for electrolyte filling and first  charging.  STORAGE OF USED BATTERY IN FILLED AND CHARGED CONDITION:  1.   If the battery is going to be left idle for a long period of time, but not more t han one year, it  should be removed from service and properly stored.  2.   Check the electrolyte level if the batteries are not maintenance free type.  3.   Fill the DM/ Distell water up to maximum limit and not below the plate surface. Only DM  water is to be used to   compensate for the loss of water during charging, and maintain the  required electrolyte level and specific gravity.  4.   Clean the battery thoroughly, and then coat the inter - cell connector with anti -   corrosion oil.
--- Page 623 ---
Hindalco Industries Limited  Electrical Safety Standard   Document No.   HI L/ O HS/STD / ES/01  Effective Date   19.03.2020  Review Date   19.03.2022  Page   25   of 58  5.   Store the battery in a clean, dry and cool   location.  6.   Do not smoke in the battery room as it is a fire hazard.  7.   Cover the battery with a sheet or plastic or similar protective cover to keep out water and  shield against direct sunlight.  STORAGE OF USED BATTERY IN DRY AND DISCHARGED CONDITION:  1.   If the s torage period is more than one year, the battery should be fully discharged to an  end voltage of 0.6 volt per cell and the electrolyte fully drained by inverting the batteries.  2.   Use a plastic seal to seal the flip top ventilation cap.  3.   Clean the battery thor oughly, and then coat the inter - cell connector with   anti - corrosion   oil.  4.   Store the battery in a clean, dry and cool location.  5.   Do not smoke in the battery room as it is a fire hazard.  6.   Cover the battery with a sheet or plastic or similar protective cover to k eep out water and  shield against direct sunlight.  7.   Store the electrolyte in cool, air tight plastic container.  8.   DONT'S DURING BATTERY MAINTENANCE:  9.   The battery will produce a mixture of oxygen and hydrocarbon gases during the last portion  of high charging ra te, so keep flames away.  10.   Do not place or drop metal objects on top of the battery.  11.   Remove rings, wristwatch, loose coins from the pockets and articles of clothing with metal  parts that might come in contact with the battery terminals.  12.   Do not use petrol, ke rosene or any strong chemicals for cleaning batteries. This may affect  the nickel plating on the steel and copper components.  13.   Do not use wire or any hard brush to clean deposits on the inter - cell connectors and  terminals since this will damage the nickel p lating.  14.   Do not use aluminium cables for making connections to the battery terminals since  aluminium reacts with alkaline electrolyte.  15.   Do not keep vent caps open and expose electrolyte to air, since this will cause some of  the electrolyte to be converted to   potassium carbonate and thereby reduce the conductivity  of the electrolyte.
--- Page 624 ---
Hindalco Industries Limited  Electrical Safety Standard   Document No.   HI L/ O HS/STD / ES/01  Effective Date   19.03.2020  Review Date   19.03.2022  Page   26   of 58  16.   Do not spill water or electrolyte on and around the battery since this will create conductive  paths leading to leakage of current.  17.   Do not measure specific gravity immediately afte r adding D.M. /Distell water since this will  indicate a lower specific gravity due to inadequate mixing of the water with the electrolyte  in the cell.  18.   Do not expose the battery directly to sunlight for long duration since this will accelerated  aging of pla stic components due to ultra violet radiation.  19.   Do not adjust terminal connections during charging to prevent sparking which can ignite  the oxygen and hydrogen gases which are given off by the battery towards completion of  charge and cause a fire/explosion.  20.   Do not use acid since the battery requires alkaline electrolyte. Addition of acid will cause it  to react with the alkaline electrolyte and cause permanent battery damage.  21.   Do not charge the battery without removing the transport seal from the vent, or whil e the  battery is covered, since it is important to ventilate the battery and permit the gases  produced towards the end of charge to escape.  22.   Do not adjust terminal connections during usage to prevent sparking which can ignite the  oxygen and hydrocarbon gase s which are given off by the battery towards completion of  charge and   cause   a fire/ explosion.  23.   Do not smoke in the battery room as it is a fire hazard.  Safety Precaution during t esting o f   Battery :  1.   Fire extinguishers are to be kept nearby machine  2.   No smokin g Zone, No any sparking near the batteries.  3.   Water availability to flood any electrolyte spill occurring in battery operation and also for  washing body parts if affected.  4.   Sufficient ventilation is to be there to prevent accumulation of explosive atmosphere.  5.   Use Chemical resistance glove, safety   glass and   other mentioned PPE in HIRA.  6.   Keep small quantity of dry Lime near the battery / wash basin to neutralise the electrolyte  during spillage.
--- Page 625 ---
Hindalco Industries Limited  Electrical Safety Standard   Document No.   HI L/ O HS/STD / ES/01  Effective Date   19.03.2020  Review Date   19.03.2022  Page   27   of 58  6.2.9   Operation and maintenance of HT breaker (3.3 KV and above) :  KEY   PRINCIPLES  1.   100% compliance of permit to work system.  2.   No   non E   & I personnel is allowed to come nearby panel area.  3.   Minimum person to be present near breaker or feeder during operation and must use the  rated Arc Flash suite.  4.   ‘DANGER’ & “ Man AT WORK” shoul d be visible and tagged in that area.  5.   Persons, who is involved in breaker operation and HT compartment   maintenance must  wear rated Arc Flash   suit till the job completion.  6.   Persons, who is involved in breaker maintenance must work outside of arc flash bounda ry/  HT door must be closed. In this condition wearing arc flash suit is not required.  7.   All persons within arc flash boundary must wear rated arc flash suit.  8.   During de - energizing and energizing the bus bar, area - in - charge must be present near the  spot.  9.   Earth discharge rod and Rescue hook must be present at site.  10.   Avoid making direct contact with any exposed conductor of the cable.  11.   Always keep the hands and the body dry.  12.   Recheck & confirm that the electrical circuit has been switched off in all respect   (More than  one feed)   before start of work .  13.   Identification to be done for good/defective breaker or feeder module, if kept outside.  PROCEDURE : -  Sl no   Description   Purpose   Method  A  1.  Normal operation  of HT breaker (3.3  Kv & above  breaker  switchgear)  For   equipment  maintenance     HT breaker is isolated/ made OFF     Control   supply   is   cut   off   (   remote  operation is not permitted)     With door closed condition, breaker is  made free (from locked service position)  and raked out with handle from outside  to   isolated   position.   Operator   (Person  doing racking) must wear arc flash suite  with face shield and gloves.
--- Page 626 ---
Hindalco Industries Limited  Electrical Safety Standard   Document No.   HI L/ O HS/STD / ES/01  Effective Date   19.03.2020  Review Date   19.03.2022  Page   28   of 58     After   work   completion   in   the   specific  equipment,   breaker   is   raked   in   thru  handle with door closed condition and  locked   in   the   service   position.   During  racking   in also operator wears arc flash  system.     Control supply is restored     Equipment is allowed to start  2.   Normal operation  of HT breaker (3.3  Kv & above  breaker  switchgear)  For HT breaker  & compartment  maintenance     HT breaker is isolated/ made OFF     Control   supply   is   cut   off   (remote  operation is not permitted).     With door closed condition, breaker is  made free (from locked service position)  and raked out with handle from outside  to   isolated   position.   Operator   (Person  doing racking) must wear arc flash suite  w ith face shield and gloves     While Breaker in isolated position, the  door   of   HT   compartment   can   be  unlocked by wearing Arc flash system  and the breaker can be drawn outside of  the   HT   panel   for   maintenance.   After  maintenance, the breaker again to be put  at i solated position and door must be  locked to proceed further. Please note  there is no rack in / rack out process  involve in this whole process.     After   work   completion   in   the   specific  breaker, breaker is raked in thru handle  with door closed condition and lo cked in  the service position. During racking in  also operator wears arc flash system.     Control supply is restored     Equipment is allowed to start  3.   Normal way of HT  switchgear when it  gets stuck up  For  maintenance  breaker in     In unit   which is under shutdown, Breaker  stuck   up   issue   is   attended   by   taking  shutdown of bus section   – A /B.
--- Page 627 ---
Hindalco Industries Limited  Electrical Safety Standard   Document No.   HI L/ O HS/STD / ES/01  Effective Date   19.03.2020  Review Date   19.03.2022  Page   29   of 58  dead bus  condition      In running unit   team try to take shut  down of bus A/B as required and     In a running unit , If bus isolation is not  possible , Team   try to operate   breaker  for   raking   IN   or   OUT   in   presence   of  senior people with door closed condition  &   with   PPEs   and   protection   as  mentioned above     If still, it is not raked in or out, then it is  marked OUT and team wait for earliest  opportunity of shutdown of unit.     Thi s type of situation happens once or  twice in a year if Preventive Maintenance  of switchgear is done properly during  overhauling of units  4.   Emergency way of  maintenance of HT  Breaker when it  gets stuck up  For  maintenance  breaker in  LIVE bus  condition  After all above conditions, if it felt by the  senior team to attend the breaker  problem in Running unit in BUS live  condition, then in door open condition  with proper PPES like  i)   Arc flash suit,  ii)   Arc flash helmet,  iii)   Hand gloves ,  Issue of breaker stuck up i s handled to  resolve.     Frequency of such operation/occurrence  is quite rare  5.   summary   In view of rare occurrence of situation   - 3 or  C and high risk involved, it is  recommended that such operation  should not be conducted in live bus  condition .  It should be attended/resolved only after  taking the relevant bus under shutdown.  6.2.10   E - waste Management System:
--- Page 628 ---
Hindalco Industries Limited  Electrical Safety Standard   Document No.   HI L/ O HS/STD / ES/01  Effective Date   19.03.2020  Review Date   19.03.2022  Page   30   of 58  GUIDELINES     Hazardous Wastes (Management, Handling & Trans boundary Movement Rules 2008 and  subsequent amendments). - Transformer oils, grease     E - Wastes (Management & Handling) Rules, 2011 .   -   Cables,   fuses, electronics component   etc.     Batteries (Management & Handling) Rules   –   2001   –   Notification Amendment 2010.  PROCEDURE     Generation of Used oil/ spent   oil, E - waste & used batteries   and keep it identified place in shop  floor / Substation .   Material should be kept at designated place and tagged .     After   reaching ¾th level of   stock at   Substation , user department will inform to Scrap  Management Cell for shifting the drums / wastes .     Scrap Man agement Cell will coordinate with Store Department for   availability of   machinery for  shifting.     Scrap Management Cell will give information to Environment Cell and confirm where the  material to be shifted.     Scrap Management will also provide a receipt note   for the collected material; though mail.     This mail is kept as a record.  6.2.11   Zero Joint Cable   Policy.  1.   New  Project/Installatio  n  a)   In principal “No cable joints “is permissible in Power (HT&LT) and Control  cables up to standard cable length   (HT   Cable   –   500 m, LT Cable   –   600 m,  Control cable   –   1000 m) which can be further increased with vendor  negotiation.  b)   To safeguard the cable from any physical damage/ Fire/ Alkali & Acidic fumes/  flammable hot fumes / dust, appropriate cable tray covering   should be  provided.  c)   All cable should have fire resistant coating in fire prone area.  d)   No cable joints should be allowed in highly flammable area i.e. Propane / LPG  yard.
--- Page 629 ---
Hindalco Industries Limited  Electrical Safety Standard   Document No.   HI L/ O HS/STD / ES/01  Effective Date   19.03.2020  Review Date   19.03.2022  Page   31   of 58  2.   Existing/ Running  Plant  In case cable joints are required to meet plant emergency   in running / existing plant  during exigency, it should be made properly as per   IS:   1255 - 1983 by skilled cable  Jointer / Electrician.  Power Cable (HT &LT): ( 3 Phase / single phase, 11.5 KV, 3.3 KV, 415 Volts AC/DC &  above and 220 V /110 V AC/DC )  a)   All   Joints to be made with appropriate & suitable cable Jointing Kit with  standard procedure as per   IS:   1255 ·1983.  b)   Maximum one joint per length of power cable is permissible as per cable  length mentioned above   (new   project /installation) in case of emergency   in  running plant.  c)   More than one cable joints in one length of power cable to be replaced at the  earliest on   planned basis.  d)   Proper records of cable jointing to be maintained with equipment and joint  location.  e)   Every jointing place / location should have prop er VIS   at nearest location.  f)   In any case no any cable joint is to be made inside the MCC / Switch house  room.  g)   All power feeders cables (Transformer to PCC)   having joints   to be identified  and replaced at the earliest.  h)   As far as possible, cable joint should   be on straight bridge or separate cable  tray ( Should not be mixed with existing cable   tray)   or should be inside separate  closed Junction Box.  i)   Joints not   to be made inside Pit / Trench where chances of accumulation of  water / Caustic / any other liquid exi st.  e)   To safeguard the cable from any physical damage/ Fire/ Alkali & Acidic fumes/  flammable hot fumes / dust, appropriate cable tray covering should be  provided.  f)   All cable should have fire resistant coating in fire prone area.  j)   No cable joints should be all owed in highly flammable area i.e. Propane / LPG  yard  Control Cable: ( Single Phase , 220 Volts AC/DC & below)  a)   Maximum one joint per length of cable is permissible as per cable length  mentioned above   (new   project / installation)   in case of emergency in runn ing  plant.  b)   Preferably   all control   cable joints to be made in CLOSED Junction Boxes (IP 56)  preferably with Wago   terminals.  c)   Joints not to be made inside Pit / Trench where chances of accumulation of  water / Caustic / any other liquid exist.  d)   All cable should   have fire resistant coating in fire prone area.  e)   No cable joints should be allowed in highly flammable area i.e. Propane / LPG  yard

It is  also expressly understood by both Parties that the re could be minor variations in the  supplier - specific description / nomenclature of a particular spare part vis - à - vis the  description/ nomenclature as per the list of mandatory spare parts included in  technical Section of the ITB and any a mendments thereto, which shall be mutually  resolved by the OWNER and CONTRACTOR with necessary inputs from the supplier.  1.8   Completeness of the EQUIPMENT shall be the responsibility of the CONTRACTOR. The status of critical activities which may affect the critical  path needs to be identified at least a week in advance and initiate corrective  actions   under   intimation   to   the   Owner. Any   corrective   actions   by   Owner  also needs to be anticipated and informed at least a week in advance to  provide sufficient time for corrective actions.  v)   The monthly report shall also generate, using primavera, one month look ahead  plan   (to   be   submitted   along   with   the   current   month   and   should   highlight  the milestone dates). The notice shall specify   the   matter   constituting  FORCE   MAJEURE   explaining   to   what   extent contractual obligations will  thereby be prevented or   delayed and the further period for which it is estimated that  such prevention or delay will continue. CONTRACTOR shall provide justificatory  documents countersigned by the local Chamber of Commerce. Notwithstanding the  foregoing, FORCE MAJEURE shall not i nclude:  (a) weather conditions reasonably to be expected for the climate in the  geographic area of the contract SITE and suppliers shops including but  not limited to the monsoon season,  (b) the occurrence of any manpower or material shortages unless such a shortage  is itself caused by an event of FORCE MAJEURE, or  (c) any delay, default or failure (direct or indirect) in obtaining materials, or
--- Page 742 ---
Client   –   Hindalco Industries Ltd  Project   -   Aditya Alumina Refinery  TCE.13833A - ME - 6002 - 6001, Rev - R0   GENERAL CONDITON OF  CONTRACT Section - A2   Page |  71 of 127  (d) in any SUBCONTRACTOR/VENDOR or worker performing any WORK or any  other   delay,   default   or   failure   (financial   or   otherwise)   attributable   to   SUB -  CONTRACTOR/Vendor/worker, unless such delay, default or failure results from  any   act,   event   o r   condition   which   would,   with   respect   to   such   SUB -  CONTRACTOR/VENDOR/ worker, constitute an event of force majeure.  33.2   If   the   CONTRACTOR   suffers   delay   in   the   due   execution   of   the   contractual  obligations due to delays caused by FORCE MAJEURE as defined above, the agreed  time of completion of job covered by this CONTRACT or the obligat ion of the  CONTRACTOR shall be extended by a period of time on account of force majeure  conditions,   provided   that   on   the   occurrence   of   any   such   contingency,   the  CONTRACTOR reports within 7 days to the OWNER in WRITING, the cause of delay  a nd likely duration of cause of delay with requisite documentary evidence.   33.3 The  decision of the OWNER, whether there is FORCE MAJEURE condition or not and  whether extension of time shall be granted or not, shall be final.  33.4 Should one or both the parties be prevented from fulfilling the contractual obligations by  a state of FORCE MAJEURE lasting continuously for a period of 6 weeks, the two  parties   shall   consult   each   other   regarding   the   future   implementat ion   of   the  CONTRACT. The mere shortage of labour, materials or utilities shall not constitute  FORCE MAJEURE unless caused by circumstances which are themselves FORCE  MAJEURE.  33.5   CONTRACTOR and OWNER shall endeavour to prevent, overcome or remove the  causes of FORCE MAJEURE.  33.6   No ground for exemption can be invoked if CONTRACTOR has failed to give timely  notice by registered letter and subsequently supported it by documentary evidence.  33.7   Delay or non - performance by a party hereto caused by the occurrence of any event of  FORCE MAJEURE shall not:  a)   Constitute a default or breach of the CONTRACT,  Or  b)   Give rise to any claim for damages or additional cost or expense occasioned thereby,  if such delay or non - performance is caused by the occurrence of any event of FORCE  MAJEURE. On the occurrence of any of the happenings stated in the first sentence of this clause,  t he OWNER shall be at liberty to:  a)   Determine   the   CONTRACT   forthwith   by   notice in   WRITING   to   the  CONTRACTOR   or to the receiver or liquidator or to any person in whom the  CONTRACT may have   become vested, and act in the manner provided in clause  32.1 (proceedings or default) or,   b) Give to such receiver, liquidator or other person  in WRITING the option for a period of   one month of carrying out the CONTRACT  subject   to   his   providing   a   guarantee   for   the   due   and   faithful   performance   of  the CONTRACT up to the CONTRACT value of the work for the time being remaining  unexecuted and subject to his taking all reasonable steps to prevent stoppage of the  work. In the event of stoppage of the work, the period of the option under this clause  shall be fourteen (14) days only.

The Dispute shall be referred to a sole Arbitrator, to be   jointly appointed  by the Parties, under the Arbitration and Conciliation Act, 1996, (as amended from  time to time) which Rules are deemed to be incorporated by reference into this  Clause. Pre - commissioning is testing individual, stand - alone components for correct installation and  functioning such as motor rotational checks, air cooler run - ins, loop testing and cleaning  activities such as flushing, air -   or steam blowing and chemi cal cleaning. Activities like steam  boiler boil - out, drying of furnaces and drying out furnace or reactor refractory and subsequent  curing are also considered pre - commissioning activities.  ii.   Depending upon the complexity of the equipment or systems, the contractor shall carry out  pre - commissioning checks of systems, subsystems or components. Involvement of vendor  representatives and process licensor if required, shall be arranged by the contra ctor to ensure  adherence to their requirements during construction and confirmation of their acceptance of  the installation. However, as a minimum, they shall provide checklists and procedures for pre -  commissioning checks on their equipment or system s.  3.2.33   Mechanical Completion (M/C)  Mechanical Completion is the point in time where all subsystems and systems in a facility (or  part of) have reached a final stage in construction and pre - commissioning activities and can  be regarded as completed with the exception of punch list items. The   Contractor shall be  responsible for mechanical completion in order to have the facilities boxed up and leak tested.  All mechanical items like static equipment, rotating equipment etc. shall be installed, all civil  and structural items completed, and piping   and in - line instruments are reinstated after hydro -  testing and cleaning.  3.2.34   Commissioning Services of Plant & START - UP  i.   Commissioning involves site/field - based execution of commissioning plan. Commissioning  is testing completed systems (consisting of several individual, stand - alone components) for  correct functioning such as establishing circulations, functionally te sting complex control

Typical activities in  Contractor’s scope include but are not limited to:  ▪   Conduct Pre - Start - up Safety Reviews (PSSRs)  ▪   Take control of turned over systems/sub system.  ▪   Implement Lockout Tagout (LOTO) procedure for Commissioning and Start - Up  ▪   Calibration and testing of equipment, instruments, prior to start - up with a special focus  on   Safety   devices   such   as   pressure   safety   valves,   rupture   discs,   fire   detection  & protection systems, emergency shutdown systems, etc.  ▪   Ensure timely liquidation of Punch List items.  ▪   Energize and commission turned over systems.  ▪   Personnel training  ▪   Coordinate   availability   of   Process   Licensor   and   Vendors’   representatives   for  Commissioning, Start - Up and Performance Guarantee test, as applicable  ii.   The ready for START - UP is the point in time, where a facility is mechanically completed, leak  tested, components functionally tested and ready to be started up. All statutory authorities  shall be suitably informed by the contractor about start - up date and permission requested  where ever applicable. Plant commissioning shall continue with initial s ystem operation and  verification of the system's operability per design.  The functionality of the overall plant shall be demonstrated at normal operating conditions  from cold shutdown to full capacity. DEEMED ACCEPTANCE  In case, COMMISSIONING and/or proving of PERFORMANCE AND GUARANTEE TESTS  of the PLANT is delayed beyond 12 months from MECHANICAL COMPLETION due to the  reasons solely attributable to the OWNER, PLANT shall be DEEMED ACCEPTED with a  DEFECT LIABILITY PERIOD   of another 6 months. On DEEMED ACCEPTANCE, the care  and custody of the PLANT shall be transferred to OWNER, and CONTRACTOR will be  relieved from proving of PERFORMANCE AND GUARANTEE TEST of the PLANT. The  Security - Cum - Performance   Bank   Guarantee   shall   b e   extended,   accordingly,   by   the  CONTRACTOR.  Final payment shall be released at ninth month from MECHANICAL COMPLETION against  bank guarantee already running as Security - cum - Performance Bank Guarantee without  any additional Bank Guarantee.  This   clause   of   DEEMED ACCEPTANCE   shall   not   be   applicable   in   case   reasons  solely attributable to the OWNER are resolved before the completion of 12 months  from MECHANICAL COMPLETION and  COMMISSIONING and PERFORMANCE AND GUARANTEE TEST is started   within 12  months from MECHANICAL COMPLETION. In that case remaining activities including  COMMISSIONING /  PERFORMANCE AND GUARANTEE TESTS   shall   be completed as per the terms &  conditions of the CONTRACT and Security - cum - Performance Bank Guarantee shall be  extended, accordingly, by the CONTRACTOR so as to ensure minimum validity of  6 mon ths from the date of PRELIMINARY ACCEPTANCE.  19. Client   –   Hindalco Industries Ltd  Project   -   Aditya Alumina Refinery  TCE.13647A - 000 - 0 - TS - 0006, Rev   0   SPECIAL CONDITIONS OF CONTRACT (SCC) For LSTK  TENDER PACKAGES  Section - A 3   Page |   2   of   4  WORK DURING MONSOON  1.0   Contractor must take due cognizance of the presence of rainy season /   days in   his  scheduled completion period and accordingly, shall take all necessary actions to protect,  reorganize and progress the   work,   uninterruptedly during the monsoon   period.  2.0   Contractor to collect all meteorological data from the local authority and collect necessary  information about the intensity, frequency and period of rainy season.  3.0   No extension of time due to interruption /suspension of   work,   water   logging,   reduce d  /slowing down of   progress,   non - availability of manpower   etc.,   whatsoever may be the  reason, shall be tenable on account of monsoon and further no claim for stand - by of  manpower and equipment, other resources etc .   shall be paid for.  4.0   The successful   bidder shall be required to submit within 15 days of Letter of Acceptance of  offer / award of work, to the Engineer - in - Charge / OWNER his contingency plan for work  during monsoon clearly stating their methodology / strategy to progress uninterruptedly  dur ing monsoon mentioning the deployment of resources viz, numbers, capacity, category  of equipment and manpower on a weekly basis for approval. The contingency plan shall  include   all   provisions   for   constructing   and   maintaining   temporary   infrastructure   like  a pproach   road, temporary   drains in and   around work   area,   dewatering whether natural or  forced to keep the work area free from water logging so as to maintain the   same always   in  a state worthy of vehicular, human and equipment movement. The work area shall be so  isolated as to restrict run - of from the adjacent a reas in the work area / site.  5.0   Contractor shall put up temporary sheds for fabrication of formwork and reinforcement. For  fabrication of steelwork / piping proper rainproof shed with concretized floor of adequate  area shall be made.
